sample_reviews: author=Anna Edoloverio; rating=5; time=2025-09-05T06:20:12.179396426Z; relative=7 months ago; text=It was quite an exciting hike from Osmeña Peak to Lugsangan Peak. Entrance fee is 50php, you can get a guide but it’s optional. We chose to go on our own since we’ve been hiking for quite a while. As long as you can manage but be careful not fall. The view is amazingly beautiful. || author=Elena Bujanovic; rating=5; time=2025-11-17T15:07:18.835180934Z; relative=5 months ago; text=From Osmẽna Peak to Lugsangan Peak, little bit more difficult hike but still not hard. || author=JG Travels; rating=5; time=2022-09-19T03:04:27.098427Z; relative=3 years ago; text=The trail going up is a bit challenging, but the 360° view at the peak is unbeatable. Tour guide is optional. Environmental fee ₱50 only. Hiking going up is around 10-15 minutes but the path is rocky and steep. || author=LeyMon; rating=5; time=2022-10-05T07:09:02.601058Z; relative=3 years ago; text=Trail is way more challenging compared to Osmeña Peak but it is shorter yet narrow. It was rainy when we went there and the view is 💯. Highly recommend to take a Osmeña Peak first before Lugsangan Peak so that you can compare. Panoramic bunch of hills will be your view. || author=Ken Michael Taban-ud; rating=5; time=2023-05-20T10:50:07.001795Z; relative=2 years ago; text=First Climb on this Peak was great as part of my Afternoon Run. It was kinda raining and foggy during the Climb. My observed conditions are that its kinda more challenging than Osmena peak. There are areas that one slip could lead to due to sudden fall but just be cautious you'll be fine. During my climb, it was having drizzle so the limestone rocks made it slippery and most of the trail have sharp rocks as opposed to Osmena peak so a little bit of care is needed and please don't climb Drunk it will be ⚕️. Slippers and casual wear is doable but not advisable though. Hiking shoes or anything with Hard Grippy Outsoles to protect your feet against hard rocks will be fine and make your climb comfy.

sample_reviews:
author=Paulo Niño; rating=5; time=2024-06-16T03:06:49.740494Z; relative=a year ago; text=Sacred Heart Church in Cebu is a serene and beautiful place of worship. Its architecture blends modern and traditional elements, creating a peaceful ambiance. The interior is spacious, fully air-conditioned, and well-maintained, with stunning stained glass windows and a reverent atmosphere. The community is warm and welcoming, making visitors feel at home. The church is conveniently located, and services are conducted in a solemn and organized manner. Overall, it’s a spiritually uplifting place, perfect for reflection and prayer. || author=AJQ; rating=5; time=2019-09-21T09:44:36.070986Z; relative=6 years ago; text=After it’s renovation, this church has transformed itself. No parish office during Monday but for those in Pilgrimage, someone can still stamp your passports. || author=MR Dy; rating=5; time=2018-01-09T07:32:16.572Z; relative=8 years ago; text=My family's church for three generations. Simple minimalist design. Good parking space. Serene.

name: Archdiocesan Shrine of Our Lady of Guadalupe
address: 969 V Rama Ave, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=cwade2009; rating=5; time=2025-12-16T07:10:11.093765843Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Tried to find the Starbuks head trail to no avail but explored this church instead. 20₱ parking. || author=Kim Alforque; rating=5; time=2024-12-17T03:24:09.501456Z; relative=a year ago; text=A must visit if you are into Churches and a devotee of Mama Mary.
The Church itself is Majestic!
It’s so solemn that it feels heart warming being inside the Church. I visited last Sunday after Our Lady of Guadalupe’s Feast day last December 12th.
They have a veneration area on the left side of the Church where you can kneel and pray to Our Lady and they also have a blessed Sacrament. || author=MENSEUNG; rating=5; time=2023-11-04T11:45:12.137028Z; relative=2 years ago; text=My recent visit to the Guadalupe church was a truly memorable experience. Its serenity, solemnity, and peaceful atmosphere make it a desirable place to worship. The interior is well maintained and orderly, featuring beautiful and ornate woodwork, which gives the building a majestic and dignified air to it. The organ is well kept and projects a wonderful reverberating sound throughout the chapel. The pews are evenly spaced, allowing everyone to have ample room to comfortably partake in the service. During our time there, the members were friendly and welcoming, which created a pleasant and warm atmosphere. I find the church is a great place to not only pray,  worship, and learn about the faith but also to take time away to relax. || author=Earl Darwin Chester Cabiling; rating=5; time=2023-03-04T23:19:25.704909Z; relative=3 years ago; text=The church is huge and looks amazing. I joined the 5:15 AM mass and people sang with the choir which gave me goosebumps. The outside of the church also looks historic with all the statues of saints and priests. || author=Diana Mellado (Miss President); rating=5; time=2024-11-20T21:09:36.092944Z; relative=a year ago; text=A very solemn place to pray and recollect for yourself. The place is very serene, especially on weekdays. It is located in Guadalupe Proper, Cebu City thus making it easy to commute via traditional jeep or taxi.

name: Archdiocesan Shrine of Our Lady of Lourdes
address: 7VVC+735, 430 Francisco Llamas St, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Caius; rating=5; time=2025-10-29T20:46:09.426015159Z; relative=6 months ago; text=The Archdiocesan Shrine of Our Lady of Lourdes has its own unique architecture. You can see its huge curve-shaped roof made of concrete and a combination blue-white paints reflecting the color of their patron saint. It is a cross shaped in birds eye view, has a grotto in front, and also a large tv screen inside for live streaming. The first I was there for sunday mass, it has a huge numbers of manimbahay. Perfect for praying to our Lord GOD and also for self reflection. || author=Enteng Nabablit; rating=5; time=2025-07-15T03:05:04.693324328Z; relative=9 months ago; text=This church is a peaceful and holy place. It feels calm and quiet, perfect for praying or just thinking. You can really feel God’s presence here. If you’re looking for a place to rest your heart and mind, this is a good spot to visit. || author=Rabbi Morata; rating=4; time=2023-02-16T01:42:13.206234Z; relative=3 years ago; text=Very solemn place & well-maintained, family - oriented & one can really be encouraged to pray & make one's devotions /prayers. Adoration Chapel is also just as solemn. The pilgrim site of our Lady of Lourdes is also clean & expansive (the whole Archdiocesan Shrine in general that is..)
There's also convenient places to eat nearby like LA Fortuna bakery...
So it's good to give a very good amount of donation to the Church/shrine since one can see where it goes... || author=Hyacinth Fidelis Joaquin; rating=5; time=2022-06-23T05:24:00.292108Z; relative=3 years ago; text=A beautiful shrine of Our Lady of Lourdes. It is by Gods Grace that pursuing up the path/ career i onced prayed for led me to places i would never think i would be able to do in this lifetime ie like seeing the Pope and going to Rome. I would love to go back to Cebu and visit this church again

name: Our Mother of Perpetual Help - Redemptorist Church
types: tourist_attraction, church, place_of_worship, association_or_organization, point_of_interest, establishment
address: 8V7W+FHP, Queen's Rd, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
description: Catholic masses & choral singing at a towering church with a striking, art deco-influenced design.
sample_reviews:
author=Ryan Patrick Cejudo; rating=5; time=2026-02-27T12:24:34.591253875Z; relative=a month ago; text=There’s a certain kind of peace I only find at Redemptorist Church, especially when I step inside away from the Mango Avenue traffic. It’s easily one of the most iconic spots in Cebu, and even if you aren’t there for the novena, the open-air architecture and the constant breeze through the side doors make it feel so welcoming. I love how the community here feels—from the candle sellers outside to the quiet hum of people praying at the shrine. Whether I’m attending a packed Wednesday mass or just sitting in a pew for ten minutes of silence, the atmosphere is always incredibly grounding and soulful. || author=Paulo Niño; rating=5; time=2024-06-11T11:13:56.959648Z; relative=a year ago; text=I attended a 5:30 pm Mass. The exterior of the church is currently being painted. Hopefully, the interior of the church will be painted too, as well as the tiles, since the church is located in the center of the metropolitan area. The renovation will enhance the church's appearance and create a more welcoming environment for parishioners and visitors alike. || author=Bethanne Cane; rating=5; time=2023-12-10T02:32:03.982642Z; relative=2 years ago; text=This place is a haven for Christians who have adoration for the Great Almighty and Mother Mary. They also have scheduled English mass for people who don't understand the local dialect Bisaya. One of the churches here I love to visit because of the solemnity and peaceful serenity of the location. My favorite place to contemplate life's journey and be thankful because life blessed us in any way. || author=Luchz P; rating=5; time=2025-03-25T10:33:59.938996Z; relative=a year ago; text=Breezy when the temperature 🌡️ is cool otherwise it's quite warm even though there are fans everywhere (most of it needs cleaning 😉).

Candle area - spacious and candle 🕯️ here are good (how it is made vs).

Choir/Songs: mm dunno why (not just here but in other Catholic Churches) they kept changing the melodies of the songs!
Back in the 90's it was lovely and people can song/hymn to it 😁

Audio: when you're seated in the front you can barely understand the Priest it sounds far.

Holy water 💦 blessing after the Mass depends on who presided. 🥹

As expected, it is crowded during Wednesday (Perpetual Help).. || author=Artemio III Masbate (Art); rating=5; time=2023-09-12T03:28:00.629584Z; relative=2 years ago; text=One of my favorite churches in Cebu. Parking is spacious so you don't have to worry about finding a spot. The audio system inside the church is superb. You get to understand every word said by the priest during the homily.

name: Monastery of the Holy Eucharist
types: tourist_attraction, church, place_of_worship, association_or_organization, point_of_interest, establishment
address: XJH2+PG6, Sibonga, Cebu, Philippines
description: Castlelike site where devotees leave offerings to the Virgin Mary in exchange for miracles.
sample_reviews:
author=cwade2009; rating=4; time=2025-12-09T00:04:01.629890535Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Dress code for entering inside but you can walk around outside and take pictures…parking is 20 pesos…beware of the scamming kids at the bottom of the hill who will tell you it’s the only place to park…drive all the way to the top and park your car in the proper place and avoid the walk uphill…it’s more secure…when I drove back down and they all saw me the scrambled and ran. Pretty awful to be using a religious site to scam people… || author=Dion McDonald; rating=5; time=2026-01-03T12:51:34.337812843Z; relative=3 months ago; text=This working monastery in Sibonga, Cebu is actually an incredible formation of multiple impressive buildings on a huge and spectacular area of land. Despite the hot day, huge crowds and long lines to enter, this was definitely an unmissable experience. Entry was free and although the grounds were vast, you could potentially complete a visit in as little as 60 mins. Touring all buildings and the grounds would take at least a number of hours however. Tip: visitors should wear modest, respectable clothing like long pants/skirts (below the knee). || author=Just Carter; rating=5; time=2025-10-31T02:25:07.473321162Z; relative=5 months ago; text=The Monastery of the Holy Eucharist, also known as Simala Shrine, is truly awe-inspiring place that blends deep spirituality with breathtaking architecture.  Nestled in the hills of Sibonga, it offers a serene atmosphere perfect for reflection and prayer.

The grandeur of its castle-like structure, the solemn silence, and the heartfelt devotion of pilgrims make it a sacred experience like no other.

Whether you come to pray, give thanks, or simply marvel at its beauty, Simala Shrine leaves you with peace, faith, and a sense of divine presence. || author=Joel Obenieta; rating=5; time=2025-12-13T03:44:30.109871572Z; relative=4 months ago; text=The Simala Church of Cebu, officially called the Monastery of the Holy Eucharist, is a famous Catholic pilgrimage site located in Sibonga, Cebu, Philippines.

It is best known for its miraculous image of the Virgin Mary (Our Lady of Lindogon). Many devotees visit the church to pray, give thanks, and ask for healing or miracles. The church is especially popular during Holy Week and Marian feast days.

Simala Church is also well known for its castle-like architecture, which resembles a European cathedral, and its scenic hilltop location. Visitors often describe it as both a spiritual and tourist destination, combining faith, prayer, and beautiful views. || author=Reymund Lara; rating=5; time=2026-02-20T13:12:47.900750049Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Simala Shrine in Cebu is a strikingly beautiful and serene destination. The atmosphere is deeply peaceful, making it an ideal place for prayer, reflection, and genuine spiritual connection with the Lord.

A holy sanctuary where faith and devotion are deeply felt. 🙏 Known as the patron saint of the souls in purgatory, San Nicolas continues to inspire the faithful with his intercession and miracles.

The classic architecture, solemn interiors, and enduring devotion of the faithful make it a must-visit for both pilgrims and travelers. 🌿 Whether for prayer, reflection, or simply to witness Cebu’s cultural faith tradition, this shrine offers a unique and moving experience.

sample_reviews:
author=Rob Mono; rating=1; time=2026-01-29T01:51:43.997046385Z; relative=2 months ago; text=If you’re a civilian clean people avoid this island!! Is the most horrible and polluted place in the planet .. traffic more intense than NY , Manila or Beijing , 24 on 24!!! also the sewer direct to the sidewalk, it’s dirty and full of rats jumping around! The only tourists  are the bag packers or the classic old mans with a young girl from here!! 6-7 millions of people living in a small island think about the mountains of trash and the smell !! || author=rune krumsvik; rating=5; time=2026-02-03T02:13:24.546832005Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Stayed 1 month in cebu,from January to February, the weather was quite nice, not too warm 25-30,clouded,so perfect for us from Europe, had two days in Oslob and Moalboal diving with the whalesharks and sardines and turtles,,the most fantastic days down here,just amazing, wouldn't miss that experience if you like nature,also tumalog waterfalls,,,but i recommend that you get a person from cebu too guide you, beacouse it can take very long waiting time,,I highly recommend Shierell Buenconsejo Ruela, just find her on Facebook,,the food down here is very internasjonal,and the local food is very good, pretty sheap prices compared too Europe, will surely be back 😀😀❤️ || author=Miky Valdez; rating=1; time=2026-02-05T10:08:09.941177003Z; relative=2 months ago; text=The island of the apocalypse.
We arrived with very different expectations: a peaceful island surrounded by beautiful sea. What we found was the complete opposite.
The beaches are very limited and mostly only in the far north and south. and not even all of them are worth visiting. That’s extremely disappointing, because when you go to an island, you don’t expect just a handful of decent beaches.
The traffic is absolutely insane. We were stuck for two hours in the city center without moving at all. The island feels dirty and heavily polluted. The chaos honestly reminded me of India or Cebu City’s center ,constant congestion, noise, and smog everywhere plus rats!!
Getting from Lapu-Lapu Airport to either the north or south of the island can take almost four hours for just 100 km. That alone says everything about how badly organized and overwhelmed this place is. || author=Pio Portanto; rating=1; time=2026-02-06T10:47:48.469547880Z; relative=2 months ago; text=The Nightmare Island
This place was a huge disappointment. There are basically only three things to do on the entire island. First, the whale shark experience,which is completely overcrowded. You’re surrounded by hundreds of people trying to do the same thing. After waiting for hours, you finally get into the water with about 20 people from your group, and the experience lasts only 20–30 minutes.
The other options are the waterfalls and Sumilon Island. Sumilon Island was packed with local tourists having barbecues, blasting loud music, and leaving the beach dirty.The waterfalls were similarly overcrowded and chaotic.And then there’s Cebu City itself. a complete nightmare. Endless traffic, cars and motorbikes driving with no apparent rules, and a city that feels dirty, noisy, and overwhelming.
Overall, chaotic, overcrowded, and not worth the hype. || author=Valentina; rating=1; time=2026-01-31T16:14:33.497746337Z; relative=2 months ago; text=The only island that feels like it has no sea. Aside from a few beaches near Oslob, there’s really nothing else. Roads and houses have been built without any planning.almost right up to the shoreline, leaving hardly any natural beach areas. The city center is chaotic at all hours, dirty, and heavily polluted. Overall, a very disappointing experience. OVERRATED FOR SURE !!!

name: Abli Cebu
address: A. Climaco St, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Mj Piedad; rating=4; time=2026-03-03T20:48:42.633784404Z; relative=a month ago; text=The food here is incredibly delicious. What I will never forget is the atsuete butter paired with their pandesal. My friends and I were truly mind-blown. It was a flavor I had never tasted before in my life as a local.

Their lechon kawali was also exceptionally good. The twist was that it came with a kare-kare–inspired sauce, but it had its own distinct elements and flavor profile—different, yet still very Filipino at heart. The pancit was delicious as well, although they didn’t really change much from the original flavor.

For dessert, we had the Morta, and it was absolutely amazing. It’s different from the traditional torta, with added elements—but what they added wasn’t foreign; it was still proudly Filipino.

As for the cocktail, it was okay. I appreciated that it highlighted Filipino local flavors, but I found it a bit lacking in alcohol since it wasn’t quite strong enough for my taste.

Overall, I had a wonderful experience and would love to come back. It makes me proud that there’s a place like this that elevates our Cebuano dishes into something we can confidently showcase to anyone—whether Filipino or foreign. I will definitely return. || author=Jan Altendorff; rating=3; time=2026-01-28T07:20:20.910947173Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Abli is easy to miss, I would urge you not to. The frontage is set back from the street, so look out for the sandwich board (see photo). It is tiny, only room for about 20 ish covers, so if you're keen call ahead and book.

Once inside the experience is faultless, the staff are attentive, polite, highly communicative and efficient. I ordered a Champorado coffee the Arice (Arroz) Caldo (small) and the Crispy Cebu Express, everything was generous, well presented and tasted exactly as described on the menu, some great cooking going on in that kitchen. I've been in the Philippines almost a month and this was the best food I've had anywhere in Manila or Cebu. I will be going back.

Update 27 Jan (Dinner)

I did go back, this time I had a garlic rice, Chicken Inasal and their Hmmmmba. Sadly, the food did not live up to my first experience. The sauce/dressing flavours and presentation were all good,. However, the pork meat was tough and dry. The char on the chicken had been taken too far and the bitterness of the sear marks on the chicken skin just came through any dressing or sauce. It didn't taste bbq smoky it just tasted burnt. Once again the components named on the menu were all there and sauces / dressings were identifiable and tasted good. Sadly, the primary component in the two meat dishes left a lot to be desired. The garlic rice was good. The dried pineapple slice on my G&T was pretty but a bit too powerful (aroma & taste) for the drink. || author=Jemarie Letrondo; rating=5; time=2025-12-30T16:53:03.367967283Z; relative=3 months ago; text=The food were great. Although, I suggest that the level of spiciness be reduced for the hmmmba and bicol express as I think customers with low tolerance for spicy food will not be able to enjoy it as much. The ginabot was spectacular. I don't normally eat ginabot but theirs is made with glaze which elevated this street food. Very good morta. I don't suggest eating here when you're super hungry as portions may not be enough, though it can be for sharing when you're with a group. Just order a couple more dishes. The prices of the food is reasonable and worth it. The indoor dining space is quite small, only sits a few people (>20?) but they have tables outside as well. However it could get hot during lunch time. No dedicated parking space. || author=Concha, Joe; rating=4; time=2026-02-27T06:54:30.759588465Z; relative=a month ago; text=We decided to visit this place after buying pasalubong, looking for a chill vibe before picking up our luggage since it was highly recommended on TikTok.

Upon ordering, they informed us that the waiting time for food would be around 30 minutes, which was fine since we were already there. We ordered a flat white and an iced Americano, but honestly we don’t recommend the coffee due to high acidity and that it wasn’t brewed well at the time. Some of listed food are not available.

For the mortas, the available flavors were mocha, ube & cheese, and tiramisu. We decided to try them, and we highly recommend the ube & cheese flavor—it was the best among the three.

Overall, it’s okay to visit once for the experience, but not somewhere we would personally come back to.

Expense (4 pax): ₱835 (3 mortas & 2 coffees) || author=Edgar Alan Yap; rating=3; time=2026-01-09T13:30:18.783570003Z; relative=3 months ago; text=This small cafe specializes in Filipino food with a creative twist. I loved their Morta (their take on torta de Cebu) topped with whipped cream and yema bits.

Their savory dishes were sophisticatedly plated and photogenic, however, fell short of my expectations in terms of flavor and texture.

The Crispy Cebu Express had a rich and spicy sauce, but the pork belly slices were not crispy enough and had too much fat. The Pasta Chorizo Olio was on the sweet side and didn’t have depth of flavor. One of the better dishes we had was the Butter Inun-unan.

Customer service was excellent at the restaurant itself, but there’s room to improve with their reservation system. The waiters were friendly and attentive. However, it was difficult to get in touch with the cafe for reservations as no one was picking up for a long time when we ringed the contact numbers listed here and on their Facebook page.

name: The Pig and Palm

sample_reviews:
author=Lisa J; rating=5; time=2026-03-05T02:29:21.004546301Z; relative=a month ago; text=What a fantastic meal!  We were offered seats at the kitchen counter, which we quickly accepted.  We love to watch the "show".  After offering menus, the staff gave us plenty of time to review and discuss before approaching.  This was VERY welcome.  All of our cocktails were delicious and beautifully balanced.  It was a real treat to be able to choose from a long list of classic cocktails, that were made with elevated ingredients.

We love to share numerous small plates, to try lots of different tastes.  The flavors of each dish were superb!  We had Ceviche, Beef Tartare, Garlic Prawns, Foie Gras, KFC (beautifully crispy and with delicious sauce, as well as kimchi ketchup), and Pork Empanadas.  Usually we don't have any room for dessert, but we were able to squeeze in a shared bowl of Poached Strawberries that were heavenly.

Service was spot on - quickly available, but not hovering.  Thank you for a very memorable evening. || author=Joey C.; rating=5; time=2026-02-21T02:01:02.587060485Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Wow what a meal and we just ordered from the starter menu. Beautiful restaurant designed by famous architects Nero and Hu (Cebuano and Shanghainese duo) based in Shanghai. Pair that with a very talented chef with a very nice menu and voila, awesome meal hahahaha! Before I mention the food, thanks to our server Jermin who  provided top notch waiting service.

It was a busy night so there was no regular table so we ended up seating by the chef bar where you can see all the food being cooked and prepared before going to service. If you’re a foodie I suggest searing here. As someone who loves food and cooking I picked up on so many pro tips.

Now the food, it a fusion of Filipino, Japanese and Western and of the 5 dishes we tried, all were excellent. I saw so many other dishes being prepared for other that looks so scrumptious that I’m definitely going back to try those.

HIGHLY RECOMMEND!! || author=Caffeinated Capybara; rating=5; time=2025-12-26T03:02:33.833899073Z; relative=4 months ago; text=We really intended to eat here because a friend recommended it. We ordered two starters, a salad, two mains, and two cocktails. All were good. Serving was more than enough for two people to share. We were even able to bring home the mains to Manila. I loved the pork confit more than the salmon. Cauliflower salad tasted different in my palette but it was good, too. Their brioche was soft, and the chili jam was the perfect pair.

The place though was quite noisy. Though not the restaurant’s fault, it just ruined the dining experience for us. There was one table celebrating something I guess (this was in November) but they were just too loud and rowdy. || author=E K; rating=5; time=2026-03-07T07:11:50.820618823Z; relative=a month ago; text=Must make reservations.
The main dining area was already fully booked so we booked for the other room but it was quite cold so we preferred the terrace seat and it was perfect!
Cauliflower risotto, squid ink pasta, ceviche,  beef tartar, and steak!
It was very delicious! || author=Kate Butaya; rating=4; time=2026-02-14T23:44:00.963017466Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Everything turned out to be such a lovely experience. I first ate here in 2022 and honestly wasn’t that impressed. But when I came back recently, I was genuinely happy to see how much they’ve improved. The food translated beautifully this time — the flavors were well-balanced and satisfying. It’s great to see how far they’ve come!

However, I did have one small concern. When I tried to order more, one of the servers commented that it might be ‘too much,’ which caught me off guard. It was the first time I’d ever received that kind of remark while dining out. Perhaps he wasn’t aware that my friends and I simply enjoy eating a lot, but it did feel slightly off. Still, overall, it was a positive experience.

name: Bell+Amadeus
address: 1120 Gov. M. Cuenco Ave, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Yana Melnichenko; rating=5; time=2026-02-20T13:35:46.224440269Z; relative=2 months ago; text=We had a wonderful dinner at Bell+ Amadeus in Cebu city, and it was truly one of the best places we’ve visited here! ✨

The service was exceptional!
attentive, warm, and genuinely welcoming!

We came for a date night, and the atmosphere was perfect intimate, cozy, and stylish without feeling overwhelming. It’s definitely a great spot for couples.

We ordered the Tuna Tartare, Grilled Pesto Chicken with Dragon Fruit Salad, the B+A Burger, and finished with the Chocolate Lava dessert.

Everything was beautifully presented and absolutely delicious. The flavors were well-balanced and fresh, and you can really feel the quality of the ingredients. The drinks were great too especially the Mr. Prodigal cocktail.

Overall, an amazing experience with fantastic food, lovely ambiance, and impeccable service.

We would definitely come back and highly recommend it to anyone visiting Cebu.

Oh, and we love the QR menu with pictures, very handy!

Kind regards,
Yana & Nick || author=Mao Soon Lim; rating=5; time=2025-12-20T11:03:49.481490814Z; relative=4 months ago; text=We had a truly memorable experience at Bell + Amadeus. Even though we arrived close to their closing time, the staff welcomed us with genuine warmth and kindness, never making us feel rushed or uncomfortable. That alone says a lot about the heart of this restaurant.

Service throughout the evening was friendly, attentive, and sincere. The team took time to explain the menu and made sure we were well taken care of, which really elevated the whole dining experience. It’s rare to find a place where hospitality feels so natural and unforced.

The food was excellent—well prepared, thoughtfully presented, and clearly made with care. You can feel the passion behind both the kitchen and the front-of-house team.

Bell + Amadeus is not just about good food; it’s about how they make guests feel welcome and valued, even at the end of a long day. A big thank you to the entire team for going the extra mile. We will definitely be back and highly recommend this place to anyone visiting Cebu. (BETTER THEN SIALO!) || author=Emil Cid; rating=5; time=2025-12-14T13:56:43.767896717Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Our experience at Bell and Amadeus was truly delightful from start to finish. The ambiance felt cozy and inviting, creating the perfect setting to relax and enjoy the moment. Our server Mel was courteous, attentive, and made sure every order was handled with care. The food was simply perfect, bursting with flavor and served impressively fast, which made the experience even more enjoyable.

We would also like to extend our appreciation to Chime who welcomed us with genuine Cebu hospitality and surprised us with a complimentary dessert that added a sweet and thoughtful touch to our visit. Bell and Amadeus truly stands out as a place where great food meets heartfelt service. It is without a doubt the perfect place to be for all occasions. || author=Renz E; rating=5; time=2026-01-19T12:50:29.910722383Z; relative=3 months ago; text=This is such a beautiful spot for a cozy yet elegant dinner in Cebu City. The atmosphere feels warm and intimate, the food is really good, and the staff are so thoughtful and attentive. They also remind you ahead of time about your dinner reservation. They even helped arrange a customized cake and a special proposal surprise, which made the night unforgettable. The saikoro beef ribeye was a standout, the chicken confit was unique, and the guava mocktail was so refreshing. A perfect place for moments you’ll want to remember. || author=Jawwad Djamla; rating=5; time=2025-12-27T08:07:32.639900468Z; relative=4 months ago; text=We went to Bell+Amadeus for Christmas dinner, and overall it was a really nice experience. They normally require reservations, so we were very grateful they were still able to give us a table even though we didn’t have one, definitely a good reminder to book ahead, especially during holidays.

The restaurant has a cozy, intimate atmosphere, very much in line with how other diners describe it, relaxed yet refined, with a European-inspired feel that’s perfect for special occasions or a slower, more intentional meal. One thing to note is that they don’t have a private restroom inside the restaurant, you’ll need to step out and use the shared public CR at 88th Avenue, which is used by other establishments nearby.

Service was excellent. The staff was very attentive, polite, and well-paced, checking in at the right moments without hovering.

The food was consistently very good. For appetizers, we ordered the Tuna Tartare with Squid Ink Tapioca Cracker, which was so good, fresh, flavorful, and beautifully balanced. We also had the Grilled Pesto Chicken & Dragon Fruit Salad with Creamy Parmigiano, which we finished almost instantly.

For mains, we tried several dishes and enjoyed all of them. The Paella Negra was rich and satisfying, the Chicken Confit was tender and delicious, and we also had the Short Rib Ragu Fettuccine, which was comforting and well-executed with deep, hearty flavor. We ordered their frozen margaritas, which were on a two-for-the-price-of-one promo, making it a great deal.

Dessert wrapped things up perfectly. We had the Chocolate Lava Cake, Pesto Ice Cream, and Poached Pears in Red Wine Sauce, and all were very good, a lovely way to end the meal.

Overall, the experience at Bell+Amadeus was warm, enjoyable, and memorable. Between the cozy ambiance, attentive service, and consistently good food, it’s easy to see why the restaurant is popular and often fully booked. Definitely worth a visit, just make sure to reserve ahead next time.

sample_reviews:
author=I AM THE GIRL WHO TRAVELS; rating=4; time=2026-03-10T07:58:21.456599467Z; relative=a month ago; text=The taste is good, but the halaan/clam was very tough almost like hard rubber. The lechon skin was not crispy, but the meat was very tender and flavorful naman.

Overall, a trip to cebu still won’t be complete without stopping by at House of Lechon. :) || author=Concha, Joe; rating=5; time=2026-02-27T13:25:18.427794252Z; relative=a month ago; text=We tried their dinner, sadly the lechon is no longer available. I highly suggest to go by atleast 5pm. We still try their dishes with lechon such as sisig, putchero, etc. It’s truly one of the best lechon restaurants, it definitely lives up to the hype on social media. The food was excellent, the service was great, and the overall experience was worth it. A place you should definitely try—and one we’d love to come back to experience there dine in. || author=Yasmin Unda; rating=5; time=2026-02-17T13:48:32.153690510Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Michelin-selected House of Lechon absolutely lives up to the hype. The place was fully packed when we went — definitely make a reservation unless you’re ready to queue for a while. Everything we ordered was delicious. The chorizo was especially memorable — juicy, flavorful, and perfectly grilled. The lechon was tender with beautifully crisp skin, though I personally would’ve liked it just a touch saltier. The scallops were tasty too, but I wish they were a bit bigger. Service was great — efficient, warm, and clearly used to handling a full house. Overall, a must-visit when in Cebu. || author=Joey C.; rating=5; time=2026-02-22T09:12:07.513535384Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Wow! Super nice flavor, moist and tender. You can come alone or come with your friends, they have all serving sizes.  They have all kinds of dishes in lechon and other Filipino dishes.  Come for the lechon while you try the other dishes.  Enough said! Best lechon with matching atmosphere! || author=James; rating=5; time=2025-12-15T12:58:58.937335124Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Place:
Spacious and comfortable. The lechon display behind the glass wall is a nice touch. Very close to Ayala Center—perfect for lunch or dinner after mall walking.

Food:
Chicharong bulaklak was crispy and not oily, with a well-seasoned vinegar dipping sauce. Lechon (1/4 kilo) was soft, juicy, and very flavorful, with a great dipping sauce. Dinuguan was served hot and tasted good but a bit salty on its own—perfect when paired with garlic rice.

Serving time was around 15–20 minutes.

Service:
Staff were very friendly and welcoming. Even though they were closing early for a Christmas party, they still accommodated customers. Special thanks to Kuya Aldrin.

Overall:
Will definitely come back to try other dishes. Highly recommended for anyone visiting Cebu.

name: La Parisienne Sky
address: Tops Road, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Pia Molato; rating=1; time=2026-01-26T15:57:34.572874508Z; relative=3 months ago; text=First went here last August 2025 with my mom and it was a really good dining experience — service, food and gelato. Take note my mom has been to EU not only once so she knows the authentic taste. But my experience tonight with my brother was the complete opposite. From looking at the dishes and first bite of the pizza till having gelato, it didn’t taste as good as before. The orders also took longer to be served than before. I told my brother maybe the previous chefs left. Then after dinner and ordering the gelato, I asked the cashier at the gelato area if the chefs are new, she said yes. I knew it. Totally disappointed and not satisfied. Not worth it anymore. Shown photos are from tonight. Will find photos from last year. || author=Coleen Faith Demerin; rating=1; time=2026-02-19T11:19:39.044725042Z; relative=2 months ago; text=We recently visited during our trip from Bacolod, Negros Occidental, and as first-time customers, we were genuinely looking forward to the experience because of the beautiful location and ambiance.

Upon arrival, we were a bit confused about the ordering process and waited several minutes at the counter without being acknowledged. While the staff may have been busy, a simple greeting or “please wait” would have helped us feel accommodated.

The most concerning part of our visit was the blueberry croissant we purchased. After my boyfriend had eaten half of it, we discovered green and black mold inside. This was very alarming. We immediately raised the concern, and to be fair, the staff member who assisted us handled it professionally. She politely apologized, offered coffee or gelato as a gesture, and processed a refund without hesitation. We truly appreciate her calm and respectful approach.

We also experienced a long waiting time for our mango shake (around 40+ minutes), and when it arrived, it was already watery.

We chose to raise our concern directly instead of posting immediately because we believe feedback should help businesses improve. We hope this review is taken constructively, especially regarding food quality control and service attentiveness.

The place has great potential given its stunning view, and we hope improvements can be made to match the overall experience with the beautiful location. || author=Genevieve Amador; rating=2; time=2025-12-28T18:02:41.251388672Z; relative=4 months ago; text=I’ve been to the original La Vie Parisienne shop closer to the city, and I’d say this branch near Tops Cebu is just as busy. The difference, however, is that service at the other locations is noticeably better.

Our main issue here was the service, especially since we were seated outside. We repeatedly had to wave just to get a server’s attention—for ordering, for water, to follow up on our food, and even for the bill. Everything required waiting.

Food service was also poorly paced. We were already finished with our pizza before any of the pasta dishes arrived. Some of us were halfway through our pastas before the last pasta was finally served. If we hadn’t followed up, I doubt the remaining dishes would have arrived in a reasonable time.

We were already tired from walking around Tops, and we were hungry, so the service experience really took away from what should have been an enjoyable meal. The pastas weren’t bad, and the pizza itself was decent, but having to cut up the pizza ourselves because it wasn’t sliced properly doesn’t exactly make for a good pizza experience.

Overall, the atmosphere felt chaotic rather than relaxed. It didn’t feel like the kind of casual-to-semi-fine dining experience the brand is known for. Because of that, I don’t think I’d be inclined to return to this particular branch. || author=Paulo Niño; rating=5; time=2025-09-15T11:02:27.804346551Z; relative=7 months ago; text=La Parisienne Sky, located before Tops, is a delightful spot to enjoy food with a view. We ordered their pizza, which came out flavorful and perfectly baked, and paired it with tasty barbecue and juicy chicken that made the meal more filling. For drinks, we tried the Meysu Fruit Antioxidant, refreshing and light, balancing the richness of the food. The ambiance is charming, especially when you visit at night, as the city lights create a cozy, relaxing vibe. A great place to unwind, dine, and share good moments with family and friends. || author=Toto John Ace (JanEys); rating=1; time=2026-02-14T18:02:25.213304633Z; relative=2 months ago; text=February 14, 2026 (My Experience) I liked the lasagna, the vibe, the overwhelming view, and the nostalgic décor. However, I didn’t like how long the food took to arrive—it was almost 2 hours. If I hadn’t gone downstairs to follow up, the food probably wouldn’t have arrived. My partner was already very hungry and wanted to eat me alive 😜. The pork chop was extremely dry, bland, and unseasoned.
On my first visit before, the pizza was very cheesy and delicious, but on my second visit it was very dry. There was also no spoon. I told the crew who delivered the food that we needed one, but no one came back. We finished eating using only a fork.
I understand that it was Valentine’s Day and there were a lot of customers. I just hope there will be better preparation so the quality and experience are consistent—like during my first visit. (Not recommended for major special holidays like Valentine’s Day.)

And it is ver accessible because it is located in the heart of the city || author=Imara Miitu (Letteriety Organization); rating=5; time=2024-08-13T07:00:12.716752Z; relative=a year ago; text=Raul's: A Hidden Culinary Masterpiece
Raul's is more than just a restaurant; it's a gastronomic treasure tucked away in Cebu.

A fortunate stumble upon this place was a culinary revelation, the cab driver took me there as response to my request for a place with great food. Every dish is a masterpiece, a symphony of flavors executed with passion and precision.

The lechon, chicken, pork belly - each a standout, yet harmoniously complementing the others. The garlic rice, a perfect canvas for these flavors, and the skewers, an addictive indulgence.

The simplicity of the cuisine belies the complex emotions it evokes - comfort, joy, and pure satisfaction. The service is equally impressive, gentle, and attentive. The restaurant itself is a haven of Filipino charm - open, well-ventilated, and beautifully traditional. It's a space that perfectly complements the delicious food.

From the moment I stepped in, I knew I'd discovered something extraordinary. Raul's is a must-visit for anyone seeking an authentic Filipino culinary experience. It's a place where food transcends expectations and leaves an unforgettable mark.

#raulscebu, #cebufoodie, #filipinocuisine, #lechon, #porkbelly, #filipinorestaurant, #foodlover

name: Kitchen Lemon

sample_reviews:
author=波風ミナト; rating=3; time=2026-01-06T03:47:23.104580358Z; relative=3 months ago; text=I went there because it has a good reputation among Japanese people. Japanese customers are the most familiar with authentic Japanese flavors, after all.

I ordered karaage, and my partner ordered tonkatsu. The karaage was slightly over-fried and had become a bit tough. The tonkatsu was quite thin. It doesn’t compare to Yabu’s tonkatsu. The meat quality was average—not extremely tough, but nothing special either.

Apparently, the most popular items are salmon and steak. However, if I’m coming to a Japanese restaurant, those are not dishes I would normally order. There were no ramen, sushi, or gyoza on the menu. In Japan, this place would probably be considered more of a Western-style restaurant rather than a traditional Japanese one.

The prices are on the higher side for a Japanese restaurant, especially when compared to Japanese restaurants in Manila, Makati, or Cebu. That said, it is located inside IT Park, so I imagine the rent alone is probably around 70,000 pesos or so.

The restaurant seats a little over twenty people and fills up completely. I wonder how many table turnovers they get at night, and how business is during lunchtime. It feels like the restaurant has been open for about five years.

For Japanese people in this area, this might be the only realistic option. I asked whether there is currently a Japanese chef, and was told that there is.

Hmm. There are still menu items I haven’t tried, but as far as the tonkatsu and karaage go, I wouldn’t order them again. That may sound harsh, but it’s my honest opinion. Still, I think this is probably a very welcome and valuable restaurant for Japanese people living here.ummm karaage wa yapari oishikunai desune || author=Jose; rating=5; time=2026-01-23T16:36:50.160563695Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Their food is absolutely delicious, and the lunch menu changes every week, which make me really excited. Also the staffs are very polite. || author=Thousand Leaves; rating=5; time=2025-11-11T10:34:23.150656131Z; relative=5 months ago; text=This restaurant is managed by a Japanese chef I think, and for Japanese people, it offers comforting and familiar flavors we truly appreciate. The weekly lunch specials provide variety that keeps things interesting, and every dish on the regular menu is generous in portion and delicious. The servings are so large that it’s hard for women to finish everything — it can even be a threat to one’s diet — but even so, I really love this restaurant. Sulit kaayo! || author=PC; rating=5; time=2025-09-20T11:25:18.112734307Z; relative=7 months ago; text=Went to Kitchen Lemon on a Saturday evening. The croquettes were great, im not too familiar with them but this one is a nice starter. The gambas was peppery, you must consume asap to preserve the experience as it is a bit oily. The iberico pork was outstanding, i liked the mildy sweet sauce and complemented by the squash puree, it was the best of all the things we got. Maybe we just requested for the paella to be served together with the maindish that it came out cold, it was loaded with seafood though, it would've been better if it just came out from the kitchen hot.

Overall, we had a great time trying new things out. I can say it is worth it and would gladly come back for other stuff in their menu 😀 || author=John Rex; rating=4; time=2025-11-29T20:57:16.392931183Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Great Food, Room for Service Improvement

We had dinner at Socarrat as first-time visitors and absolutely loved every single dish! The ambiance and flavors were excellent.

The Highlights
The chocolate dip of the churros was phenomenal—rich, perfectly blended, and its subtle flavor profile worked magic with the churros' cinnamon coating. I'm still thinking about it even as I write this!

A Minor Note
The only slight letdown was the pork belly on the paella; I found it wasn't as tender as I would have preferred. However, everything else was truly delicious.

Customer Experience Suggestions
I had a great time, and the quality of the food is already exceptional. The overall customer experience could easily hit a full five stars with a few key service improvements:
1. Faster Response Time: Responding more quickly to online messages for reservations/inquiries.
2. Immediate Refreshments: Offering initial refreshments, like water, immediately after seating guests.
3. Food Explanation: Providing a brief, informative explanation of the food as it is delivered to the table.

Overall, I highly recommend Socarrat! || author=Yasmin Unda; rating=5; time=2026-02-17T15:55:37.433317408Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Had a wonderful experience at Socarrat, truly deserving of its Michelin selection. Even though we only tried a few dishes, every plate was beautifully executed and packed with flavor. Quality was evident in every bite.
Well worth it.

Just make sure to reserve ahead—they’re almost always fully booked. Definitely coming back to try more.

name: Pusô Bistro & Bar

sample_reviews:
author=Stephen McQuarrie; rating=5; time=2025-10-21T10:04:00.945283079Z; relative=6 months ago; text=First time eating at this venue. Located in the Quest Hotel in Cebu (where we are staying for the week) we decided to eat before check in. Plenty of eating area not crowded with back to back tables. We ate the lunch Buffett. The displays were amazing. Made your mouth water looking at the variety of food. I went back for seconds then had the chef whip me up some Mongolian stir fry. Food tasted good with only a few items a little cmolder than I desire. The staff were professional in every way. Always there to assist if needed and checked on our table offering different menu items, coffee, tea drinks. Truly a great eating experience for both my wife and I .  Overall we had a great experience and will definitely come back for more. || author=Vito Parzelle; rating=5; time=2025-04-21T01:38:17.392281Z; relative=a year ago; text=Puso offers an excellent breakfast buffet with great variety. The croissants and danishes were fresh, flaky and buttery as they should. There was fresh cooked tuna, longganisa, bacon, eggs cooked any way you like, assorted breads and cheeses, fruit, salad bar ... amazing! Delicious! || author=Boyet Chi-bu; rating=4; time=2026-01-07T02:32:53.312455925Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Pusô Bistro & Bar offers a delightful breakfast experience with a wide selection of reasonably priced buffet , including an abundance of bacon. I was promptly seated at 9am, and the friendly staff were proactive in clearing my used dishes. The food was delicious, especially for a buffet, and the egg station was a highlight. However, some empty serving dishes were not refilled or removed, such as an empty juice container and a crusty scrambled eggs dish. Overall, it was a pleasant dining experience with minor areas for improvement. || author=Anne Kirby; rating=3; time=2025-01-23T23:22:21.523810Z; relative=a year ago; text=If you’re heading to Pusô Bistro & Bar in Quest Hotel, Cebu, let me set your expectations: the atmosphere is lovely, the staff are fantastic, but let’s just say… don’t arrive hungry if you’re expecting top-tier cuisine.

First off, a huge shoutout to Erick (the manager) and Guelbert (the bartender). These two are absolute gems—professional, attentive, and the kind of people who make you feel like royalty, even if the kingdom around you is a little… chaotic. Honestly, their service was the highlight of the night.

As for the food, well, let’s call it a missed opportunity. The buffet was overrun in no time and not restocked fast enough, leaving diners circling the empty trays like vultures. Dietary restrictions? Forget about it. If you’re gluten-free, the options are practically nonexistent. It’s almost as if the menu comes with a silent disclaimer: “Best of luck!”

Adding to the mayhem, the dining experience lacked structure. Guests were darting from station to station with little rhyme or reason. Some tables seemed to have been blessed by the gods of customer service, while others (like mine) were left to fend for themselves. Getting attention from the staff was hit-or-miss, which is a shame because, when you do, they’re absolutely lovely.

In the end, the people and the atmosphere carry this place—just don’t expect the food to do any heavy lifting. If you’re after a vibrant vibe and great service (from the right employees), it’s worth a visit. But if food is your main focus, you might want to look elsewhere. || author=Dean Fearing; rating=5; time=2026-03-05T03:18:06.787079664Z; relative=a month ago; text=Stayed at Quest and got breakfast as part of the stay. Huge breakfast buffet with a lot of options. The food I had was good. The service was top notch.

author=Joey C.; rating=5; time=2026-02-20T11:31:49.496329104Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Looking for a casual dress down BBQ grill place where locals go and yummy food with late service and really good price? Just a heads-up though—the prices have noticeably increased compared to when they first opened. Still, for the taste and quality, many would say it’s worth it. || author=Pipoy_C; rating=5; time=2026-01-02T16:34:59.413279606Z; relative=3 months ago; text=⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️

Yaksi might be my bias, and for good reason. I’ve been eating their BBQ since my college days, back when they were still at B. Rodriguez. Seeing how they’ve grown into multiple branches over the years is honestly nice, and through all that, I still keep coming back for their spicy BBQ. That flavor never gets old.

I personally still prefer going to their main branch in Capitol. For me, the serving there is more consistent compared to some of the other branches. The quality just feels more reliable, especially when it comes to the BBQ.

Based on my experience, I highly recommend dine-in if you can. The food is best enjoyed fresh off the grill. If you really need to take out, it’s better to order directly from them rather than through a third-party app. I’ve noticed that BBQ orders via delivery apps often arrive overcooked. Still fresh, but definitely overcooked 😅

Overall, Yaksi will always be a favorite. Solid, nostalgic, and still satisfying after all these years. || author=loje; rating=1; time=2026-01-18T12:33:42.030084426Z; relative=3 months ago; text=I tried to order here through Grab. My family waited 2 hours for our food, just for them to cancel the order 10 minutes before the order was “supposed to arrive”. My family really liked this place, and would’ve understood if the restaurant was simply too busy. But, did they really have to make us wait those 2 hours before cancelling our order? We could’ve ordered something else instead had they cancelled the order immediately. Super disappointing. || author=AngBRYANKoh; rating=5; time=2025-02-08T12:10:28.550253Z; relative=a year ago; text=I recently dined at Yakski BBQ and had an absolutely amazing experience! What really stood out to me was how affordable everything was, especially for the quality and portion sizes. You get so much value for your money, making it a great spot for a casual meal with friends or family.

Overall, Yakski Barbecue is a must-try for anyone who loves great food at an affordable price.

name: Sialo Cebu
address: 7A Pres. Laurel St, Kasambagan, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Suzy Fayoumi; rating=5; time=2026-02-01T21:23:48.893101412Z; relative=2 months ago; text=One of the best fine dining experiences in a long time! We had the 13 course menu and it was from beginning to end truly amazing!

Every plate tells a story and combines so many (local) ingredients that all sing in harmony together. Every bite you have is one packed with great flavours.
The chef created picture perfect plates that look super appealing and you see the eye for detail in every plate. You can feel his pride for his country, his respect for produce and his passion for cooking and creating dishes that amaze his guests.

The staff is super warm and kind, professional and they also love to joke around. A perfect combination.

The price is, compared to Europe, ridiculously low for a 13-course fine dining experience.
I would highly recommend to go to Sialo! || author=Cyndy Ferolino; rating=5; time=2025-12-10T01:23:40.385849624Z; relative=4 months ago; text=I honestly came in expecting the food to be just okay - but wow, was I wrong. I was blown away by how beautifully each course was crafted. Every bite was a clever balance of flavors that delighted the palate and stirred up cherished memories of childhood meals.

Despite the fine dining setting, the ambiance was relaxed and comfortable. We felt at ease, free to forget about everything else and simply savor the gastronomical journey unfolding before us. The staff took such thoughtful care in serving and explaining each dish, adding even more meaning to the experience.

Our dinner lasted about two hours, and it was time - and money - truly well spent. We have absolutely no regrets choosing Sialo to celebrate our 13th wedding anniversary. If anything, we’re grateful for the 13-course journey that mirrored our marriage so far: rich, surprising, and deeply fulfilling.

Thank you, Sialo, for making our anniversary extra special - definitely one for the books. || author=Sebastian Foster; rating=5; time=2026-02-24T00:08:01.659737450Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Sialo in Cebu is widely recognized for elevating Filipino cuisine with modern techniques while preserving local flavors. It combines refined plating, quality ingredients, and a warm contemporary atmosphere, making it one of Cebu’s more polished dining destinations. || author=Benoit Doumas; rating=5; time=2026-01-17T03:51:56.435967496Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Fantastic experiences with 19 beautifully crafted dishes that make you travel across cebu. Truly the number one fine dining restaurant in Philippines.
The staff was amazing and so nice, passionate about giving us a top service. Wine selection was also very solid, the Spanish Tempranillo a very good pairing with the variety of food. || author=Rey Arvin Generale; rating=5; time=2026-01-08T17:05:33.257347854Z; relative=3 months ago; text=I am a Filipino who does not like Filipino food because I find the flavors too boring, one dimensional and overpowering. Sialo is the first thay truly serves authentic Filipino flavors with delicate balance and finesse. I have always hated utan bisaya for it's ve4y overpowering taste, slimy soup, and the crazy amount of vegetables in a bowl that really adds to the poor texture. We had the 19-course dinner and if it wasn't part of the menu, I would never have ordered it. It was easily the beat utan bisaya I have ever tasted. The flavor is authentic but the balance, the texture, the presentation was just perfect! All of their other food screamed perfection on a plate. Sialo set the standard in terms of how Filipino food should evolve!

Went because of the reviews.   Won’t go back.  Had a good meal.  Cost  a lot. Enjoyed but not wowed. || author=Jay Alacida; rating=5; time=2026-01-13T22:53:04.646994913Z; relative=3 months ago; text=I get it now, many people have been saying this is a great treat looking for a place to indulge themselves with the best food in metropolitan Cebu.

If you intend to visit this place, take a reservation first, especially during peak hours. It is noteworthy, if you are taking Maxim, Grab, or any related app to get you there. Make sure you know where to depart, because it is confusing. This house has 3 branches if you wanted the main branch it can be accessed via Capitol Road, or if you are nearby in the IT Park or Lahug can be accessed going to Deped R7 road. It’s quite tricky. Since that was my first time, my driver from Mandaue wasn’t  really familiar with the place although this has been popular to many, the result i have to booked another one because i arrived at wrong branch in Paseo in Banawa.

There are 3-4 branches now in the city I guess. One is in Shanks Paseo Arcenas via Green Valley Subdivision, this one for is the main branch, others Pitlane Restobar Acacia Steakhouse in Banawa. So please don’t forget to double check before heading to.

As you enter you will be welcomed with the smell of cooked meal. The atmosphere there and ambiance were really admirable. The vibe was amazing and service catered to us all incredible. The crews were great. The food and drinks were sumptuous and in competitive, pricy, of course.

Oh boy, i am no beef man so steak is not for me. But, the gambas, chicken were really good for me!

Cards or cash can be used on payments.

Overall, this has made my day with great company! Definitely coming back in the main! || author=Jobhelle Ann Montes; rating=5; time=2025-10-22T13:23:41.909204717Z; relative=6 months ago; text=​Had a wonderful and romantic date night at Acacia Steak House. We started the meal with the Gambas, which were sautéed perfectly in garlic and chili, giving us a fantastic, spicy kick to begin the night. We both ordered the Black Pepper Steak set and it was absolutely perfect. The steak was tender and juicy, enhanced by a fantastic, bold black pepper sauce. The side of mashed potatoes was heavenly—incredibly creamy and rich. Coupled with prompt and friendly service, this was a memorable, upscale dinner. The Black Pepper Steak set is a must-try!

The food is exceptional, beautifully presented, and full of flavor. Every dish reflects the restaurant’s commitment to quality and sophistication. name: Wolfgang's Steakhouse Grill - Cebu City

sample_reviews:
author=Rodney Co; rating=5; time=2026-02-15T09:42:02.569127009Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Finally, a true world-class steakhouse experience in Cebu.

We ordered the crab cakes, and they immediately reminded me of the best crab cakes I’ve had in Maryland — packed with real crab meat, perfectly seasoned, and beautifully seared. No fillers, just pure flavor.

The ribeye was exceptional. It honestly brought me back to the finest steakhouses in the U.S. — perfectly cooked, rich, tender, and full of that classic dry-aged depth. You can taste the quality in every bite.

The staff were attentive, professional, and genuinely warm. The ambience is elegant yet comfortable — perfect for date nights, celebrations, or closing serious business deals.

It’s great to know that this level of steakhouse experience is now available right here in Cebu.

Highly recommended. 👌🥩 || author=Kenneth Pitzer; rating=5; time=2026-02-25T07:56:19.810889247Z; relative=2 months ago; text=1st time ever to Wolfgang’s . Exactly what you should expect from such a high end restaurant !
The staff was well trained & knowledgeable .
The French onion soup was the best I’ve ever tasted !  The Lobster Mac & cheese was wonderful ! The Wolfgang’s signature salad was great . The pecan pie was also good .
Great job on opening this place . The interior is beautiful , even the CR is elegant !  Dark wood
for a classic steak house !  Staff is very friendly & confident !  Looking forward to come back for a great steak !  Hope to see Beef Wellington on a special some night !  Thank’s to are servers Grace & Edu || author=Cebu Food Critic; rating=5; time=2026-02-05T06:35:14.788014445Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Wolfgang's Steakhouse opened this week in the Northwing of SM Cebu City. Greeted and seated immediately, the chairs are very comfortable. The restaurant is a bit small and feels dark and romantic.
Complimentery fresh bread and butter was delicious and we were given 2 free glasses of a nice Rose wine.
I ordered the Caesar salad which was good, perfect amount of dressing but lacking in croutons. Ordered the steak fries, they were plentyful and perfect. The french onion soup was phenomenal. Best I ever had, savory, cheesy and hot and just off the charts excellent.
Now to the steak, I chose the 8 ounce Filet Mignon ordered medium rare with the peppercorn sauce. This was the amazing. Cut for me, the cook was perfect, the sear was perfect and the quality was Grade A, melt in the mouth delicious. Grilled to perfection with only salt and pepper.
The servers are trained to meet your every need and management is excellent. I will mention be prepared to be upselled. The staff has been trained to offer you higher priced food.  Edu my server tried hard to get me to order all the higher priced items on the menu.
Is it Worth It?
Some feel the price is too high for the quality and I can relate. But the reason I will return is on those special occasions when I want the best steak, I know now Wolfgangs has it. || author=Geian Karlo Callejo; rating=5; time=2026-03-07T13:44:57.799278288Z; relative=a month ago; text=The ambiance is great if you want an mildly intimate dinner with a loved one or a get together with family and friends. The staff are accommodating, you don’t need to call them, and they have the initiative to tend to the diner’s needs. The food is great, we ordered the porterhouse steak, jumbo lobster, and creme brûlée for dessert. The steak is great and worth its price, so is the creme brûlée, however the lobster could use some more boiling on butter but atill bery good. Overall an excellent experience. || author=M T; rating=3; time=2026-02-18T00:51:51.104356232Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Ate there last February 13,2026. Atmosphere is great, too dim though cant see the menu properly but its a fine dining restaurant what do you expect.

The meat we ordered was the Porterhouse for 3 (14,988 pesos) to be honest the meat wasnt worth the price. Was expecting better from it. Thats the only downside i could think of for the experience.

Service was great. Servers always tend to your needs. They have a lot of servers when i went.
Porterhouse - 6.5/10 (I have it low becuase of the price)

Grilled Salmon - 8/10
Mashed Potatoes - 8/10
Creamed Spinach - 8.5/10
Chopped salad - Tasted like a salad should

See photos and price below
MY OPINION. Try to experience first hand.

name: Itchoy Batchoyan sa Cebu by Pares Batchoy Food House
address: 12 C. Rodriguez Street, Capitol Site, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Franz Buns Zaitsev; rating=4; time=2026-03-01T01:06:18.574401878Z; relative=a month ago; text=Pares sauce adding to batchoy soup? Why not. The place is near the hospitals and a lot of people come here to visit and eat, from students to employees. I like the simple vibe, even though the restaurant employees talk too loud to each other. I ordered the batchoy pares, I enjoyed it. Price was reasonable. || author=Nika Izabelle Duran; rating=4; time=2025-11-20T11:33:45.209778572Z; relative=5 months ago; text=Great place if you’re craving for batchoy! I ordered the regular batchoy and sisig.

Batchoy tastes really great partnered with their pandesal (LOVE THIS SM) and I like how the noodles capture the overall taste of the batchoy. The batchoy in itself is flavorful and peppery, and for me just right. It’s a good for one batchoy and if you order other foods (like what I did), the batchoy becomes good for 2.

I also really like their sisig, but it’s on the less sizzling side when it arrives on the table.  Break the chillis and your mouth will be on fire! I prefer my sisig a bit more drier though compared to what they serve, but their sisig is okay overall.

Prices are okay for the portion that they serve.

Staff was kind and very helpful to us. They give complementary pares soups as well which is a great way to introduce their pares and it was yummy!

Atmosphere is nice but the area was a lil bit hot despite the aircon turned on.

Lastly, there was a fly going around our tables so that’s minus points for me.

Nonetheless, the place is okay and would rate a 4/5. Would definitely eat here again next time and try their other dishes like the bestseller pares batchoy ✨ || author=Queen Dy Guani; rating=5; time=2026-02-11T10:57:31.628899425Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Got the la paz batchoy and serving was generous with just the right amount of noodles, tender beef, and an egg. Soup is rich and peppery. You can’t go wrong for 179.00! Place has a lot of flies but I guess that’s part of the experience. Very good deal for a bib gourmand awarded restaurant! || author=Kamote 6127; rating=3; time=2025-11-29T10:24:30.600927453Z; relative=4 months ago; text=The service was notably swift, with dishes arriving at the table faster than expected—a promising start to the meal.

I began with the Bone Marrow Pares, which featured a marrow that leaned unexpectedly sweet, almost as if infused with butter. While the texture was rich and indulgent, the sweetness slightly overshadowed the savory depth typically associated with a good pares. The accompanying soup, unfortunately, suffered from an overpowering presence of star anise, muting what could have been a more balanced, aromatic broth.

For my main dish, I tried the Sisig Silog. The sisig appeared to be made primarily from pig face cuts, lacking the additional components—like liver—that usually give the dish complexity and character. Flavor-wise, the profile was dominated almost entirely by salt and sugar. From the first bite to the last, the excessive seasoning created a cloying and dizzying effect, overshadowing any natural pork flavors.

Overall, while the restaurant delivers quick service, the dishes would benefit greatly from better flavor balance and more thoughtful seasoning. || author=Bryan Mcdonald; rating=3; time=2026-01-21T11:26:36.177100798Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Not sure how this place got a Michelin recommendation. Average at best. The Batchoy was just odd tasting. My girlfriend came home and got sick. Tiny place,  but I will say it is a great value.  Less than$12US for 3 dishes,  a beer  juice and extra rice. Not a place I would go back to.

While ordering to change the smoothie to a juice, staff declines cancellation as it was punched already 😂 even if i just told her less than a minute, I was still in front of the counter. Staff left twice the counter while I was ordering. Seems like they did not undergo a restaurant /hospitality seminar. Did the management just pulled them out somewhere? || author=Shanzy Agustines; rating=3; time=2025-12-12T21:18:59.911501187Z; relative=4 months ago; text=The place is nice especially it's giving aesthetic vibes but kinda disappointed in some of their staff like their cashier she's not giving her full attention to us because she's talking to the other staff that's why she didn't able to punch all of our order. And also knowing it's a restaurant I'm kinda expecting  that when we enter there's some staff who will accommodate to us immediately such as giving us the food menu, instead we figured out it ourselves to order over the counter. Drinks is kinda expensive 195 pesos for a hot mocha that is only half of the cup it's not full. As a service crew also, I hope this will make any improvements in your service. || author=Raphael James Mansueto; rating=3; time=2026-02-14T12:48:18.814297834Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Do not go here on Valentine’s Day or during peak holidays, it’s a mess. Food was served without any cutlery, and our table number was taken away before staff even checked if all our orders had arrived. I had to stand up and find a waiter myself just to request cutlery and follow up on our missing items. Very frustrating and disorganized experience. Management seriously needs to improve service during busy times. || author=Joy 921; rating=5; time=2025-08-30T06:25:41.484993734Z; relative=8 months ago; text=Romantic place for couples. Nice place with view of the city lights. We went there for dinner. Decent food servings has ice cream bar and wide selection of liquors and wine. || author=Baby Ruth Estrada; rating=1; time=2025-12-21T01:55:13.057928746Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Not worth it, there are far better places than this. ₱200 entrance fee, fully consumable, but the food’s not appetizing, we just bought wine to take out instead. We tried one croissant to use up all the fee but the pastry isn’t good. Service is bad, the staff’s minding their own business. The place is not properly maintained - if they’re aiming for the antique/retro Parisian vibes, this just isn’t it; most are just worn out, old furniture pieces & the sofas look like you would get an itch if you sit on it. I think you need to be wine drunk in order to see the good stuff, visiting at night could also help. The city view is really nice, but you could also get that from Tops or elsewhere. Cellular signal is weak and they charge ₱10/hour for the wifi. Overall experience is not good, wouldn’t come back and wouldn’t recommend.

name: Xin Tian Di - NUSTAR
address: NUSTAR Resort & Casino Kawit Island, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Chris Francis; rating=5; time=2025-11-11T04:41:35.015865548Z; relative=5 months ago; text=Xin Tian Di, located in Cebu, is a delightful dining establishment under the Nustar brand that offers a unique blend of culinary experiences. The restaurant is designed to provide guests with an inviting atmosphere, complemented by a menu that features a variety of Asian-inspired dishes.
One of the standout items on their menu is the Ube Bochic, a dessert that captures the essence of Filipino flavors with a modern twist. This treat features a soft, chewy exterior filled with a rich and creamy ube (purple yam) paste, which is a beloved ingredient in Filipino cuisine known for its vibrant color and distinctive taste. The combination of the slightly sweet and earthy flavor of ube, paired with the delightful texture of the buchi, makes the Ube Bochic a must-try for anyone looking to indulge in a unique culinary experience.
Whether you’re craving comfort food or exploring new flavors, Xin Tian Di offers a memorable dining experience that beautifully reflects the region's rich culinary heritage. || author=Caitleen Allego; rating=5; time=2026-02-17T11:41:22.040476695Z; relative=2 months ago; text=I was there for a simple family gathering with relatives, I have been to the location before but forgot to leave a review. But recently when I came back I remembered to, the food was amazing, I ordered the Hainaneise Chicken and it was absolutely delicious, the chicken was perfect and even more so paired with the rice and the soup. The service was quick, they were attentive and very kind. And the Atmosphere truly gave me that feeling I was looking forward to having since I was also celebrating Chinese new year. Overall, amazing. || author=Glenn Bermejo; rating=4; time=2022-07-28T13:24:22.088094Z; relative=3 years ago; text=Maybe I was expecting too much from a Chinese restaurant from a much hyped hotel and casino resort but I was not very impressed by the furnishings and overall ambiance of this restaurant.  The place wasn’t that big, and I wouldn’t consider it luxurious and I don’t think it had that wow factor. I wonder why the server asked for our name and contact info. Nevertheless, contrary to most reviews here, my friends and I did not really wait long for our orders to be served. Maybe because we came during lunchtime on a Thursday. Our servers were very friendly and accommodating.  The food servings were rather small, but we knew about it beforehand because we asked the server how many servings each dish is for, so we ordered enough to fill all four of us in our party. I must admit, their dishes were quite delicious. The menu was very limited compared to other Chinese restaurants in the city, but their dimsum is quite authentic.  For the first time I found chicken feet comparable in size and quality as what I’m accustomed to at Dimsum restaurants in the U.S. The food was not that expensive considering its quality and taste. I would go back if I visited the Casino again, but I will not make a special trip specifically just to dine in at this restaurant. || author=Juan Mariano; rating=4; time=2024-08-08T20:41:32.473426Z; relative=a year ago; text=Well-regarded Chinese restaurant specializing in Cantonese cuisine. Elegant dining atmosphere and attentive service. Must try dim sum, Peking duck, and fresh seafood, all prepared with high-quality ingredients. While the prices are premium, the experience is considered worth it for those seeking a sophisticated take on authentic Cantonese dining. || author=Randee; rating=1; time=2026-02-16T13:29:30.499992653Z; relative=2 months ago; text=On my 3rd visit, i Went on a Monday, dinner for 10. Ordered Banquet A.  Please DO NOT ORDER Banquet A. The food was delivered bit by bit, the service normally good was extremely poor. Normally the waiters would pour your tea & serve your soup, they didn't, we fend for ourselves, the staff behaved as if they're new & clueless. The food was disgusting, the duck was old, hard & served cold. The dessert Buchibuchi was served cold. Tonight's experience was a downfall. Previous visit was good. This visit 1 out of 10. I wasted over P8000. Find a different restaurant if I were you.

sample_reviews:
author=Radz Padriga; rating=2; time=2025-12-31T03:16:32.817584377Z; relative=3 months ago; text=The food presentation was beautiful and the setting is really nice, which initially made us excited to try the dishes.
