At first, we were told tofu was no longer available, then later we were informed that it was still available for the agedashi tofu.

We understand that accommodating vegetarian requests can be challenging, but we hope there can be more care and consistency for vegetarian options in the future. || author=valeriy ogurtsov; rating=5; time=2026-02-11T11:08:19.923641293Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Everything was fine, good place to meet sunset with nice food and service. Worth to visit for sure. Stuff is very kind and helpful. || author=The Ramases; rating=2; time=2025-04-11T06:44:28.729389Z; relative=a year ago; text=If you’re looking for delicious Japanese food, this place isn’t it. Their tonkotsu ramen, which costs ₱512, tastes like instant noodles. The sushi rolls and aburi were dry and cloying — same with the udon.

There were two servers when we dined in. One of them was okay, but the other looked annoyed, didn’t smile. Maybe it’s because we arrived at 12:30 PM (even though their website says they close at 2 PM). They told us the cut-off is actually at 1 PM and they close by 1:30 PM.

The place also isn’t well maintained — the chandeliers were broken and dusty.

Overall, the prices are way too high for how everything tasted. || author=Aydan Khalilova; rating=1; time=2024-12-27T05:57:10.339467Z; relative=a year ago; text=PROCESSED FISH! Avoid at all costs! -We had dinner here a few weeks ago, and to say I was disappointed is an understatement. The food was absolutely horrible. I don’t usually write bad reviews, but this experience demands one because people need to know what they’re walking into before wasting their hard-earned money on this so-called Japanese restaurant.

First of all, the sushi is overpriced, and for what? Processed fish. That’s right not fresh fish, which is the bare minimum you’d expect at a sushi place. In the pictures it might look nice and presentable, but don’t let that fool you. The sushi tasted like it had been sitting out for a week. We couldn’t even finish the meal because the taste was unbearable.

We had to call the staff over and to speak to the manager because something was clearly wrong. At first, they denied it, but after the manager finally admitted that the fish was not fresh and was in fact, processed!

This place is a disgrace to Japanese cuisine. Avoid it at all costs. Go literally anywhere else, your stomach and wallet will thank you. || author=Christine Paula Love Bernasor; rating=4; time=2022-07-31T17:07:45.150261Z; relative=3 years ago; text=Servings were quite big and worth the price. The service was quick and attentive. The dishes were quite good and flavorful. The only thing I would change is that their sushi tend to have a thicker layer of the rice which I am not so keen on. Overall, a good dining experience, I would recommend for those that want Japanese food with a nice view and less people.

name: STK ta Bay!
address: 6 A. Climaco St, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Kleinberg Fletcher Tabel; rating=5; time=2025-11-01T07:55:55.527720243Z; relative=5 months ago; text=STK ta Bai easily ranks among Cebu’s must-visit dining landmarks — a true heritage dining experience that blends nostalgia, flavor, and heart. Imagine stepping into a friend’s 19th-century ancestral home, greeted by the scent of grilled seafood and adobo wafting through the air, and being treated to a feast that feels both familiar and grand. That’s exactly what it’s like here.

The food? Absolutely top-tier. We tried their Adobong Nokus (squid adobo) and baked scallops — each dish bursting with flavor and cooked to perfection. Portions are generous, so you’re definitely getting value for money. It’s that kind of home-cooked comfort food that feels elevated, not just in taste but in presentation.

The service deserves special mention. Our server was genuinely warm, attentive, and welcoming — the kind of hospitality that makes you feel like an honored guest, not just a customer. From the moment we arrived until we finished our meal, the team was nothing short of exceptional.

And then there’s the ambiance — the crown jewel of STK ta Bai. The restaurant is set in a beautifully preserved bahay-na-bato, adorned with antiques and old photographs that whisper stories of Cebu’s past. Dining here feels like being transported back in time, surrounded by the charm and soul of old Cebu.

If your time in Cebu is short, make sure STK ta Bai is on your list. And if you have more days to spare, dine here twice — once for the nostalgia, and again for that grilled tuna I’m saving for my next visit. Truly, one of the best dining experiences Cebu has to offer. || author=Anabel Morelos; rating=5; time=2025-07-29T05:17:11.800757894Z; relative=9 months ago; text=STK ta Bay offers delicious Filipino seafood dishes served in a setting that exudes old-world charm. The vintage decor and antique pieces make the dining experience feel both nostalgic and cozy. It’s a must-visit in Cebu for those who love great food paired with a touch of heritage. || author=Logan Ly; rating=5; time=2024-11-20T12:04:43.818391Z; relative=a year ago; text=Truly spectacular Filipino food!! Where surf meets turf. In a traditional ancestral house, this restaurant has really friendly service as well as great meat and seafood dishes. Awesome portion sizes and affordable price point too for such quality and fresh food😋 It was the highlight of the evening for me.

This was such a delightful find in Cebu, and really if I had more stomach room I would’ve love to try out more dishes! || author=zec yan; rating=1; time=2024-12-23T12:05:32.062885Z; relative=a year ago; text=Food was bad, not sure how they got such good reviews. But on the bright side, service was great.

Tuna belly was charred till someone would’ve gotten cancer the next day after eating it.

Fried prawns and crispy kangkong were not deepfried well and contained way too much moisture.

Fried rice was soggy and oily.

Honestly, food was also luke warm and not served hot at all. || author=Maria; rating=5; time=2024-11-23T09:17:28.264316Z; relative=a year ago; text=We had a pleasant evening in this very nicely decorated restaurant, with ambient music, with special people, welcoming, kind, attentive. The food is absolutely delicious!  We had sissling gambas, grilled pork belly and a salad with seaweed, pickled ginger, with a special taste.  Everything was just perfect! Congratulations, you are doing an excellent job! 💯👍👏🫶

I am very pleased about the place! Great views! Great food! Great service. The lady gave me a king chair 🪑 😂 we where 8 and i was the only one offered the king chair! 😂🤣🤣 love it 😍 || author=Timothy Carter; rating=5; time=2025-09-02T03:10:41.463071770Z; relative=7 months ago; text=Wonderful view of Cebu City. Great salad and watermelon shake. Filet mignon was good. No waiting late night. || author=Jeoffrey Gudio; rating=5; time=2025-02-18T13:39:36.859164Z; relative=a year ago; text=My team enjoyed a wonderful dinner under the cool, cloudy night sky. The food was delicious, and the portions were great for sharing.  The servers were attentive and ready to help.

I'd been here before the pandemic and had a great time.  I was excited to come back now that they've reopened.

There's plenty of parking, and lots of seating for families, friends, parties, and special events. || author=Gniddew Liame; rating=4; time=2025-04-16T03:22:50.537257Z; relative=a year ago; text=Visited in April '25 with family after visiting TOPS.
As we were driving up, we saw a sign for Mr. A which was tucked away from the main road.

They have an extensive menu which has a good mix between Filipino, Chinese and Italian.
Their portions vary! Which can be great for sharing meals as some dishes are massive as is the case for their Lomi/Calamari.
If you're with a big group of people, this restaurant can get pricey real fast.
They tack on a 10% service charge on your bill.

The restaurant is sizable and they have powerful speakers.
Other patrons were singing karaoke which we thought was off-putting; especially during dinner.
A live band or performer, sure. But maybe singing patrons can be tucked away in a private, karaoke room.
I know folk love being a main character; but sometimes you just want an easygoing dinner where you can actually hear people at your table.

The ambiance was alright, they had led lamps which you need to read their menu as it was pitch black.
Mosquitoes were also feasting during dinner; we recommend switching the lighting to warm/yellow as the cool/blue light seemed to attract them.

I have to say, this is probably one of the best pizzas I have had in Cebu!
It is very authentic, and tastes fresh (not a pre-made frozen pizza!).
Bit of a trek to get to if not in the area but would be keen to bring my partner and friends when we find ourselves back in Cebu.

name: Olio Restaurant & Lounge
address: Unit T, Crossroads Mall, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Whity; rating=5; time=2025-10-03T01:06:45.416715664Z; relative=6 months ago; text=We visited as a group of four, men and woman. Rather than a typical restaurant, the place had the atmosphere of a stylish lounge where adults can relax. Since our goal was to enjoy a good steak, each of us chose from their selection of ribeye and tenderloin according to our preference. The two men, being hungry, went for sharing the “buy one, take one” promo on US ribeye. Each 250-gram block was truly impressive, and the doneness was carefully adjusted to our liking. The women chose the grilled Norwegian salmon. But what left the strongest impression was the oysters we ordered as an appetizer (I forget the origin), served Salisbury-style on the grill—the balance of cheese and seasoning was simply exquisite. || author=Philip Raymond Lim; rating=5; time=2025-04-21T10:57:40.079479Z; relative=a year ago; text=Olio wins the crown! No question about it. For more than a decade, the quality has remained consistently excellent. The service is top-tier: warm, attentive, and professional without being intrusive. I had the chance to try their steak, oyster, truffle pasta, Chilean sea bass, and a few other dishes, and each one was masterfully prepared. Lots of good wine options. This is high-end dining done right. Perfect for date nights or intimate dinner. Very private. The ambiance is quietly simple and elegant, making it one of my favourite places to unwind and enjoy a meal in Cebu if I’d have to pick a place. A true gem that continues to set the bar. This has got to be in my top five in Cebu. || author=Jonathan S. Ng; rating=3; time=2025-08-26T08:01:35.177391457Z; relative=8 months ago; text=Olio presents itself as the premier restaurant at Crossroads Cebu, though the experience falls short of the high expectations set by its reputation and accolades.

The baked scallops were delicious but suffered from being a bit too creamy, overwhelming the delicate seafood flavor that should be the star of this premium appetizer. The carbonara fettuccine proved more successful, delivering delicious flavors with generous portions of bacon and mushroom that created a satisfying and well-balanced pasta dish.

Unfortunately, the beef stroganoff pasta was too salty, while the beef proved disappointingly tough - fundamental execution failures for what should be a comfort food staple. The cream of mushroom soup was decent and worked particularly well when paired with their fresh bread, providing a reliable if unremarkable start to the meal.

The baked salmon featured a very generous cut and proved decent in preparation, offering good value through portion size even if the preparation didn't reach memorable levels.

I felt that Olio was somewhat overrated based on the overwhelmingly positive reviews on Google Maps and various awards it has received. The restaurant is certainly not THE BEST in Cebu, far from it, especially considering that their basic dishes were not executed perfectly - a concerning issue when fundamental preparations show inconsistency.

Additionally, the staff were inattentive and not focused throughout the meal, creating service gaps that detracted from the dining experience. Each dish proves quite expensive compared to similar restaurants of the same caliber, making the combination of service issues and execution problems even more frustrating.

Olio delivers adequate dining with some successful dishes, but the gap between reputation and reality, combined with high pricing and inattentive service, makes it difficult to recommend when Cebu offers better value and execution elsewhere. || author=Karen Faith; rating=5; time=2025-03-13T05:47:20.689857Z; relative=a year ago; text=Excellent restaurant just outside of IT Park in Cebu. We started with the portobello salad and it was fabulous! We loved the Salmon, the Chilean seaman's, and the lamb ribs. The sauces are delectable! We recommend the chocolate Soufflé for dessert! The chef certainly knows what he is doing! The atmosphere is quiet, with lovely ambient music, and is excellent for business as well as dates or gathering of friends!  The staff is so kind, especially Marc, who really aims to please! || author=Chi Go; rating=5; time=2025-11-29T12:41:34.733043331Z; relative=4 months ago; text=the best lobster risotto i have ever tried. sincerely the best!!! enjoyed it to the last risotto!!!

name: Manam SM City Cebu

sample_reviews:
author=Chris ian Mendez; rating=5; time=2026-02-15T11:37:18.838232102Z; relative=2 months ago; text=I had such a wonderful dining experience at this restaurant! The food was absolutely delicious from start to finish. Every dish was beautifully presented and bursting with flavor. You can really tell that fresh, high-quality ingredients are used, and the attention to detail in each plate makes a big difference.

The flavors were perfectly balanced — rich, satisfying, and thoughtfully prepared. The portions were generous without being overwhelming, and everything tasted freshly made. Each bite felt special and well-crafted.

I’ll definitely be coming back, and I highly recommend this place to anyone looking for amazing food and a memorable meal! || author=KD Abellana; rating=5; time=2026-02-09T01:56:57.538895589Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Manam never disappoints when it comes to comforting Filipino flavors. The crispy sisig was sizzling, crunchy, and packed with bold flavor in every bite. The pancit was light yet savory, perfectly cooked and great for sharing. The beef sinigang with watermelon was the standout, rich and tangy with a subtle sweetness that made it extra refreshing. Their food is more on the Luzon type of Filipino food. Their ambiance if very nice and Homey.  The waiting is more than an hour maybe because we dined in there during Valentine’s day.

A must try Restaurant in SM City Cebu. || author=JESSICA ABUYABOR; rating=5; time=2026-01-18T13:40:02.466348599Z; relative=3 months ago; text=I had a wonderful time dining at this restaurant! name: Tong Yang, SM Seaside Cebu
address: Lower Ground Floor, SM Seaside City, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=C G; rating=4; time=2026-01-13T13:59:49.305771912Z; relative=3 months ago; text=We went there for the hotpot and we were not disappointed.

The table have individual induction stoves for the hotpot soup and a central round grill.

What I love about the individual hotpot is that you can choose your own even if its different from your group. I got spicy mala, my partner got saté soup and our friend got the pork soup. Spicy mala is more flavorful than the saté soup.

They have two out of stock soups, one is laksa and we were looking forward to try it.

They have all the food for the hotpot too. From veggies to seafood balls, beef, pork and seafood too. Widest array of raw meats from seafood, pork, chicken and beef that I have ever seen. Also very much appreciate the sauce station, they have unli calamansi and garlic!

They have two kinds of noodles, instant ramen noodles and sotanghon.

On their cooked food, I love the pancit, mapo tofu, chicken siomai and sweet & sour chicken.

Take a pass on their sushi and mini cakes. For the dessert, go for the halo-halo sans the langka, and go for the fruit, ice cream and free popsicle.

I love the drinks too! I love the nutrifrizz cola frizz and I mix it with 7-up. I also made iced americano since they have a coffee vending machine.

They have san mig draft beer which I didn't try.

Overall, I absolutely love the freedom of choice that you get from a wide variety of selections. There are hits and misses thus the 4 star, but overall, its an enjoyable experience! We will hopefully be back for more! || author=Brent Go; rating=1; time=2025-11-12T11:30:34.572915457Z; relative=5 months ago; text=No filter review.

For a quality cost, you get to see a limited amount of food, tempura is hidden and mentioned that they do not serve anymore but they might. Raw meats are available except for the beef and pork because they are placed at the back counter where you have to ask to be served. I understand that they do not have a lot of customers and that they have to do their best to stay alive, either by limiting food variations, or posting reviews using profiles that do not actually review food to keep the ratings in Google high, but they have to understand that the quality of service when I went there was just awful and it shows why it is struggling to be the best hotpot experience despite having a sign near their ball stations that you will experience the best hotpot.

I love food but i even struggle to choose from what they can offer. I can make better dishes than what they can provide. I have provided my friends my own dishes made from the ingredients there. I even asked for a raw egg, which I do not know if that is okay.

You can see all the ingredients piling up in their box behind the stations, but the plates in the stations are constantly empty, if not, old and probably spoiled. My remark to this is that what you can save now can be served tomorrow, and that is what I felt  with the old, dark fishballs.

I'm not angry with my experience because I was having fun with whatever comes my way when I ate there. But with the amount of fake reviews that I have seen here, I think it would be best to shed some light that during my dining experience on an evening that half of the restaurant was empty. || author=Tryker Segovia; rating=5; time=2026-03-07T12:48:02.614858097Z; relative=a month ago; text=Ate Lucila is a very kind and accommodating lady. Her friendly attitude and willingness to help make a big difference, and it shows how much she cares about the people she serves. Thank you, Ate Lucila, for your kindness and for going the extra mile to assist others. Your effort and positive spirit are truly appreciated. 😊 || author=Harlan Sanchez; rating=1; time=2025-10-24T03:37:38.356988768Z; relative=6 months ago; text=When we went to get dessert, we saw cockroaches crawling around. name: Balay Sa Busay

sample_reviews:
author=Jayson De guia; rating=5; time=2026-03-10T01:57:23.824069692Z; relative=a month ago; text=An honest review of Balay Sa Busay is that it’s a scenic mountaintop Filipino restaurant in Cebu City that many people visit for its breathtaking views of the city and sunset, cool breezes, and relaxed atmosphere, making it a memorable spot for special occasions or meals with friends and family. Many diners praise the Filipino comfort food like coconut shrimp, sisig, and other home‑style dishes and find the overall experience pleasant with generally friendly service and reasonable pricing for the setting. However, honest reviews also note that the food quality and consistency can be hit or miss—with some saying certain dishes were just average or underseasoned, drinks like shakes were disappointing, and service can be slow or limited during busy times—so the culinary experience doesn’t always match the beautiful location. Overall, Balay Sa Busay is worth visiting for the view and atmosphere, but expectations for food and service should be moderate rather than perfect. || author=Armi Shane Salvatierra; rating=5; time=2025-09-02T14:00:16.519798741Z; relative=7 months ago; text=Great place, great food, great view! Prices are okay too! The staffs are all nice. It's a nice place to check out when in Cebu || author=Zion; rating=4; time=2024-04-23T03:11:37.189560Z; relative=2 years ago; text=Food was excellent. Had the coconut shrimp, it was heaven on earth. The serving was little compared to price, but still affordable. The view was magnificent, a bit cold. The place was a little ran down, outdated and quite old. Service was great, it would be better to have more staff. Thanks for offering the free halo halo.

Downside, please improve your drinks. It was really bad, sugary but bland. I had mango shake without the taste of mango.

Highly recommended, quite far. Will visit again but not soon, great for special occasion. || author=Geezelle Maningo; rating=5; time=2018-10-16T16:30:40.447185Z; relative=7 years ago; text=Delicious Food and Breathtaking View
The food has familiar taste yet still creatively presented. Childhood memories and family gatherings flashback while I was enjoying my meal. Good service, and waiting time around 15 minutes.

It is located about 200 meters from main Busay road to Tops road. Going to Tops it is highly visible on the left side.

✔Free WiFi
✔Parking space
✔Wheelchair accessible
✔High chair for toddlers || author=Carlo Andrew Olano; rating=5; time=2018-10-02T01:47:28.695544Z; relative=7 years ago; text=This is fast becoming my favorite restaurant in the area. Great view, amazing food and awesome ambience. The view is just spectacular as you can dine while overlooking metro Cebu. The food is good old Filipino food with familiar flavors but with some enjoyable twists. The place is perfect for families and couples.  I would surely be back here.

The Social is a fantastic spot to chill, eat, and enjoy drinks in a lively atmosphere. The food was delicious and came out fast, and the cocktails were well-made. The service was excellent — friendly and attentive staff made everything easy and smooth.

The ambiance is modern, energetic, and perfect for both casual nights or meeting people. One of the best places in the area for good food and fun vibes. Highly recommended! || author=Will O'Connor; rating=5; time=2025-12-10T15:37:28.809534797Z; relative=4 months ago; text=I had a night to kill in Cebu City before I flew back to the UK. I went on a Thursday night and found it to be quite busy. Half is indoors and the other half extends out on to the roof garden of the Ayala Mall (4th floor).
I wasn't eating and just wanted a beer. The beer is more expensive than most places if you buy them individually but there was a good deal on the ice bucket with 5 beers which was a very reasonable 520 pesos.
There was a live band and they performed a good version of Deep Purple's Highway Star which was something I didn't expect to hear. Service was good and they even put fresh ice in my bucket after I had drunk 2 or 3 to keep the remaining ones cold.
Cold beer, live music, I couldn't really ask for more. Just wish I had arrived earlier to hear a bit more of the band. || author=Calvin Duong (The Goods); rating=3; time=2025-04-22T09:12:16.126965Z; relative=a year ago; text=I visited The Social located on the 4th floor of Ayala Mall in Cebu City around 9 PM on a Monday night, looking for some Western-style food. The experience was okay, but the service could have been better. We waited 25 minutes after being seated to receive our order, and then another 10 minutes for the bill after asking for it. We eventually had to go to the bar to request it ourselves. The food was decent; we had the Caesar salad with a nice olive dressing and a poached egg, and the chicken parmigiana with a spring salad and fries. The mojito was on the stronger side, which was nice. There were quite a few tourists, and it felt like the servers catered more to the Western guests. Overall, while the food was good, the service left something to be desired.

sample_reviews:
author=John Harlow; rating=5; time=2026-02-14T13:11:30.753537300Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Great food at a good price. The super thin sweat and sour pork is about the best I have had. We beef with flat noodles was also very good as were the spring rolls.
Highly recommended. || author=Trevin Lingren; rating=4; time=2025-10-10T20:38:13.566932173Z; relative=6 months ago; text=Somehow at 7:30 on a Friday night, my fiancé  and I were the only ones there, except for a private party in an adjoining room. We had fried pork in sweet and sour sauce (my fiancé loved it and I liked it, I’m just used to meat chunks being fried not just some pork fat in a thin crispy layer), pineapple fried rice (came also with chicken and shrimp and was our favorite food item) and a seafood soup that was just okay. The table cloth and my tea cup were dirty, which is never acceptable. Service was very friendly and professional. Prices are on the higher side because of the nice hotel it’s in (The Waterford). Oh—I also had a sidecar cocktail and it was truly outstanding 🍸 || author=Ryan Williams; rating=5; time=2026-01-13T12:26:38.797257292Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Best Chinese food I have ever had.  Service was so good.  Robert our waiter especially took good care of me and steered me off my initial maim course choice.  Just an excellent experience.  A little pricey but worth it. || author=Glenn Bermejo; rating=5; time=2022-04-10T12:57:39.663060Z; relative=4 years ago; text=When I told my friends I was having lunch at Tin Gow, they told me that I should go somewhere else. The place was expensive and the food was not so good. So I came with some prejudices when my cousin invited us for her birthday lunch. It was an 11 course meal which she customized and ordered in advance for three tables.  First came the dried scallop soup with vegetable green which looked plain and simple, but it surprisingly impressed my taste buds. That first impression got me excited for the dishes that were to follow:  Shrimp salad, crispy roasted chicken, steamed Lapulapu fish, Sweet and sour pork, Dim sum sampler platter, braised beef, the birthday pancit, Stir fried green beans, and finally fried rice with fried anchovies. For dessert,  it was Japanese fried eggroll with cream cheese and seaweed, and sesame balls. Even I was amazed by my appetite at this table. I loved every single dish.  Well prepared, delicious, properly textured and well presented. I was looking forward to their Peking duck which was not available because of the pandemic. I learned they import their Peking duck from  Hongkong. I guess that will be a good reason to go back. I will definitely proclaim Tin Gow as one of the best, if not the best Chinese restaurant in Cebu.  I believe good restaurant food is knowing what to order and what you’re ordering. Prior visits aid in making that decision. Knowing the chef and talking to them also helps. This was definitely a gastronomically memorable dining experience. || author=Rachel Ho; rating=5; time=2025-05-20T08:04:31.837796Z; relative=11 months ago; text=Delicious, authentic Cantonese cuisine in Cebu, although it is on the pricier side. The sweet & sour pork was thin and so crispy!

Atmosphere was clean, casual, comfortable and nicely done to feel inviting and relaxing. I will return for the salsa on Wednesdays and Sunday and try some other dish. || author=Stefen Fangmeier; rating=2; time=2026-02-11T04:54:21.078832307Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Tried this Mexican restaurant. The portions are massive. Had the guacamole which was a generous portion. It lacked flavor, not enough garlic or raw onions. Then the tostada, also rather large. A bit more flavor, but not really enough.
After finishing it, I felt immediately bloated. The next morning, my stomach was rather upset, which I don’t need to describe here.
I also had three margaritas due to the happy hour deal. They tasted odd, probably an artificial lime mix. I prefer them with just fresh lime juice and nothing else other than the tequila.

name: SUKIDESU MODERN JAPANESE RESTAURANT
types: japanese_restaurant, chicken_restaurant, ramen_restaurant, restaurant, food, point_of_interest, establishment

sample_reviews:
author=Mark David Calzada; rating=4; time=2026-02-14T13:02:00.341981239Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Another one of Cebu's hidden gems. I was randomly walking along the streets until I came across this place as I was intrigued by their name. They provide affordable japanese cuisines and their raman was decently good! The staff were all friendly and greeted me in Japanese when I came in. Their katsudon however has room for improvement. But for the price of their food and the quality I am receiving, i'd say it was really good and I definitely would recommend this place. || author=Hero Aldrieme Batiancila; rating=5; time=2025-11-23T10:55:14.548314777Z; relative=5 months ago; text=I stumbled on Sukidesu while waiting for my phone to get fixed (at Rize), and wow, I’m so glad I did. I was craving Japanese food so badly, and walking in felt like a tiny escape — spacious, cool, and full of Japanese décor that instantly lifted my mood. The staff even greeted us in Japanese, which made me smile like a kid.

We ordered Ajitama Ramen, Black Ramen, Prawn Tempura Rice Bowl, and California Maki. And honestly? This is the best affordable Japanese food I’ve had in Cebu so far. The ramen tasted real — rich, comforting, not too salty, not too oily. The tempura? Perfection. Light, fluffy, and so satisfying. The maki was good and nicely balanced, though I wish the mango flavor popped a little more.

The staff were genuinely kind and accommodating, even suggesting a cooler spot for us because of the heat outside. Service was fast, the place was clean, and despite the simple look, it felt warm and inviting.

For the price, super worth it. I’d definitely return if I’m in the Fuente area. Highly recommended for Japanese food lovers on a budget or couples looking for a cozy date spot.

In one sentence: A surprisingly authentic, heartwarming Japanese meal without breaking the budget. || author=Faith Go; rating=4; time=2025-12-06T06:14:25.533962630Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Went there at 11am just as they opened. We got the Mt. Apo maki which was fried and their sukidesu maki with tuna and cheese on top. We also ordered their okonomiyaki and cheese tempura. It was good for its price since they were very affordable. Was seated inside their indoor dining space. || author=John Paul Robin; rating=4; time=2025-05-21T23:16:40.598305Z; relative=11 months ago; text=The ramen was a bit too salty for my liking, which was disappointing. On the bright side, the bento and maki were excellent—fresh, flavorful, and nicely presented. I’d recommend it if you’re into sushi or bento sets, but maybe skip the ramen. Overall, a decent spot with some great options on the menu. || author=Ed King; rating=1; time=2025-09-16T13:18:14.098504131Z; relative=7 months ago; text=I ordered the Chicken Wings (Plain, 6 pieces per order) as stated on the menu, but I was only served 5 pieces.

I also ordered the Beef Pares with plain rice and soup. However, what I received was plain rice and two bowls of soup—one of which consisted only of thickened cornstarch and a single 1 cm cube of beef. 😑

author=Tito Alex; rating=5; time=2026-03-07T07:48:50.120384511Z; relative=a month ago; text=From the moment you walk in, the staff set the tone for an incredible dining experience.

They’re warm, friendly, and attentive—always making sure you feel welcome and taken care of.

The value here is unbeatable: an amazing price for a huge variety of food, with an enormous selection of sides and options that make every visit feel like a feast.

Whether you’re craving classic samgyup favorites or exploring new flavors, the choices are plentiful and satisfying.

Service is consistently fast and efficient, yet never rushed.

The team balances attentiveness with genuine hospitality, creating a dining atmosphere that’s both relaxed and enjoyable.

If you’re looking for a place in Cebu that combines great food, unbeatable variety, and truly wonderful service, The Samgyup 31 Premium Grill deserves to be at the top of your list. || author=Tp; rating=5; time=2026-01-26T13:54:43.998874612Z; relative=3 months ago; text=This Korean barbecue restaurant has a variety of food choices. This samgyupsal restaurant delivers a really satisfying Korean BBQ experience. The meat is fresh, well-marinated, and cooks beautifully on the grill—juicy, flavorful, and not overly greasy. I loved the variety of side dishes (banchan); each one complements the meat well, especially the japchae, kimchi, and sauces that add different layers of flavor.

The lettuce is crisp and fresh, perfect for wraps, and the dipping sauces give you lots of options depending on your mood—sweet, spicy, or savory. The ambiance is cozy and modern, with proper ventilation so you can enjoy grilling without leaving smelling like smoke. Drinks are refreshing, and everything is served in stylish metal plates that add to the overall Korean BBQ vibe.

Service is attentive and friendly, making the whole experience relaxed and enjoyable. Overall, it’s a great spot for bonding with friends or family over good food. Definitely recommended for samgyupsal lovers! 🥓🔥 || author=Kate Nichole Amplayo; rating=5; time=2026-01-27T05:46:12.773447882Z; relative=3 months ago; text=I’ve been to this samgyupsal restaurant many times, and the customer service is always top-notch. The staff are very friendly, accommodating, and attentive—they consistently make sure that we’re satisfied throughout the whole dining experience. The food is really great, and refills are served quickly, which I appreciate a lot. The ambiance is also nice and comfortable, making it a great place to dine with friends or family. Overall, the combination of excellent customer service, good food, and pleasant atmosphere makes this one of the best samgyupsal spots in Cebu. Highly recommended for everyone! || author=Dean; rating=5; time=2026-03-03T19:18:14.065050064Z; relative=a month ago; text=Honestly, maybe the best or one of the best korean unli samgyup in Cebu City. Servers go around in carts so you don’t have to keep standing or looking for servers to get your orders. The place is compact but also offers a a sense of privacy too. Do not miss this place out!

name: Eddie's Log Cabin

sample_reviews:
author=Paulo Niño; rating=5; time=2026-02-13T14:17:56.773566888Z; relative=2 months ago; text=We had a satisfying dinner at Eddie's Log Cabin, enjoying a mix of savory dishes and classic pies. The Original Corned Beef (P622.25) was flavorful, tender, and generously served—definitely a standout. The Sotanghon Guisado (P426) was well-seasoned and comforting, perfect for sharing. For dessert, we tried four pies: Apple (P182), Pineapple (P130), Coco (P162.75), and Choco (P130). The Coco and Choco pies were our favorites—rich, sweet, and satisfying with every bite. Overall, it was a delightful dining experience with hearty portions and comforting flavors that made the visit truly worthwhile. || author=Andrew Holbert; rating=5; time=2026-02-17T11:13:15.986723807Z; relative=2 months ago; text=This place is full of charm and character and apparently known for their corned beef which is what we both had.  I had a Reuben and she had the “sizzling” corned beef which is several generous slices of corned beef served with a brown gravy over the top and a dollop of corn.  This is served on a hot, hot, skillet similar which puts a flavorful crust on the bottom of the skillet.

Eddies is also known for their pies and we tried the coconut cream which we both loved.  The pie had a real thick meringue and flaky crust while the filling is just the right amount of sweet.  Oftentimes I cannot eat more than a few bites of coconut cream pie because they’re commonly too sweet for my preference but Eddie’s was not the case for me, I loved their pie and will be back for more !

Quick update, I’ve been back twice and the chicken fried steak is something definitely worth trying.  Mine was excellent, two big pieces of battered and fried beef cutlet served with white gravy.  For me it’s a toss up between their regular corned beef and cabbage or the chicken fried steak. || author=Rwaters; rating=5; time=2026-01-28T09:56:48.157422293Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Their pineapple pie is sooo good!!

Their calamansi juice is too sour, thoughh || author=Paul Stegeman; rating=5; time=2026-03-09T01:51:57.048037137Z; relative=a month ago; text=Perfect place for breakfast and dinner! Very nice staff. Very friendly. Really good food. Free coffee refills with breakfast. In short: THIS IS THE PLACE YOU WANT TO GO! || author=Saul Jacob Labares; rating=5; time=2025-09-13T11:29:59.647249625Z; relative=7 months ago; text=They have a good servings. Food was fairly delicious 🤤

name: Kaayoo The Filipino Kitchen
address: Pres. Quirino St, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=won Lee; rating=5; time=2026-02-12T06:00:31.780168438Z; relative=2 months ago; text=We had a wonderful lunch at the newly opened KAAYOO in Streetscape. As a Korean couple living here in Cebu, we found this place to be even more charming than we had expected.

First of all, the upscale and sophisticated atmosphere of the restaurant was very impressive. When the food arrived, we were delighted by the clean and stylish plating, which showed great attention to design. Along with the inviting aroma and the freshness of the ingredients, the portions were generous, making for a truly satisfying and hearty meal.

The Aglio Olio Pasta and Native Grilled Chicken we ordered today were excellent, bringing out the natural flavors of the ingredients. What stood out the most, however, was the professional and warm service from the staff. Their attentive care made us feel genuinely welcomed and well-served throughout the meal.

With its perfect balance of taste, ambiance, and service, I highly recommend KAAYOO to anyone looking for a high-quality dining experience in Cebu. || author=Sacha Oh; rating=5; time=2026-02-22T06:16:51.723204791Z; relative=2 months ago; text=The food here is great, not overly seasoned but hits the spot. If you love Bicol Express, you should try their version.  Rich, creamy and spicy sauce - perfectly paired with rice.

They also serve Palabok. What made it special were the thick cuts or fried pork topped over the noodles.

Their soup is served by pouring the broth in front of you. Adds to the experience.

The only thing I noticed about their food is that all saucy dishes, soups and noodles seem to be creamy/ sticky as if cornstarch or some thickening agent was used a lot. Taste is still great though but the thick creamy consistency is there.

Staff were friendly and approachable. You can also reserve ahead and have the reservation fee deducted to your bill. There's a 5% service charge. || author=Enzo Ren Mier; rating=5; time=2026-01-30T01:56:41.836377116Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Went to Kaayoo Streetscape for breakfast-the food was genuinely good, and the service made the experience even better: attentive, friendly, and welcoming. The place has a calm, relaxing ambiance that makes you want to stay a little longer and enjoy the moment. Everything felt well thought out, from the flavors to the overall vibe. A must-try are their puto and sikwati-simple, comforting, and absolutely satisfying. Definitely a spot worth coming back to. || author=Concha, Joe; rating=5; time=2026-02-27T06:59:32.797018006Z; relative=a month ago; text=The food was great and truly met our expectations. The ambiance felt luxurious, and the service was warm and accommodating with no judgment at all.

We paid ₱2,400 for 4 pax, which already included 2 main dishes (sinigang and pata), 6 servings of rice, dessert, and coffee. Overall, it was a satisfying dining experience with good value for money and excellent service. || author=Paulo Niño; rating=5; time=2026-02-23T07:21:56.641701800Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Dining at Kaayoo The Filipino Kitchen was a delightful Filipino feast. We ordered the Crispy Daing na Bangus, which instantly became my favorite—perfectly fried, crunchy on the outside and tender and flavorful inside.

The Inasal na Manok, served with two generous pieces of chicken, was juicy and smoky, paired with a spicy sauce that perfectly complemented its savory taste. We also had the Fresh Kinilaw, which was bright, tangy, and refreshingly balanced. Every dish felt thoughtfully prepared and satisfying, showcasing classic Filipino flavors done right.

name: Wildfire Asian Fusion Restaurant
types: restaurant, asian_fusion_restaurant, asian_restaurant, fusion_restaurant, food, point_of_interest, establishment
address: R. Duterte St, Banawa, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Aaron Nuñez; rating=5; time=2026-01-31T06:49:33.957374565Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Wildfire is an Asian infusion–inspired restaurant that leans heavily into Japanese fusion rolls, and overall, it delivers a satisfying and enjoyable experience.

I tried their Signature Roll, made with tempura shrimp and cream cheese, topped with salmon. This was easily the highlight for me. The roll was well-balanced, indulgent without being too heavy, and the flavors worked nicely together. It’s the kind of roll you’d happily order again.

The okonomiyaki with beef was also delicious, comforting and savory, though I did find the sauce a bit overpowering, slightly masking the other flavors. Still enjoyable, just leaning more on the bold side. The shrimp tempura, while flavorful, lacked the light, crisp batter texture I was hoping for. It wasn’t bad, just not quite there in terms of crunch.

That said, the food overall is something you’ll enjoy, especially if you like fusion-style Japanese dishes that don’t shy away from flavor. The price point is reasonable, and portions are fair for what you’re paying, which I appreciate.

Wildfire is a solid spot for casual dining, especially if you’re in the mood for creative sushi rolls and Asian-inspired comfort food. Not perfect, but definitely worth trying. || author=Mark David Calzada; rating=4; time=2026-02-14T11:37:19.728569048Z; relative=2 months ago; text=The food was really good and features a bunch of dishes from Asia. The service was very nice though and the staff were very attentive. I really enjoyed the chicken tonkatsu and okinomiyaki. Unfortunately though when i visited they their fusion rolls and nigiris were unavailable, so hopefully when i return it would be available. || author=Francis Ryan; rating=5; time=2025-08-01T14:40:28.155809833Z; relative=8 months ago; text=Wildfire Asian Fusion has always been one of our top picks—and for good reason. The food is consistently delicious, well-balanced, and suits our palate perfectly. From the appetizers to the mains, it’s a menu that never disappoints.

The ambiance is cozy yet vibrant, and the vibe is just right whether we’re out on a casual date or catching up with friends. We usually sit at the bar, not just for the view—but because we love seeing how our meals are prepared. There’s something comforting and honest about that transparency.

We’re quite picky with restaurants, but Wildfire has become a true comfort spot for us. We’ve recommended it to countless friends and loved ones, and every single time, it delivers. Always a pleasant experience—always worth it. || author=Kentoy Galano; rating=5; time=2025-09-27T04:57:07.910111689Z; relative=7 months ago; text=All foods were so good! This restaurant is truly hidden gem! We will definitely go back again! || author=Cara Langub; rating=5; time=2026-01-24T07:58:48.327927276Z; relative=3 months ago; text=My absolute favourite sushi place. I first tried it in May 2025 and have been hooked ever since. I counted how many times I’ve been (to date), and it’s 16 times!

I go at least once a month; it’s honestly unbeatable. The staff are super friendly and accommodating (no service charge - that’s the real kicker), and the ingredients are always 100% fresh.

Please open up more branches!

name: Eat&
address: One Montage, Archbishop Reyes Ave, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Anna; rating=4; time=2026-01-30T14:19:51.828889819Z; relative=2 months ago; text=I used to visit this place back when I was living in Cebu (was even present during its early days).

I really liked the ambiance and I used to order their gochujang carbonara, a genius fusion of East meets West. For now, I ordered their alfredo rigatoni pasta. It was good but I was surprised with the healthy amount of serving. I also ordered their strawberry milk. It was okay.

Staff was accommodating and warm. I always enjoy dining in this establishment. || author=Jhon Melky Chumacera; rating=5; time=2026-01-20T07:07:12.385632230Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Eat& doesn’t play around. The place looks clean, modern, and relaxed — the kind of spot you actually want to stay in, not just eat and leave. Good lighting, comfy seats, and a solid vibe whether you’re brunching with friends or just killing time solo.
Their brunch menu is legit. No filler, no boring options — just well-thought-out plates that actually deliver. Portions are right, flavors are on point, and nothing feels rushed or half-baked.
But let’s be real: the shrimp toast is the star. Crispy, savory, perfectly seasoned — absolute for the win. One bite and you’ll get why people come back for it. It’s not hype. It’s just that good.
Bottom line: great ambiance, strong brunch lineup, and a shrimp toast that carries the whole menu on its back. Eat& is worth your time and your money. || author=Carlo Andrew Olano; rating=5; time=2026-02-04T03:33:28.607952593Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Had dinner at Eat& Restaurant (One Montage, Archbishop Reyes Ave., Cebu City) last night and it turned out to be one of the more memorable Korean fusion meals I’ve had in Cebu.

The place has a modern, stylish vibe but still feels comfortable — good for date nights, business dinners, or barkada catch-ups. Service was attentive and the pacing of the dishes was just right.

Here are the dishes that stood out for me:

• Crispy Cauliflower – Surprisingly addictive. It has that sweet-spicy, savory flavor profile similar to Korean fried chicken, but it’s actually cauliflower. Crispy outside, flavorful inside. Great starter.

• Shrimp Tostada – Shrimp with lettuce and herb mayo on a crispy wonton base. Fresh, slightly spicy, and well-balanced. Perfect to share.

• Beef Galbi Stew – Tender and deeply flavorful. Pairing it with their hot stone rice made it extra satisfying. This is comfort food done well.

• Classic Tteokbokki – You can’t go wrong with the classic. Chewy rice cakes in a rich, sweet-spicy sauce that complemented the other dishes nicely.

• Grilled Octopus – Cooked properly (not rubbery), with interesting sauces that added depth without overpowering the seafood.

Prices are on the mid-range side, but considering the quality, presentation, and overall dining experience, it feels worth it. Portions are reasonable and flavors are well-executed.

If you’re looking for modern Korean fusion in Cebu with good ambiance and solid dishes, Eat& is definitely worth trying. || author=Jennifer Salvacion; rating=5; time=2026-01-07T02:36:26.279756902Z; relative=3 months ago; text=My husband and I went here fo breakfast. We ordered Grilled Chicken Toast and Beef Quesadilla. Food is great, price is just right (this is me speaking as someone who saves an item in my cart for a long time before I check it out willing to pay the price of this grilled chicken toast 😂). Above all, my picky eater husband did not pick anything out of his meal- not the greens, not the pickled onions. Will definitely go back and try other items on their menu. || author=Philip Raymond Lim; rating=4; time=2025-04-21T11:07:51.150993Z; relative=a year ago; text=Eat& is the perfect spot when you’re craving Korean fusion done right. While the menu isn’t extensive, every dish feels well thought out and carefully prepared. The flavors hit the mark—familiar and comforting, yet elevated just enough to feel special. It’s not wildly inventive, but what it does, it does really well. The ambiance adds to the experience, set in the iconic Montage building in Cebu Business Park. It’s cozy, stylish, and a great place to enjoy quality comfort food. Definitely worth a visit for the vibe when you’re in the area.

sample_reviews:
author=Dannah Cartagena; rating=5; time=2026-02-10T06:58:36.138215670Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Perfect place for brunch with the girls! The menu looked 🔥 with lots of healthy options, so I was hyped.

🚧 Last time I tried, they were under renovation. Finally went today… but ngl, the place felt super cramped 😬. The kitchen is tiny, which might explain why service was kinda slow ⏳ even though we only ordered basic breakfast/brunch meals 🍳🥞. Honestly, I expected faster prep.

🎨 The design is cute tho — lots of green + pink shades, giving off that condo-designer vibe 🏡💖. But it legit felt like stepping into someone’s studio unit, not enough space for staff + diners 🫠.

🥖 Tried the French toast (lol they forgot the powdered sugar 😅). Still, the food quality was decent. Ordering is BTO style like fast food 🍔➡️ you line up, pay, then they deliver to your (tiny) table. Space is tight, so don’t expect to order a feast.

👀 Saw one diner solo-ing at a huge table… classy flex 😂. Anyway, they’re still on soft opening, so maybe expansion is coming soon 🤞.

💸 Price is kinda high for what it is, so my expectations weren’t fully met. Payment is only cash or GCash 💵📱 — no cards, so heads up.

Overall: nice vibe, okay food, but cramped space + slow service. Hoping they improve once they’re fully open 🚀. || author=Anna; rating=4; time=2025-12-17T14:18:13.702808845Z; relative=4 months ago; text=I was on my way to Merci, but I found this shop instead. But honestly the experience was a letdown. I ordered the pesto and it just didn't taste good at all. It felt really overpriced especially since the portion was so small for what they charge. The service could’ve been better too. Staff didn't feel accommodating or welcoming at all. It’s a bit disappointing to pay that much for mediocre food and lackluster service despite the nice decor.

The pasta is just heavenly. Unfortunately I forgot the name but if you’re a seafood lover looking for the perfect red sauce pasta, show them this picture :) because actually it’s not even on the menu! I just asked for something like it❤️ (₱895 + service charge).

author=Lay San; rating=5; time=2026-03-08T04:27:19.655683344Z; relative=a month ago; text=Oh, I love the Smoked Salmon Tartine. It was so delicious.

I enjoyed the alfresco dining. || author=ask-teleVision (ASK-TV); rating=3; time=2026-02-08T01:54:39.110694100Z; relative=2 months ago; text=The location is good, easily reachable, and the coffee shop is nice. Personally, I wouldn't use tablecloths like that — they only make everything more expensive. The staff is lively and takes good care of you. The food I tried was generally good; I love the croissants and the chocolate croissants. As a former chef (2 stars) I know about food and can judge the quality. Providing that level of quality here in the Philippines is a masterpiece. Even the cheesecake looks like the one I make, and I'm sure it's tasty, though I didn't try it.

The negative, however, is a major issue for me: they don't provide Wi‑Fi, which is a no‑go these days (even though I know some people abuse it by sitting there with a glass of water and using the internet for hours). That could be managed by allowing one hour of connection and requiring a new password with each order.

I write books and produce films. I LOVE sitting in coffee shops and restaurants to write, like many writers do. I like ordering different things throughout the day and trying varieties. But it's impossible here. I need at least some power, yet they've blocked all the power outlets, which is so ridiculous I can't express how stupid it is. I have no idea why they do this. Charging 100 mobile phones from zero to full would cost less than 50 pesos. Charging 100 laptops from zero to full is about 55 pesos — roughly €0.70 or $0.83. With my usage alone they could make a good profit for days. I simply don't understand such a policy. Unless they want to get rid of guests as quickly as possible.

Actually, I would give them 5 stars even if it's a bit dark inside and needs more lighting. But I must deduct one star for no Wi‑Fi and another for blocking the power outlets. As a guest, I find that really bad and it gives me a bad feeling — it should really cost them two fewer stars just for blocking the power. I will come back for another cappuccino and croissant or maybe a Benedict, but it still leaves a bad impression of a stingy owner. || author=davepick989; rating=5; time=2026-01-08T01:39:40.870394028Z; relative=3 months ago; text=What an amazing place! sample_reviews:
author=Rod Miler Gutierez; rating=5; time=2026-02-24T01:47:12.472628443Z; relative=2 months ago; text=I had such a fun and authentic food experience at Pungko-Pungko sa Fuente in Cebu City! The food was freshly cooked, crispy, and absolutely satisfying—perfect if you’re craving classic Cebuano street food. I loved the laid-back, local vibe that really lets you experience the culture up close. Everything was flavorful and affordable, making it a great spot to enjoy a hearty meal without breaking the bank. Definitely a must-visit when you’re in Cebu! || author=Chee Loh; rating=5; time=2025-12-08T06:15:29.252091477Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Unique food, unique atmosphere and unique experience of Filipino food. Each table is operated by different operator. It is very fun and the operator that I approached was very friendly and patience with me who don't even know what I am doing. Take a photo of your orders so that when you pay for them, you know what you were eating. I took time to look and listen to the surrounding happenings. Very interesting experience. You should come to here if you are in Cebu as it is Cebuano way.
Almost forgotten about mentioning how great value the foods are. || author=Angelo Miguel Cañete; rating=5; time=2025-11-21T02:43:09.243796391Z; relative=5 months ago; text=A truly Cebuano food experience

Its location is strategic. Near the Fuente Osmeña Circle in the heart of Cebu City, once you enter, there are many different tables with pungko2x waiting for you to eat as each server will entice you to eat in their table.

Foods available are porkchop, ginabot, bulaklak, alimango, ngoihong, lumpia, longanissa and more. Served and eaten best with their trademark puso (rice) and vinegar(suka).

Always a great experience eating here especially for meat lovers like myself.

Always remember what you got since thats how you'll pay, based on what you get as you'll dictate it to them after eating.

Will definitely be back. || author=Armando Salayo Jr (Manman); rating=4; time=2025-12-06T07:01:33.014512568Z; relative=4 months ago; text=All for cultural experience. If you want to eat palpitating and extra heart warming foods, then this is the place.

Cool to experience one time but I will personally not go there again. There are some delicious fried selections but overall they taste the same. I really liked the eggs and bulaklak - forgot the Cebuano terms.

Servers are all alive and energetic. Once you have the plastic with you, there is no turning back. You’ll have to it. And that is the fun of this all. || author=Jan Altendorff; rating=2; time=2026-01-25T07:30:26.613842357Z; relative=3 months ago; text=I've just left Pungko-Pungko, the staff are very helpful and no doubt speak several languages. Of course, you'd expect them to be, it's self-serve and a simple dining proposition, no frills. You pick your food choose how many puso you want and are asked to remember for the teller at the end. I just took a photo of my choices before they chopped it up. Sadly, beyond the novelty the food is unexciting, a testament to the generally very poor quality of base ingredients in the Philippines, poorly seasoned and deep fried. The pork belly, battered shrimp and fish ball really didn't taste of much. Without the spicy vinegar, there would have been little to tickle the taste buds. Go for novelty not the food. Thankfully it's cheap to eat.

name: Pukot Kitchen - Banilad Branch

author=Francis Ryan; rating=5; time=2025-12-27T05:17:37.756713605Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Pukot Kitchen is such a great place to dine and honestly exceeded our expectations. We loved everything we ordered and I mean everything. The steak was insanely good and cooked just right. The Caesar salad was really delicious and not boring at all. The Hawaiian pizza was so good, well balanced, and perfectly baked.

Drinks were a win too. The Amaretto Sour and sangria were both solid and very enjoyable. What really sealed the deal was the excellent customer service. The staff were attentive, friendly, and made us feel taken care of the whole time.

The place itself is quiet and comfortable, which I loved because you can actually talk and enjoy the moment without shouting over noise. This is genuinely a place we would recommend and definitely come back to. Such a good dining experience overall. || author=Charyl Azarcon; rating=5; time=2025-08-26T11:21:47.030090402Z; relative=8 months ago; text=Ordered the salisbury steak. Loved how each spice and ingredient blended seamlessly like they're meant for each other. Especially loved the spicy kick in every bite. Also enjoyed the tiramisu. Yummy is an understatement. What stood out to me though were the framed photos that take you to different places in the world. They were very fascinating to watch and they were great conversation starters especially while waiting for your serving. You gotta see for yourself. The interior aesthetics is very simple but classy. You could always find an Instagrammable angle. || author=Food Explorer; rating=3; time=2025-10-18T12:32:03.749177828Z; relative=6 months ago; text=I am in the hunt for the best steak in town and so far their Wagyu A5 disappointed my palate.

I can now say that it’s not about how great the beef quality is. But it’s how it’s cooked. The secret spices, the timing, the preparation… so important.

But I gave them a plus point for trying to be a fine dining restaurant.

I asked for medium rare but it’s definitely not. || author=Robert Fajfr; rating=5; time=2025-10-31T06:31:23.343310554Z; relative=5 months ago; text=A world-class dining experience in a classy setting. Pukot Kitchen is a masterful celebration of Cebu's local ingredients, transforming familiar Filipino dishes into culinary art.

With impeccable service to match, this is a true five-star destination. || author=MikeTestMike; rating=4; time=2025-08-26T03:12:55.994490703Z; relative=8 months ago; text=Pukot Kitchen is a cozy and yet stylish spot, perfect for intimate gatherings with family and friends. The moment you step inside, you're welcomed by walls adorned with artworks that set a creative and inviting atmosphere.

Although everything on the menu looked tempting, we decided to go with their best sellers. We started with their Mozzarella Sticks, a treat any cheese lover would appreciate. For mains, the Herbed Porkchops were tender, juicy and perfectly seasoned while the Pan Seared Salmon was tangy and flavorful. The Marinara Bianco stood out. It was a refreshing twist on the traditional Aglio Olio. We couldn't miss their pizzas. Both the Quatro Formaggi and Creamy Spinach came in thin, crispy crusts topped  with cheesy goodness. Each dish came out with good presentation, it was visually appealing as it was delicious.

All in all, the restaurant doesn't disappoint. We left with full stomach. Looking forward to try their other dishes.

name: Napolitani Pizzeria
address: N Escario St, Molave St, Camputhaw, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Angelyn; rating=5; time=2026-02-21T18:32:10.943280640Z; relative=2 months ago; text=​⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ An absolute must-do for pizza lovers!

​We recently joined the pizza-making session at Napolitani, and it was the highlight of our week. Chef Jeremy is a wealth of knowledge; he didn't just show us what to do, he explained the science behind the dough - from ingredient reactions to how to get the perfect rise and tips for home-cooking. He kept the energy high with fun interactions that made the learning process so memorable.

​The pizzas we made were incredible, but the experience didn't end there. We stayed for dinner afterward, and every dish was a hit. You can really taste the freshness and authenticity in every bite. (Pro tip: Don’t skip their Iced Tea—it’s incredibly refreshing!)

​From the seamless reservation process to the attentive staff on the day, their customer service is truly top-tier. Whether you're with family or friends, bring them along for this workshop. Highly recommended! || author=Brie; rating=2; time=2026-02-14T20:59:15.118602650Z; relative=2 months ago; text=I had mixed feelings about this restaurant.

First, the service. I was honestly really grossed out by our server. The moment he placed our drinks on the table, I noticed his nails were dirty. Like actual dirt under his nails. At first, I even double checked because I thought maybe it was just dark nail polish. But when our starters were served, that’s when I confirmed it was dirt. He noticed I made a face and immediately tucked his nails away, but he didn’t clean them. That really bothered me. Hygiene is a big thing, especially in food service.

Now for the food. The bruschetta was good, and the ravioli appetizer was probably my favorite. Some pieces of the ravioli were slightly undercooked, but flavor-wise it was good.

For the mains, we had the chicken and the grilled pork. The chicken was bland and didn’t stand out much, though the risotto underneath was actually great. The pork was very tender, which I loved, but it was a little salty. Paired with the risotto, though, it balanced out nicely. The tiramisu for dessert was just okay. Nothing special.

The place itself is decent, but I wouldn’t recommend it for couples since it’s quite loud. Even the servers were loud and casually cursing here and there. We also asked for service water and had to wait around 15 minutes, and we had to follow up before it arrived.

Will I be back? Probably not, mainly because of the hygiene issue with the server. But if someone wants to try their food, I’d still say it’s worth experiencing at least once, especially with friends rather than for a romantic dinner. || author=Richard; rating=5; time=2026-02-28T09:56:48.268772867Z; relative=a month ago; text=I ordered the Italian supreme pizza, and I have to say the flavor was bursting in my mouth. It is by far the best pizza I’ve had in all 14 provinces I’ve been to here in the Philippines.  If you’re In Cebu and you want a good pizza look no further! This place is top notch! || author=Peter Klein; rating=5; time=2026-01-17T11:50:32.196668155Z; relative=3 months ago; text=When you walk into Napolitani Bistro you’re  transported to a typical European restaurant that has a Cebuano flair. The excellence of the deco is matched by the excellent food. The owner (Jeremy) came to our table to explain how he makes perfect pizza - we had the Italian Sipreme with the homemade sausage plus pepperoni. Don’t miss this delicious and delightful place near Cebu. Business Park. Nothing in the mall comes close. || author=Zera Fei; rating=3; time=2026-01-06T09:37:33.778514837Z; relative=3 months ago; text=I have ordered from Napolitani on Grab before and they got my order of pizza wrong. I did not want to judge this restaurant for this single mistake so I decided to give them a proper chance in person before leaving a review.

I was initially planning to try one item per category on the menu but I wanted to test one dish, one dessert first, just to see where it will lead.

I ordered their chicken pesto alfredo. Serving time was relatively quick. Maybe just a matter of preference, but I prefer my pasta to stay intact. Their pasta would kinda tear apart even just by forking them up. But it does not taste overcooked.

The bread 'stick' was literally like wood. I don't know if it was intentional but not safe for grandmas and grandpas with no teeth.

The sauce was good but maybe too sweet? It doesn't make you want to try another plate, kinda overstimulating my taste buds. BUT it's good enough.

I ordered their Tiramisu and it took awhile to get served, a couple of follow-ups were needed, until I got fed up and had to sound irate. It doesn't taste extra-ordinary. Maybe just a matter of preference again, but I felt like the coffee powder on top was too thick and almost snorted the powder while eating.

I also ordered their Caffe Latte and it was okay, nothing special.

Judging by taste alone, their food is kinda good. Service quality can be improved, active-listening could be beneficial. For the price, they can do better. I hope they do. Because their pizzas DO taste good.

We ordered the Pork Sisig, Pancit Bihon, and Sinigang na Baboy. Unfortunately, the overall food experience felt pretty average. The Pork Sisig wasn’t sizzling when served, and while it wasn’t bad, it didn’t stand out — I’ve had better sisig in Cebu. The Pancit Bihon was on the dry side, and the Sinigang na Baboy was just okay. The dishes gave off “sosyal karenderya” vibes, decent but not particularly flavorful or memorable.

Overall, Aysee is a decent spot if you’re nearby and looking for a quick meal in a cool, comfortable setting.

name: Laguna Garden Café

sample_reviews:
author=Carlo Andrew Olano; rating=5; time=2025-11-24T09:21:36.906896644Z; relative=5 months ago; text=We had a family event here yesterday and all I can say is their venue is perfect for intimate gatherings.  Great food, amazing service! Corkage is reasonable.

The food selection is unbeatable. They have a solid if not one of best Menu of Filipino favorites made the right way.  Among my favorites here is their Kare-Kare, Fresh Lumpia, Palabok, and High Tea.

Yesterday, I discovered that their Kansi is awesome too.

What are your favorites here? || author=Kleinberg Fletcher Tabel; rating=4; time=2025-10-17T13:29:53.306564450Z; relative=6 months ago; text=Located at the right wing of Ayala Center Cebu’s Terraces, Laguna Garden Café is the elegant sister restaurant of the iconic Café Laguna. Despite the name, there’s no actual garden here — but what it lacks in greenery, it makes up for in comfort, air-conditioning, and culinary excellence.

The interiors are clean, bright, and sophisticated — think of dining in a refined tita’s home, where every dish feels lovingly made and beautifully plated. The menu celebrates Filipino heritage in full: baka, baboy, manok, pancit, and sabaw dishes that are flavorful, generous, and consistently well-cooked.

We started with their fresh lumpia, surprisingly large and good for sharing, followed by calamares that were crisp and perfectly seasoned. The sinigang, served on a burner to keep it hot till the last sip, was the star of the table — comforting and full-bodied. For the main course, I tried their lechon kawali with pinakbet, two Filipino favorites harmonized into one plate. Then came a delightful surprise — their puto bumbong, one of the few places in Cebu that serves it, and easily one of the best.

It’s not cheap — prices lean toward the higher side — but you get what you pay for: quality, presentation, and the cool, air-conditioned comfort of a well-run restaurant. Service can be hit or miss; some staff were warm and engaging, others seemed distracted.

Still, Laguna Garden Café remains one of Cebu’s must-try Filipino dining experiences — where classic comfort food meets understated sophistication, right in the heart of the city. || author=Eren; rating=4; time=2025-11-12T15:19:55.018785480Z; relative=5 months ago; text=The food is delicious but the serving time is too long. author=Koh Victor; rating=5; time=2025-12-07T12:08:25.537633085Z; relative=4 months ago; text=I’ve been wanting to try Pino Talamban for ages, and it completely exceeded my expectations! From the moment I walked in, the warm, cozy ambiance made me feel right at home—wooden accents, soft lighting, and that familiar, welcoming vibe that makes Filipino restaurants so special.

The food was incredible. We ordered their signature Chicken Adobo—tender meat soaked in a perfectly balanced sauce of vinegar, soy, and garlic that had just the right kick. sample_reviews:
author=Ryan Patrick Regalado; rating=5; time=2026-02-14T07:12:55.223122356Z; relative=2 months ago; text=We ordered munggo with gata - this is new to me, i never had gata in munggo and wow this blew me away with chef’s kiss. As for a poor man’s food, the taste is rich considering it’s a non meat dish, no bad after taste on the palette and it has soothing soupy feels nostalgic, taste like childhood like how my father used to cook mungo of course without the gata.

And the crispy roasted pork, this is the perfect pair to munggo. Soft, meaty, melts in your mouth and crispy at the same time.

Service and Atmosphere feels homey staff even recommended us to get a half order cuz we won’t be able to finish the single order and yes she’s correct. Overall experience is amazing and recommended.

Well done Michelin for discovering this! || author=Camila Francia; rating=5; time=2026-02-18T08:26:54.352314506Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Abaseria Deli offers a truly comforting home-cooked experience in Cebu!

The staff's warm hospitality made us feel right at home, with friendly service that's like being cared for by a loving mother.

We thoroughly enjoyed the Cebuano-style Monggo with Gata - a first for me, and it was a delightful surprise! The dish was delicious and comforting. The Humba, a Cebu staple, was also a hit, with flavorful meat that fell apart easily.

Highly recommended for a taste of home-cooked comfort in Cebu! || author=EBF; rating=5; time=2026-02-03T13:00:32.530084448Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Im a regular of Abaseria for more than 10 years. This place is our comfort place for food, ambiance and feeling of Home. I usually bring friends to the place who eventually became regulars as well. Through the years the place also became our Associations refuge for Christmas Parties . I have known the staff and management who warmly welcomes us everytime. Their food is consistent since its the same staff who are preparing the food for years. The photos are compilations of my various visits to the place. || author=Alberto; rating=4; time=2025-12-23T04:13:28.015716308Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Ordered the eggplant, humba, and munggo. I found that it was a bit heavy handed on the oil for both of these dishes and would have preferred if it was less oily. Otherwise we enjoyed the food and found it worth visiting. Parking can be difficult so be prepared. Getting the bill and attention for servers was a bit difficult and could be better. || author=Ronalyn Dela Cerna; rating=5; time=2026-01-15T05:07:27.288274988Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Definitely 10/10 this is a Michelin Bib Gourmand restaurant in Cebu that you should try. sample_reviews:
author=Wyll Jie; rating=4; time=2025-04-07T00:41:32.539442Z; relative=a year ago; text=Cafe Laguna at NUSTAR – Sulit and Very Pinoy!

I recently tried Cafe Laguna at NUSTAR and I was really impressed by the ambiance. It feels super Filipino—very homey, with detailed artistry all over the place. From the wooden accents to the intricate designs, parang nasa ancestral house ka. Ang ganda!

Food-wise, it was okay overall. What stood out for me was their Turon with Langka—crispy, flavorful, and definitely something I’d go back for. Must-try talaga! The Pancit Palabok was good too, though medyo maalat lang ng konti and the noodles were thicker than what I’m used to. But what I loved about it is the puto on the side—nice touch!

Their coffee is strong and rich, perfect if you like your coffee bold. I also tried the Puto Bumbong—hindi siya masyadong matamis, which is great for those who are trying to avoid too much sugar. The Suman, Mangga, at Tsokolate was also good. The mango wasn’t super sweet, but the whole combo still worked for me.

All in all, I had a great experience. The prices are reasonable and the vibe of the place is just relaxing and proudly Pinoy. Would definitely recommend if you’re looking for a cozy spot with Filipino comfort food! || author=Khey D. Eats; rating=5; time=2025-09-07T02:34:40.905604449Z; relative=7 months ago; text=Beautiful place to enjoy Filipino food at its best! Cafe Laguna at NUSTAR has a cozy yet elegant vibe with both indoor and outdoor seating. The food is excellent highly recommend their exclusive Cochinillo along with favorites like Fresh Lumpia Ubod, Baked Scallops, Sinigang sa Sampaloc, Lechon Kawali, and Chicken Relleno. Perfect spot for family gatherings or special celebrations. || author=Joseph Lim; rating=4; time=2024-07-09T04:49:15.836007Z; relative=a year ago; text=Good tasting filipino food always. Their taste is very consistent and show why they have been around for so long.

Price is steep, this is because it is in NUStar. We got the crispy kangkong with garlic mayo, crispy crablets, chicharon bulaklak, seafood platter, crispy pata, and karekare. All really good.

The service was okay at the start of the meal, but i do not understand why they don't go near the customers even if therr are only 3 tables occupied. We celebrated my daughter's birthday here and it took so long for us to call a waiter for any followup orders. Hope this improves down the road || author=Bernisha Chen; rating=4; time=2026-02-22T01:34:07.827367070Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Pork lechon is delicious while the other dishes are just okay || author=Matt Guitguiten; rating=4; time=2024-04-05T04:48:55.722684Z; relative=2 years ago; text=Everything in NUSTAR is expensive. sample_reviews:
author=Ferly Jane Tampus; rating=5; time=2026-02-09T00:01:08.358490233Z; relative=2 months ago; text=We’re so happy we chose this restaurant for my mother’s birthday. The food was incredibly delicious and the staff were very friendly, attentive and accommodating. It was indeed a memorable birthday celebration for my Mama. Thank you. || author=juls O; rating=5; time=2025-06-07T19:05:32.977473743Z; relative=10 months ago; text=We’re so glad we chose Fina to celebrate my mama’s birthday dinner—it turned out to be such a wonderful experience. From the smooth online booking to the thoughtful service, everything was just perfect.

The staff went above and beyond to help us surprise my mama. They sang a sweet birthday jingle and presented her with our secret birthday gift—she was so touched! Since there was no cake, they even gave us a complimentary leche flan with a candle, which made the moment even more special.

Every dish we ordered was authentic and incredibly tasty—true Filipino comfort food that filled us up and made us feel at home. The flavors were spot-on, and it honestly felt like a celebration of our culture and family.

The service was warm and personal, and the staff made us feel genuinely special. Thank you, Fina, for giving us such a memorable night. Everything was perfect. || author=more to see with JC mtswjc; rating=4; time=2025-06-24T05:07:58.191840101Z; relative=10 months ago; text=We’ve had dinner here a couple of times—good food overall (though on the pricier side) and decent service. However, their palabok could use some improvement in flavor; it was bland the last time we had it.

There are times when some of their meals are unavailable, so it’s best to ask what’s available upfront rather than wait for the staff to come back and forth just to let you know it’s unavailable. || author=ilminfocus; rating=4; time=2025-01-14T00:58:50.269880Z; relative=a year ago; text=We visited Fina to celebrate my mom's birthday, and our overall experience was quite enjoyable.

First, we were accommodated promptly upon arrival, which set a good tone for the evening. The ambiance and menu reflect Filipino culture, creating a warm and nostalgic dining atmosphere. The food was excellent, showcasing authentic flavors, though the serving sizes were a bit small for the price point. Considering its located inside Nustar, this is understandable, and we still savored every bite.

One downside was an unpleasant interaction with a staff member we asked to take photos of us. He seemed unwilling or annoyed by the request, which was disappointing for such a celebratory occasion. In contrast, another staff member who assisted us with the bill and packing leftovers was exceptionally professional and accommodating, leaving a much better impression. I wish the level of service was consistent across the board.

Overall, Fina is still a fantastic place to dine with family and friends. To ensure a smooth experience, I recommend arriving early or making a reservation in advance.

01/02/2025 || author=Dean; rating=5; time=2025-07-27T16:56:01.220539461Z; relative=9 months ago; text=Visited there on a Sunday at 9 pm and I guess it’s the best time to visit because it is not so crowded. sample_reviews:
author=Michaela Ramas; rating=5; time=2022-11-03T22:47:36.246749Z; relative=3 years ago; text=The perfect place for merienda. I found all my favourite food that I miss so
much having lived o'seas for decades. A good cozy feeling of being home in the company of beautiful lifelong  Cebuana friends. I highly recommend it. It is a  buffet of most love Filipino delicacies. Service is first class! I love ❤️ the ambience of place. || author=AJQ; rating=5; time=2019-05-06T11:53:37.502498Z; relative=6 years ago; text=I have personally witnessed the transformation of this place. Kabilin used to be a small lounge area for guests who were waiting to check-in to the hotel.

Then the small lounge space was then converted into a beautifully-designed classic. They call it the "First Heritage Home in a Hotel". Kabilin (Heritage Lounge) is actually known for its afternoon snack buffet (locally termed as merienda). It is now offering Cebuano (and Filipino) favorites in a newly revamped Ala Carte menu.

A place perfect for family gatherings and private meetings! Every corner is also great for pictorials! || author=Carlo Andrew Olano; rating=5; time=2018-12-07T05:41:33.224819346Z; relative=7 years ago; text=One of the best places to have local delicacies. This is a local Food hot spot. || author=Jo Stuart; rating=5; time=2025-08-14T07:54:45.528782905Z; relative=8 months ago; text=Update:

This is just for anyone that’s wanting to come here on a weekend as there’s no info online about time or price. sample_reviews:
author=Weni Denoy; rating=1; time=2026-02-25T02:38:31.406407569Z; relative=2 months ago; text=We recently dined here and unfortunately, it was a very disappointing experience.

First, the service was poor from start to finish. The staff didn’t seem welcoming, and the cashier in particular had a bad attitude. There was no sense of customer service at all; no smile, no courtesy, and it felt like we were an inconvenience rather than paying customers.

As for the food, we understand that pares is naturally on the salty side. However, what we were served was on a completely different level; it was extremely salty, to the point that it was no longer enjoyable. It didn’t taste balanced at all, and we couldn’t even finish our meal.

Did not even bother to greet us good evening and went right a way to say that this how the meat is actually should be. name: Cebu's Original Lechon Belly
