sample_reviews:
author=Christopher Gallagher; rating=5; time=2025-12-04T08:25:13.860894368Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Excellent food, very friendly service! First time I tried lechon, so I can't compare it to other options, but it was so crispy on the outside and absolutely succulent on the inside. Even more impressively, I got it near closing, so there's no way that it was just made. Nevertheless, incredibly delicious! || author=Criza Montano; rating=5; time=2026-02-26T05:06:39.025098355Z; relative=2 months ago; text=I had an amazing dining experience at Cebu's Original Lechon Belly. The lechon belly was absolutely delicious — perfectly crispy on the outside and juicy, flavorful, and tender on the inside. Every bite was rich and satisfying, and you can really taste the authentic Cebu-style preparation.

The restaurant has a warm and casual atmosphere that makes it perfect for family meals or group gatherings. The servings were generous and well-presented, and everything was served fresh and hot. It’s definitely a place where you can enjoy hearty Filipino comfort food.

The staff were friendly, attentive, and efficient, making the whole dining experience smooth and enjoyable. Overall, I highly recommend Cebu’s Original Lechon Belly to anyone craving authentic and mouthwatering lechon. I’ll definitely be coming back for more! || author=Kai D Gregorio; rating=5; time=2026-03-09T18:44:02.603449348Z; relative=a month ago; text=This is my Go To lechon place. They also give something to your driver when you buy from them. I usually go to their Maxwell hotel branch which is a little harder to fins but all worth it since it is quiet and have some other food options. || author=Space Cowboy; rating=5; time=2026-02-19T04:08:14.698121700Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Probably the best lechon Ive eaten in Cebu City and I’ve had House of Lechon. Cute canideria-house and run by a very sweet family, I cannot recommend this place more.
Go early for the most options!

Vegetarian food options:
1. Ginataan- coconut milk based vegetables soup
2. Mongo- mung bean stew
3. Pinakbet- sauteed mixed vegetables
4. Vegetarian lumpia
5. Jackfruit ginataan- coconut milk based curry-soup
6. Seaweed salad

They also serve delicious, fresh juices
1. Calamansi
2. Watermelon
3. Coconut

Service is impeccable
So friendly and kind
Very authentic experience
Prices are super affordable
💚🌱💚 || author=Iain Lauder; rating=5; time=2024-07-18T07:11:36.861235Z; relative=a year ago; text=What a fantastic find this was!  The lovely lady who runs this local food spot has been here 8 years and it's a crime she isn't more well known.  We initially came across it on the hunt for well priced local vegetarian food but found they also cater for meat eaters like myself too.  It's by far the best local food we have tried in the Philippines.  We ate there 3 times in the end and will desperately miss the food, but will also miss the owners warm personality.
Opening times are 5am-8pm.

sample_reviews:
author=Isabella Blake; rating=5; time=2026-03-05T06:59:12.575398834Z; relative=a month ago; text=Cebu Crab Seafoods Restaurant at JPARK is a great place to enjoy fresh and flavorful seafood. The crab was freshly caught from the aquarium and the taste was telling - the flesh was sweet and soft. The seafood fried rice was incredible alonside with squid, cheesy shrimp and morning glory. The price was also very fair compared to other places I have checked. Much recommendable! :) We would definitely be coming back the next chance we get! || author=Danessa Marie Ymas; rating=5; time=2025-08-18T08:46:57.600818685Z; relative=8 months ago; text=I heard about Cebu Crab Restaurant from a friend who recommended it to me, so I decided to give it a try — and I definitely have no regrets! Their recommendation was absolutely right!
The food was incredibly delicious, especially their crab dishes — very fresh! You can even see the crabs in their aquarium, which assures you of the quality. Every bite was worth it! The place is also very clean and relaxing. The ambiance is beautiful — very welcoming and comfortable, perfect for dining with family or friends.
One of the highlights of the place is their pool area — it's really nice and so relaxing to look at. Whether you're waiting for your food or just finished eating, the view is refreshing and adds to the overall experience.
The staff were super accommodating and friendly. They made sure we were comfortable the whole time and were very attentive. The owner was also very kind, personally reaching out to give us some recommendations on what to try. That level of hospitality is rare and truly appreciated.
If you're looking for a place with great food, a relaxing atmosphere, and warm, genuine service — Cebu Crab Restaurant is highly recommended! It's quiet here and not crowded perfect for an elegant dinner. we will be back, Kodus to the chef of this restaurant. || author=Анна Милкина; rating=1; time=2026-01-31T09:51:23.666476581Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Extremely disappointing! sample_reviews:
author=Chee Loh; rating=5; time=2025-12-10T09:52:49.078795212Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Love the grill and raw fish on offer. If you like to eat like local, this is the place for it. Simple, local and authentic. Tuna belly, a PH treasure was sold out (2pm) so the grill chef recommended another choice hunk of freshly grilled tuna. You have to help yourself - no waiter service. Then went to the rice station to get 2 places of rice and 2 bowls of Tinola and another portion of boiled Tuna. The soup was stunning - rice with fish and tomato umami and the perfect comfort food with rice. The grilled tuna was enormous and easily enough to feed a family of 4. I topped up with another bowl of tinola for the road. The place was filled with locals and a great experience. Arguably the best we had in Cebu. name: Fresh Catch Seafood Restaurant and Sports Bar
address: besides Wheeltek, Englis V Rama Ave, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Stephen John; rating=3; time=2025-12-12T17:28:58.010786033Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Dined here before a few weeks after they newly opened. It was 499 and it was totally worth it.

Dined here again the second time. The price increased to 698. Understandable, since everything increases in this country due to inflation. But the thing is, the service, operations, and the dine in experience took the opposite.

From buffet style, it’s now ala carte. Serving time per order is 20-25 minutes as per staff. You have to pause eating and wait for the refill. This consumes time and loses your appetite. When you think of unlimited food, a buffet set up comes in your mind. I Read responses from the management here about the equal supply of each seafood item. That is a valid argument, but it’s a common complaint by customers expecting an unlimited buffet experience.
Other solutions perhaps?

Before you had side dishes such as kinilaw, chopsuey, guso. Now it’s gone.

Disappointingly, It’s not truly unlimited since seafood supply is actually limited. You paid for unlimited seafood, it should be unlimited. Period. Better change the promotions if otherwise. The selling points is that people dined at your place for Crabs, Shrimps, and Oysters. Not for other substitutes in case your supply runs out.

Limited staff, only one was serving for all the tables. Thankfully, the staff is friendly and accommodating.

The the roof leaks during rain. Something should be done with the large flies. I also saw a huge rat running just outside the kitchen.

This would probably the last time will dine here unless significant changes in the operations are made.

I hope they will not only reply here but also apply the feedback. I believe what they offer is unique but the lapses in operations will turn off the customers. || author=Edward Tiqui; rating=2; time=2025-10-17T10:48:52.496572489Z; relative=6 months ago; text=We ate in Fresh catch Seafood Oct 17, 2025
Price ₱ 590+ for a “SO Called UNLIMITED”
But its not a buffet style dinning it is a plated per order dinning.
You will see in the photo 2 or 3 pcs crabs and alot of tahong to fill up the plate. So we came and sat around 5pm as the unlimited start at 5pm but we received our first order around 5:45 almost 1hour waiting for the food to be serve. The service was ok but the releasing of the food from the kitchen took so long. The food itself is not really good it was ordinary. And the serving of crab and shrimp are really small sizes. We order for a refill the first time it took another 30mins.  But they have a time limit of 2hours per table. So they really know how to maximize the price they offer to the experience you will received. In short it is NOT WORTH IT! I recommend spend you 590+ to the local market and cook at home i think the serving and the satisfaction will be more worth it than this so called restaurant. They dont really give the Unlimited vibes because it will take so much time for waiting before you get to eat. || author=Stephanie Bacon; rating=2; time=2025-10-21T15:43:45.778698297Z; relative=6 months ago; text=We’ve been here twice already, but we only got to eat once. The opening hours listed on Google are different from their actual operating hours. Google says they open at 11 a.m., but in reality, they only start accepting customers at 4:45 p.m.

We paid first and placed our order, but they only had two options for the unlimited seafood:
1.	Buttered Garlic Mixed Seafood
2.	Spicy Mixed Seafood

We received our order at 5:24 p.m. — imagine, we already waited for them to open, then had to wait again for the food to be served. It seemed like they were still cooking, but what we got didn’t taste freshly made. The seafood was overcooked, which was quite disappointing.

The management, food quality, and order process all need improvement. It’s okay to try once just for the experience, but personally, I wouldn’t come back. || author=hamad aljohani; rating=4; time=2025-07-28T17:23:01.447729564Z; relative=9 months ago; text=🌊 Fresh Catch Seafood Restaurant and Sports Bar – Cebu City

From the moment we sat down, we were greeted with crispy fried bread followed by caramelized sweet potato – the perfect warm-up for a seafood feast!

Then came the real deal:
Option A – Unlimited Crabs Mixed with Shrimps for only ₱498 per head (~$8.50)!
Truly one of the most affordable seafood experiences we’ve ever had.

🧺 The baskets are refillable once you’ve finished your plate – just as stated on the menu (“Refill basket only – no rice refill”).
However, keep in mind: each refill takes about 20 minutes, so plan your appetite accordingly 😉

The flavors were bold and satisfying – garlic butter, sweet corn sauces, and spicy blends that made every bite worth savoring.
The rice is paid separately, but the portions are generous and the staff is friendly and responsive.

We ate until we were full… then ate some more.
For seafood lovers on a budget, this place is an absolute gem.

💬 Big thanks to the one who recommended this spot!
Highly recommended if you’re visiting Cebu and craving an unlimited crab-and-shrimp indulgence without breaking the bank. || author=Nate V; rating=4; time=2025-03-09T03:25:19.324560Z; relative=a year ago; text=I recently visited Fresh Catch Seafood, and overall, it was a great experience! The food was incredibly fresh, and the highlight for me was the Kinilaw na Bangus, which I’m a huge fan of it was absolutely delicious. The oysters were perfectly sized and fresh as well. The only minor downside was the crabs they were a bit smaller than I expected, but I understand that might depend on the season. I would also have liked a bit more shrimp, but everything else was excellent.

The service crew was very polite and proactive, which made the experience even more enjoyable. The only challenge was the parking, as there’s limited space available. If you can, it’s better to bring a motorbike because you can park beside the road, and there’s someone watching over your vehicle, so you don’t have to worry.

Overall, I had a fantastic time and will definitely be back. Hopefully, next time, the crabs will be a bit bigger!

author=Dom Albarico; rating=5; time=2023-07-26T22:06:29.689590Z; relative=2 years ago; text=Excellent dining experience. Overall this is one of the best dining place I had in Cebu. This restaurant was closed during pandemic and now making a comeback in Mandaue. sample_reviews:
author=Eunice Faustino; rating=4; time=2025-06-26T05:36:28.901243978Z; relative=10 months ago; text=The servers at Lobster Grill in Mandaue were very accommodating and attentive, which we truly appreciated.

The restaurant is conveniently located and has a welcoming ambiance with a decent view. The staff were friendly, but the service was a bit slow, especially considering it wasn't very crowded during my visit.

The food itself was a hit and miss. The lobster, which is supposed to be the star of the show, was fresh and flavorful, but the portion size felt a bit small for the price. Some of the side dishes were well-prepared, while others were underwhelming. The calamari was too chewy, and the salad lacked dressing and seasoning.

One positive note is the drink selection, which included a variety of refreshing options. However, the overall dining experience felt average. For the price point, I expected more consistency in the quality of the dishes.

In summary, Lobster Grill Cebu has potential but needs to work on service speed and dish consistency to provide a more satisfying dining experience. || author=anna Hangsitang; rating=3; time=2025-12-22T06:16:14.561357242Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Service took far too long, especially considering there were only two other tables dining. I thought it would be expensive but you really get your moneys worth here. Food is super fresh and the dinning experience is made better since youre encouraged to use the whole table as your plate without having to worry about making a mess. Only deducting one star for service  since they were understaffed at the time but still super nice and accomodating. (Understandable since it was a holiday when I visited)

Definitely coming back to pig out on the shrimp, crabs and lobsters || author=Geezelle Maningo; rating=3; time=2025-09-14T18:41:34.174522259Z; relative=7 months ago; text=Pros:
* so many variety of seafoods to choose from
* crabs, scallops, shrimps, crayfish, lobster
* decent price range
* huge parking lot
* accommodating and friendly staff

Cons:
* I thought it was just me but most of us (6/9) thought the food are salty even the pancit and soup
* first time to have a very sweet baked scallops sayang ang 2 tray serving mga 3/4 ang nahabilin
* the menu says small lobster but those are crayfish but its fine kay medyo pareha lang man sila pud, but gagmay lang gyud ang crayfish compared to lobster

We will be back though for the real lobster and crabs. name: Aqua Dining adventure
address: Cebu Ocean Park, SM Seaside Complex, 7VJH+97X, F. Vestil St, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Daniel Roberts; rating=3; time=2026-01-05T13:33:58.626241512Z; relative=3 months ago; text=The venue is really great. The rest has a lot to work on. We did the 8 course meal. The playing was nice. For the price, the food was very average and had a lot of areas to improve including a very fishy octopus course, inedible shrimp tail inside a spring roll, poo still inside the shrimp (not cleaned), and “tomato caviar” on every single course. The cold appis were just not good at all. The rest were decent, the beef was nice.

Then the other big issues. Probably my biggest issue with the whole meal: when you spend that much money and they still make you buy bottled water. Talking about sustainability at an aquarium, then you should have filtered water to reduce plastic. Also, The room was freezing cold. Many other people were trying to cover their bodies as much as they could.

Overall I’m still glad I went because it was such a cool venue, but I wouldn’t go again. || author=Razvan Pele; rating=1; time=2026-02-13T15:06:47.748835580Z; relative=2 months ago; text=We reserved the 8-course dinner at Ocean Park (February 13) after unsuccessfully trying to call for a Valentine’s Day booking — nobody answered the phone for a long time. In hindsight, we’re honestly glad we didn’t come on Valentine’s Day.

When we arrived, we were brought into a waiting room. Every other guest received a welcome drink that was supposed to be included — we never got one. We then waited about 20 minutes and nobody came to assist us, so we had to approach the staff ourselves before finally being seated.

After sitting down we waited another 10 minutes just to receive the menu and drink list. We later asked if we could move to a premium table beside the aquarium to actually see the fish. Only then were we told it costs 500 PHP per person — something nobody mentioned beforehand.

From there the evening went downhill:

• Drinks: Cocktails tasted very bad and arrived late — some courses came before the drinks.
• Food timing: Courses were poorly coordinated. The main course arrived together with dessert, which made no sense and felt rushed.
• Food quality:
- My steak was ordered medium but came basically raw.
- One side dish was still frozen and cold inside.
- There was a shrimp shell inside my girlfriend’s dish.
- Soups were inconsistent (hers had beans, mine didn’t).

The staff tried to fix things but the overall organization was chaotic and clearly overwhelmed. Toward the end it felt like they just wanted to get guests out quickly — probably why dessert was served together with the main course.

Overall this was a very disappointing and poorly managed dining experience. The food quality and service did not match the price at all. We paid and left early because the evening was simply unpleasant.

Would not recommend. || author=Henry Leung; rating=5; time=2026-01-11T18:40:15.194597631Z; relative=3 months ago; text=The experience was recommended by Agoda and I am so glad we went for it! The vibe was amazing and they even had a live singer.

We had the 4 course menu because both me and my partner have small stomachs and it was definitely enough to fill us up. The broccoli veloute was the favourite of the night, we have no idea how they made it so delicious. Definitely would recommend for a romantic date night 🌹

Also wanted to add that a complimentary drink was given on entry. Not sure what it was but it was fruity and delicious, I should have asked. || author=Jodie Cadampog; rating=1; time=2026-02-14T16:20:17.049794809Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Very Disappointed with Aqua Dining at Ocean Park

I am giving Aqua Dining at Ocean Park a 2 out of 5, and that’s only because the venue itself was beautiful and we appreciated the free photos and ref magnet at the end. Unfortunately, the overall dining experience was extremely disappointing.

For a place positioned as fine dining, the service was poor and very disorganized from start to finish.
•	We ordered water and had to follow up five times before it was served.
•	There was no service water available — we were required to purchase bottled water, which was surprising for a fine dining restaurant.
•	Courses were served out of order. Our 3rd course arrived before the 2nd, and it genuinely felt like the staff forgot.
•	The wine was not properly chilled.
•	The soup was barely warm, almost cold.
•	The table light ran out of battery even before our 3rd course was served.

The pacing was unacceptable. We were seated at around 7:00 PM, and by 9:00 PM we were still waiting for our 4th course.

For reference:
•	3rd course – 8:40 PM
•	9:18 PM – someone asked if we had already eaten our 4th course (we had not)
•	4th course – 9:25 PM
•	5th course – 9:39 PM
•	6th course – 9:57 PM

There was roughly a 30-minute gap between courses, especially on the second floor where we were seated. Meanwhile, tables on the ground floor were already on their 6th course (dessert) while we had not even received our 4th. By 9:30 PM, many ground floor tables were already empty, yet most of us upstairs were still stuck mid-service.

It was clear there was no proper monitoring of which table was on which course. Staff even asked us if we had already eaten a course we never received. Used glasses from our welcome drinks were never cleared — they stayed on our table until the end.

By 9:47 PM, waiters downstairs were already loudly cleaning tables with plates clanging, while many of us were still dining. The music ended at 9:54 PM, which made the experience feel rushed and poorly managed.

As for the main course:
The pork was tender and decent. However, the beef was very dry and difficult to chew — it tasted like it had been sitting out too long before being served.

The venue itself is beautiful, which makes this even more disappointing. The only thing that ended the night on a positive note was the complimentary photo and ref magnet collage when we exited.

For the price and “fine dining” positioning, the service, food temperature, timing, and coordination were far below expectations.

Very disappointed. || author=Serene; rating=5; time=2025-09-19T17:19:58.060923195Z; relative=7 months ago; text=That was a fantastic experience! I can hardly find words to describe how amazing it was. This was my first time dining inside the Ocean Park, and I was lucky to get a window seat. We could clearly see the performances and so many kinds of fish swimming around—so vivid and beautiful!

Every staff member was very nice, helpful, and friendly. We booked by phone on the day, but it’s better to book through the website so you can allow enough time for everything.

We chose the 5-course menu, and the broccoli soup was my favorite dish! The drinks were also really good.

sample_reviews:
author=Mery Ghyrzle Ann M. Bargaso; rating=5; time=2026-02-17T14:55:18.500222635Z; relative=2 months ago; text=On our first visit, we waited for more than an hour for our order to arrive. We waited patiently, assuming the food simply needed time to prepare, although I was already very hungry. When I finally asked the waiter to follow up, we learned that the staff member who took our order had forgotten to place it.

The manager came to our table personally to apologize and immediately processed our order. After a few more minutes, our food arrived along with complimentary mango shakes and fruits.

Honestly, a sincere apology and properly served food would have been enough for us. We even offered to pay for the complimentary drinks and fruits because we were concerned that the staff might be charged for the mistake. However, the manager insisted that it was on the house, explaining that it was their way of compensating for the inconvenience. As my husband mentioned, it felt similar to the Korean style of service, where compensation is given for customer inconvenience.

The food was freshly prepared when it arrived. The Chopsuey was delicious. || author=Seira; rating=5; time=2025-12-09T08:24:34.710448667Z; relative=4 months ago; text=The mangoes I had here were so delicious that I went back three times during my stay. They even celebrated my birthday! Thank you! The watermelon shake is also great, as it's not available in Japan! I'll come again! || author=Mr. & Mrs. Mondia; rating=5; time=2025-10-25T10:01:01.624982819Z; relative=6 months ago; text=It was an absolute pleasure to dine at Boodle Cebu, a place that perfectly captures the charm of Cebuano hospitality and culinary artistry. The ambiance was warm, refined, and welcoming — ideal for both casual gatherings and special celebrations.

The menu offered a wonderful variety of authentic Cebuano dishes, each thoughtfully prepared and beautifully presented. Every bite revealed the freshness of the ingredients and the dedication of the kitchen team. The flavors were bold yet balanced, paying tribute to the heart of Cebu’s rich culinary heritage.

A special commendation goes to the restaurant manager, whose excellent leadership and genuine care for guests were clearly reflected in the seamless service we received. Her professionalism and gracious demeanor created a positive atmosphere that elevated the entire experience.

From the first greeting to the final farewell, everything about Boodle Cebu spoke of quality, commitment, and heart. I highly recommend this restaurant for anyone who values not only good food but also sincere hospitality and outstanding service. Truly a dining experience worth remembering. 🌸🌴 || author=Jan Perez; rating=5; time=2026-01-11T21:52:49.242209702Z; relative=3 months ago; text=The ambiance here is really nice. We ordered two large boodle platters for our group—portion sizes were decent, but nothing particularly memorable. Overall, it feels like you’re paying more for the atmosphere than for the food itself. || author=Janessa Birao; rating=5; time=2025-11-12T12:19:53.517168192Z; relative=5 months ago; text=If you’re looking for a fun and filling dining experience in Cebu, Boodle Cebu is definitely worth a visit! The restaurant gives off a relaxed, tropical vibe — perfect for barkada hangouts or family dinners. The Hawaiian-inspired theme adds a nice twist to the traditional Filipino “boodle fight” style.

We tried their Seafood and Grill Platter, which came with a generous mix of shrimp, squid, grilled pork, chicken, and vegetables served on banana leaves. The portions were huge — easily good for 3–4 people — and everything was seasoned well. The grilled pork was flavorful, the seafood tasted fresh, and the dipping sauces added a nice kick.

Service was friendly and quick. The staff explained how the boodle setup works and made sure we had everything we needed. The place can get quite lively during dinner, but it adds to the whole festive vibe.

author=Nico Miller; rating=3; time=2025-10-17T14:05:33.439579750Z; relative=6 months ago; text=We ate at the restaurant after checking out the other venues within the mall.

The place we sat was within the walkway of the mall outside of the restaurant so unfortunately the atmosphere wasn’t very unique however inside I suggest it’ll be a better atmosphere and ambience.

The pork was quite good flavour yet not a large serving, the tuna cheek was a great dish having lots of grilled flavour and smell! The soup and fish dish, the Sinigang, was light in flavour yet it was good in texture.

I like the service and attitude of the team who worked here, one of the waiters did a funny yet amped up dance when a certain signature dance was ordered and this was a great little touch to the experience.

Theres not a lot of people inside so we’re able to take orders. The food is not that affordable. Also didn’t wait longer time for the food. I think it’s not good that they gave the bill to us even we still didn’t ask for it and we still didnt finished our meals. || author=Dino Batain; rating=4; time=2024-07-11T16:03:45.587105Z; relative=a year ago; text=One of the best and highest quality seafood places out there! Seafood and Ribs Warehouse here in SM City is the place!

We ordered the shrimp butter garlic and pancit canton. It was somewhat pricey, but for what it’s worth, I think it’s good.

The 350 grams of shrimp cost around 550 pesos. They also have unlimited rice here for only 42 pesos per person.

The quality of the food is excellent. Every taste is fulfilling. The shrimp butter garlic tasted good, as did the pancit canton.

The food they serve here is very fresh, which is why the quality is so good. The prices for the seafood are based on weight, but there is no cooking fee, so you can have the food cooked just the way you want it.

The service here is also excellent; they treat their customers well. The ambiance of the place is decent.

Overall, the experience was good. I would love to come back here again soon. || author=J E; rating=5; time=2024-09-12T13:27:32.230883Z; relative=a year ago; text=The food is not salty and delicious. Seafood was fresh, so the price was high, but it was worth it. Especially the shrimp dish was so delicious 😋
All stuff is kind of friendly.
We plan to revisit. || author=Indianong Amaw; rating=4; time=2022-08-30T01:23:30.648407Z; relative=3 years ago; text=Seafoods have been one of the foods I like to dig in, and growing in an island province, it's hard-wired within me to select at least good food when it comes to seafoods.

Outside this store, you already have a view of the fresh under-the-sea bounty displayed at the entrance of the store, which kinda reminds me of the "talipapa" food places I've been way back when I was still in Manila. I strayed away from that when I got back to the province since I always prepare and cook my own.

Today, my kid, wife and I, craved for some seafoods so we got in here. We decided to only go for grilled style of cooking because we were thinking that the soups make take quite a while to cook. Now I got myself a Pompano, my kid got a Tuna Belly and my wife got a squid. We instructed the lady to have the fishes grilled and the squid done as calamares.

The place was cozy, lighting was warm, overall ambiance is welcoming and suggests comfort. The tables and chairs looked basic and simple but I don't like the idea of the table where the spaces between the wood materials were a bit broad and if your glass or saucer sits on that uneven surface, it may have the possibility to tilt or get spilled.

On the other hand, our orders came.in after 15-20 mins, standard time for serving orders. I like the presentation of the grilled tuna belly and the calamares, but the grilled Pompano could have been better. It could have been different if the Pompano was wrapped in foil so that the scales wouldn't be that burnt. But the taste of all three dishes were good, saltiness were enough, spices were adequate to compliment taste, and of course, sawsawans were readily available.

The staff are approachable and nice. And oh, if you order that barkada set (P999), that's wherr you get their crew do the sexy dance for you and your team.

Overall, I like to visit here again and try their grilled prok and ribs.

author=Kate Pal; rating=5; time=2025-03-09T08:16:53.225339Z; relative=a year ago; text=Authentic Chinese Cuisine with a Luxurious Bayfront Ambiance

Our dining experience at this Chinese restaurant was nothing short of exceptional. From the moment we walked in, we were impressed by the spacious and beautifully designed interior, which added to the luxurious ambiance, especially with its stunning bayfront location.

The authenticity of the flavors truly stood out—each dish was expertly crafted, rich in traditional Chinese taste, and cooked to perfection. First of all, the staff are very accommodating and always smiling, which shows great customer service. Secondly, the food is delicious and worth the price. Lastly, the ambiance is very relaxing, making the overall dining experience enjoyable. Everything tasted great, and I would definitely recommend this place. || author=Nicole Dippie; rating=5; time=2025-11-28T10:45:19.272698027Z; relative=5 months ago; text=The place is really nice and spacious. You can really see the city view. We also appreciate how accommodating they are. Food is really goood and worth ordering again. || author=Jeriden Francisco; rating=3; time=2026-02-02T14:15:51.107718998Z; relative=2 months ago; text=We enjoyed the atmosphere, scenery, and the food we ordered during our visit. This was actually our second time dining at the restaurant. On our first visit, the service was very good, which is why we decided to return.

However, during our second visit, we experienced some issues with attention to detail. When making the reservation, I specifically requested not to be seated near the balcony because we were dining with senior citizens, and our ETA was 5:00 PM. We arrived at 4:54 PM, and there were only a few customers at the time. Unfortunately, our table for seven was not yet ready, and we were initially advised to wait while being assigned seats near the edge of the balcony. I had to remind the staff again about the senior citizens with us, after which they prepared another dining area—but this resulted in additional waiting time.

Another concern was during billing. No one from the team asked if we had senior citizen IDs to avail of the discount, unlike other restaurants that usually offer this proactively. When I asked if senior citizen IDs were accepted, the hostess refused, explaining that the bill had already been finalized and that my aunt and uncle could no longer avail of their legal privileges. Unfortunately, I did not get her name.

Overall, the restaurant has great potential, but I hope this feedback helps improve service consistency and attention to guest requests moving forward.
(Visited February 1, 2026)

The food was absolutely fantastic! Every dish was flavorful and beautifully presented, from the dim sum to the seafood specialties. We especially loved the Peking duck, stir fried crab, steamed fish and the steamed prawns—they were fresh, tender, and bursting with authentic Cantonese flavors.

It was lami and barato! || author=Rena Mae Gocotano; rating=5; time=2023-07-10T17:22:55.116246Z; relative=2 years ago; text=⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

Post-birthday dinner @ the Collab enchanted by the culinary prowess of celebrity chef Mark Philip Neumann!

The food was beyond tasteful! It made me feel like I was experiencing life at its finest. For someone like me who isn't fond of vegetables, their famous appetizer, Samosas, was a game-changer. When the Porkchop with Pesto Butter was served, it felt like a party is just about to start. The meat is tender and the pesto butter definitely did not disappoint. When we thought it couldn't be as good as it already was, their White Brew bestseller made us rethink our idea of coffee.

And to cap this beautiful moment, Chef Mark offered his personal stash of wine as we were looking for one (mga hilason HAHAHA). It was heavenly good!  Also, he said it was because it was my birthday. Pak, the main character. 💜🤣

I recommend Collab as your go-to for your special celebrations or even up to days when you just want to experience good food and warm accommodation. Chef Marke is one talented gentleman!

Thank you po for making this day extra special. || author=Melvin Rodriguez; rating=5; time=2023-06-22T14:02:13.575100Z; relative=2 years ago; text=I recommend their resto. Food is 10/10 for me. Atmosphere, it has a very nice ambiance with big parking space. Overall, I'll rate it as 10/10. :)

name: Entoy's Bakasihan

sample_reviews:
author=Errol; rating=3; time=2025-04-27T17:12:32.782652Z; relative=a year ago; text=If you’re coming from Labangon side, best route is through CCLEX to get to Entoy’s. This place is kinda far, even from the airport area—you gotta go all the way to Cordova.

If you’re hunting for seafood and exotic dishes, this is the spot! The place itself doesn’t look super inviting, but trust, they’ve got a good variety of seafood. Every time we visit, we usually get crabs, saang, oysters, grilled fish, and ubod. We love everything except the oysters this time—they tasted kinda weird, like they weren’t fresh or had been cooked way too early. We barely touched them, sadly.

Also noticed that compared to a few years back when we first tried Entoy’s, the portions are getting smaller and seafood prices have gone way up.

We got there a little before 9AM thinking we were early, but not long after, more customers started showing up too.

We are still coming back this place when around the area. || author=Stephie G.; rating=5; time=2025-07-16T07:19:50.076290753Z; relative=9 months ago; text=It is in carenderia style, like a local eatery/restaurant. Food is already cooked and served upon order. We arrived hours after lunch so there was no traffic and queue. Food was delicious and price was affordable. The seafood were all fresh. Definitely a must try to all food lovers, will definitely revisit. || author=mynames ssheeesh; rating=4; time=2026-01-24T03:23:02.144060055Z; relative=3 months ago; text=The only Bakasihan place we love to come back to. Fast dining, tasty food, nice view. || author=Tamara; rating=4; time=2024-02-14T10:29:07.661918Z; relative=2 years ago; text=The place is not easily reachable and also on first glance doesn’t look really appealing. However, service is super fast as majority of the offer is already ready, the taste is amazing and you are eating really local local dishes. Only recommandation to the stuff is to be less shy and offer what is really typical and really good. 😊
Would recommend to those looking for some culinary and travel adventure. P.S forgot to mention the price is really very cheap (for 5 dishes we paid 250 pesos). || author=Niño Michael Inting; rating=4; time=2023-07-01T05:41:11.306947Z; relative=2 years ago; text=The food at Entoys is exceptional! The place though smells like dried seaweeds, if your not picky with the smell then  I highly recommend this place

Store: Sparrow Cafe

I hope this message finds you well.

I am writing to formally raise a complaint regarding my recent dining experience at your establishment. Unfortunately, the meal served to me contained pork from a male pig that had a very strong and unpleasant odor. The smell was quite noticeable and made the dish difficult to eat, which was very disappointing.

When I brought this issue to the attention of the staff, I respectfully requested that the dish be replaced with another meal. I also clearly stated that I was willing to pay the price difference if the replacement dish cost more. However, my request was refused, and no alternative solution was offered. Additionally, I did not receive an apology for the inconvenience.

We also tried to explain that in Korean culture and cuisine, pork from a male pig is known to have a stronger and often unpleasant smell, which is why it is usually avoided or handled differently. Unfortunately, our explanation was not acknowledged, and our concern seemed to be disregarded.

As a customer, I believe it is reasonable to expect that food served meets acceptable quality standards and that concerns raised by customers are handled professionally. The way this situation was managed left me feeling disappointed and undervalued as a customer.

The café’s bright, cozy, and plant-filled interior creates a relaxing atmosphere that makes dining here even more enjoyable. 🌿🍽️ the best! || author=Peanats; rating=5; time=2026-02-07T12:46:36.529159359Z; relative=2 months ago; text=We had a really nice time at Huemann. Overall, we left happy and satisfied. The quality of the food, drinks, and service made the experience feel well worth what we paid. We’d definitely love to come back.

name: Onward Coffee
types: coffee_shop, cafe, food_store, store, food, point_of_interest, establishment
address: 8WJ2+Q7V, Wilson St, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Kari Amor; rating=5; time=2025-12-22T04:50:28.791529883Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Top-tier specialty coffee without the pretense. Onward is our absolute fave coffee spot in cebu! My husband and I come here almost everyday. We usually get a flat white or an iced cold black, and it’s consistently great.

They also have a good rotation of pour over coffee beans so there’s always something new to try. Baristas are very welcoming and knowledgeable. || author=Arnie McCoy; rating=5; time=2025-11-28T08:28:30.734498019Z; relative=5 months ago; text=Finally! I found a fine cup of coffee in Cebu City! Most everything in the Philippines is over roasted and/or the extraction is poor. Not this place!!! If you want a lightly roasted, perfectly executed pour over coffee or espresso, make the trip to Onward Coffee! The attention to detail in every aspect of execution is inspiring.  From the filtering of water to the choice of pour over container to the manual espresso machine, Brent and BJ prove they are masters of their craft!  I cannot overstate how much this place impresses me!

If you want a typically over roasted and burned cup of crap, this ain’t your place. This is for people who appreciate specialty coffee that won’t ruin your stomach for day with acid. My description sounds snobbish but the baristas and ambiance are definitely Not! Brent and BJ are a dynamic duo who would fit well into the specialty coffee scene in America. This place is chill and the vibe is friendly and joyful.

The location is a short walk from IT park. Going up Wilson Street, look on the right for Pamahaw Cafe. Onward Coffee is tucked back in there on the right. Trust me: I travel the world and look for fine coffee. This place is LEGIT and worth the walk or ride. I will be back! 😎

***I returned the next day for more and to see if my previous experience was a fluke or if I was just over enthusiastic and caffeine deprived. I am happy to report that it was better than the first visit. I was served a pour over from their “Secret Stash.”  It was a Washed and Cold Fermented Ethiopian. It was easily the best cup of coffee I have ever had!  It takes the title away from the Vegan Shack in Panglao. Who would think that after traveling the world and exploring coffee establishments, the very best and most memorable cup would be here in Cebu City! || author=Berns Vergara; rating=5; time=2025-11-13T07:09:20.467479543Z; relative=5 months ago; text=If you're in Cebu, you really shouldnt miss this coffee place!!! Their coffee is honestly some of the best Ive had and you can tell they put so much love and effort into finding good beans and making every cup right. The baristas are super friendly and accommodating!!! and you can feel how passionate they are about what they do. I also love how quaint and cozy the place feels. Please stay consistent - both in your service and quality 🙏🏼💕 || author=Chikugo; rating=5; time=2025-12-14T14:07:24.813132940Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Onward Coffee has a really chill vibe, and the barista is super nice and welcoming—it makes the place feel even better. The coffee is great, and the pesto chicken sandwich is a must-order. Definitely worth stopping by! || author=Menekşe Arancak; rating=5; time=2025-09-16T02:57:32.088576782Z; relative=7 months ago; text=The coffee was delicious and the pesto chicken sandwich was amazing. The staff were very kind and the place was spotless. Highly recommended!
Great coffee, tasty pesto chicken sandwich, friendly staff, and a very clean place. I’ll definitely come back!

I never expected to find such a quaint and aesthetic cafe tucked away in a somewhat secluded space, but the interior is incredibly chic and elegant without feeling overly commercial. While the ambiance is perfect for a quick catch-up with friends or a stylish coffee break, the seating isn't really designed for long hours, so it's probably not the best spot for remote work. sample_reviews:
author=Kiss Soft Living Ph; rating=1; time=2025-10-19T09:01:27.182370166Z; relative=6 months ago; text=Visited them for the first time today — and sadly, it’s a one-and-done experience for me. 🤬

We went there around 12 noon, and aside from the group sa ground floor (murag meeting room), kami ra jud ang customer that time — pero the 3 staff didn’t even approach us right away. I had to call their attention pa before someone assisted us.

I ordered a hot Spanish latte and some food. I even told the server to make it less sweet kay I’m minimizing my sugar intake, but she didn’t relay it sa other staff. Pag-abot sa drink — grabe, super tam-is gyud! I asked the staff about it and guess what, standard daw nila to put ug 20 ML CONDENSED MILK sa gamay nga cup (est 140 ml size). 1 sip pa lang, sakit na sa throat, kung kinsa man gane nakahuna’2 ana nga ratio, wa ko kasabot sa reasoning. 🤯My partner tried it too, and he said it’s even sweeter than his cookies & cream drink.

When I told the staff, wala man lang apology or offer to fix it — instead, ila ra gibasol ilang kauban. Like, seriously? No effort to make it right.

Overall, disappointed gyud ko — both sa service and sa drink. Definitely not coming back, and I’ll tell my friends to skip this place. || author=Dom Sam; rating=5; time=2025-09-16T06:14:20.936651161Z; relative=7 months ago; text=Bee Cafe is such a great spot! We really enjoyed their pasta and katsu — both were flavorful and well-prepared. Cozy ambiance and friendly service made the experience even better. Definitely worth a visit! || author=Morning After Pill Cebu; rating=5; time=2025-09-06T12:00:56.001313570Z; relative=7 months ago; text=Bee Cafe Cebu is my go to spot whenever I want to hang out with my furbaby and still sneak in a bit of work. It has the perfect mix of cozy and functional. You can sit back and relax, but at the same time it is a good place to open your laptop and get things done. As a shop owner, I like escaping the stiff office vibe, and this place gives me that balance.

Now let’s talk food. My all time favorite here is their shrimp aglio olio together with a Spanish latte. I know, the combo sounds a little strange, but it makes sense in my world. The pasta is flavorful, the latte is smooth, and together they somehow fuel both my belly and my brain.

Bee Cafe is also more than just a chill spot. I had a family event here once and the staff went above and beyond. They were so friendly and accommodating that I already decided my next birthday party will be here. Planning an event with them feels stress free, and the atmosphere makes it even better.

This cafe has quickly become like a second home. And as someone who also runs a wellness shop in Cebu (Morning After Pill Cebu), I really admire places that know how to take care of their people. Bee Cafe does exactly that and it keeps me coming back. || author=Jim Irwin; rating=2; time=2025-10-23T04:36:47.810477134Z; relative=6 months ago; text=The façade and the interior are very nice and inviting. author=Aldz Solantz; rating=5; time=2026-01-06T13:08:20.733966018Z; relative=3 months ago; text=ONE OF THE BEST QUALITY MATCHA in Cebu that’s worth the price. The matcha to milk ratio is right. I frequently order their matcha and I have never experienced being given a bland tasting matcha latte from this cafe. Food was really good!

Interior was aiming to have cozy vibe but it can be cozier. i love how spacious the outdoor is, perfect for my toddler to roam around but it can be more appealing specially people who loves to chill outdoors.

But you pricing is perfect for the service and ambiance they offer. But to a trained professional eyes like mine they can take it up a notch. || author=Nina Muire; rating=5; time=2024-04-07T14:42:16.458872Z; relative=2 years ago; text=Location:

9/10
The location is not right next to the highway.
You will have to make a turn and your destination will be on the right! 😃
It’s not a difficult place to find.
The location is also good because it’s next to other restaurants/bistros in case you’re interested in other dishes aside from the ones they serve in the cafe.

Their interior is simple. There wasn’t too much decoration. The cafe’s name is accurate. It simply is a NEIGHBORHOOD cafe.
I loved how they also made the effort to decorate as much as they did though. They curated their interior by adding the necessary furniture and accessories to establish a statement— the ambience that they wanted to give off on their patrons.

There’s not a lot of seating though. If you’re going to this cafe as a group, I suggest you (a) prepare to be seated separately, (b) wait until there’s an available seating enough for your group, (c) message their page beforehand to inquire if reservation is possible. I had no experience of this though…just offering a suggestion.

Food and Beverages:

10/10
Their food and drinks were so good! I loved their tuna sandwich. Unlike some sandwiches I’ve had in other cafes, Neighborhood Cafe’s sandwiches don’t give off that “sum-ol” feeling after a couple of bites.
It was just right. Not too heavy, but definitely makes you full— a good thing since this was what I wanted at the time.
I absolutely loved their fluffy Japanese Souffle Pancake too! The taste was just right— although I didn’t get to finish all of it.

Staff and Service:

10/10
Their staff and service is commendable. They were patient and welcoming— which are two very important traits in customer service (next to professionalism, of course).

Pet-friendly: Yes

PWD-Friendly:
5/10
I wouldn't say the location is absolutely PWD friendly.
They can definitely seat PWD, but for someone who might be using a wheelchair or crutches, the location and the place might prove to be a bit of a challenge.

Overall, if you’re near the area, I would definitely recommend visiting the place. || author=Lyra FA; rating=3; time=2024-08-17T03:58:45.489494Z; relative=a year ago; text=I visited this café for the first time after hearing good reviews, but I was a bit disappointed with my experience.

The food and drinks were decent—nothing fancy, just your standard café fare. While I appreciate their “neighborhood café” theme, I still expected a certain level of cleanliness. Unfortunately, the place fell short in this area. There were small cockroaches on the walls, and one even made its way to our table. The restroom was also not as clean as I would have liked. The biggest concern was the back kitchen, which was disorganized, messy, and far from meeting sanitary standards. To add, there are two aircons but it's hot inside!

I would also suggest replacing the cloth chairs. They were heavily stained and seemed unhygienic, and they appeared to be a hiding spot for the small cockroaches.

On a positive note, the service was good—the staff were friendly and attentive. However, cleanliness is a crucial aspect that needs improvement here.

author=Edward Hunter; rating=5; time=2026-03-06T10:01:34.207510362Z; relative=a month ago; text=I've eaten at Tamp Cafe multiple times, and it never ceases to amaze me how good their food is. My go-to order is their delicious pork chops paired with a refreshing mango shake. They also have a great ₱1 cake slice promo, which is a fantastic deal if you're already ordering a drink.
The café itself is very comfortable, with cool air-conditioning that feels especially nice after coming in from the heat outside. The staff are friendly and accommodating, which makes the dining experience even better.
Overall, it's a great place to relax and enjoy good food. I’ll definitely keep coming back. || author=Jhon Melky Chumacera; rating=5; time=2026-01-20T07:05:01.961616404Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Tamp Cafe doesn’t try too hard—and that’s exactly why it works.
The ambience is clean, modern, and chill without feeling pretentious. It’s the kind of place you can actually relax in, whether you’re catching up with friends or just zoning out with your laptop and good coffee. No loud gimmicks, no forced “aesthetic”—just solid, comfortable vibes.
Their coffee selection is legit. You can tell they take their beans and brewing seriously. Every cup we had was balanced, smooth, and properly made—no burnt bitterness, no watered-down nonsense. Whether you’re into strong espresso or something more mellow, they’ve got options that actually deliver.
But let’s be real: the Yema Cake is the real MVP. That thing alone is a reason to come back. Perfectly moist, rich without being too sweet, and that yema topping? Straight-up addictive.
Bottom line: Tamp Cafe is the real deal—great space, great coffee, and a dessert that absolutely slaps. If you’re skipping this place, you’re doing it wrong. || author=Shennah Casio; rating=4; time=2025-12-20T13:53:16.059449234Z; relative=4 months ago; text=We visited this place with friends for our year-end get-together, and it turned out to be a great choice for a group celebration. Even though it was a busy night and we had to wait a few minutes for a table, the staff was wonderful—the servers were kind enough to expedite our orders so we weren't waiting long once seated.

​Food & Drink Highlights:
​Chicken Parmigiana: The serving of chicken was absolutely massive—definitely great value for the price. Pasta was good esp with their tomato sauce.

​Blended Fruit Drink: I tried the Mango Passionfruit, which was incredibly refreshing and a perfect palate cleanser.

​Kids Meal: A solid choice for the little ones. It came with chicken pops, pineapple juice, rice, and scrambled eggs. My only small critique is that the eggs were a bit bland (ofc bc I am not a kid!), but the rest of the tray was well-received.

​The "Piso Cake" Promo:
We were lucky enough to avail of their Piso Cake promo, and I opted for the Mocha Mousse Cake. While it was a little bit too sweet for my personal preference, the mocha flavor was spot on and very satisfying for a coffee lover.

​Final Verdict
​A lovely atmosphere for a group gathering with attentive service and generous portions. We left with full stomachs and great memories!

I want to visit again and try out their other tablea-based drinks. During our visit, I learned that they serve a different menu for morning & for their lunch and dinner time. author=Anna; rating=5; time=2026-01-23T19:32:15.880156088Z; relative=3 months ago; text=I used to frequent this café because of their Wi-Fi and sockets. I tried their oat latte with sugar syrup, and their rice meal which I forgot to take a picture of.

I had been curious about this café for a while, so I finally decided to visit. The space is clean, calm, and relaxing—perfect for spending some quiet time alone. There’s also a restroom available, which makes it a convenient spot for those who enjoy staying at cafés for longer periods.

All the staff members were very friendly, polite, and helpful. They left a really positive impression with their warm and professional service.

During my visit, I ordered the Basque cheesecake, regular cheesecake, ube cake, cookies, madeleine, and a café latte. Honestly, I found the sweets a bit underwhelming. The ube cake had a dry sponge, and the madeleine was not sweet at all—actually quite salty. The cheesecake was also too salty for my taste, and might not suit those who prefer sweeter desserts.

However, the Basque cheesecake was the highlight—rich and creamy with a perfect melt-in-the-mouth texture. It wasn't overly sweet, making it a great match with coffee. The café latte was also excellent, with a well-balanced ratio of coffee and milk. The coffee flavor was strong yet mellowed out nicely by the milk—truly delicious.

It seems they also offer food items, which I haven't tried yet. Perhaps the food menu is better than the sweets. I hope the desserts improve in the future, as the atmosphere and drinks are definitely worth coming back for. || author=Gene Hong; rating=5; time=2024-10-09T03:32:38.794167Z; relative=a year ago; text=Dragonfly Cafe has a clean and minimalistic atmosphere with comfortable seating and strong air conditioning.

I recommend the Eggs Benedict which includes fries. I removed 1 star on food only because the temperature was lower than optimal and the English muffin could have been toasted slightly longer. Overall, it was very well prepared.

The iced sea salt cream coffee had a double shot of espresso and was delicious and not too sweet. We were really looking forward to celebrating, but unfortunately, we left deeply disappointed with our experience last night.

We reserved a table a week in advance and even came back prior to our date to reconfirm. However, when we arrived, there was no table available for us. It was disheartening and felt like our reservation wasn’t taken seriously.

We understand that the restaurant may have been understaffed, especially on a busy night. What truly upset us, though, was being consistently ignored. The lack of coordination and overall INCOMPETENCE in handling reservations and guests was very evident.

We arrived at 9:00 PM. We repeatedly asked for the menu, but the waiter simply walked past us several times without acknowledgment. It took nearly an hour before we were finally given one. The service came across as RUDE and dismissive, which made the situation even more frustrating.

From 9:00 PM to 11:20 PM, we waited — and still were not served anything. Waiting over two hours without receiving any service at all is unacceptable.

We stayed because we didn’t want to immediately give up on what was supposed to be a special evening. In the end, however, what was truly wasted was our time.

Valentine’s Day is meant to be special, and sadly, this experience fell far short. author=Reymart La Cara Inot; rating=5; time=2025-12-31T11:30:14.955255105Z; relative=3 months ago; text=The drinks weren’t that impressive, but the overall experience made up for it.

We availed the ₱600 entrance ticket, which includes two free drinks. The first drink is served in the initial room, where a projector illuminates the table and reacts when drinks are placed on it—such a creative touch.

After that, we headed to the second floor to start the main tour. We genuinely enjoyed the digital art experience, as each room features a different theme and immersive visuals.

Our favorite part was the Ocean Park aquarium, where the final drink was served. It was the perfect way to end the tour.

Highly recommended for those who appreciate digital art and love taking photos. We especially loved the aquarium section. || author=Patricia Rodriguez; rating=4; time=2026-02-22T10:31:21.682313141Z; relative=2 months ago; text=₱600 per person, which includes two drinks — the first is tea, and the second can be coffee, juice, or hot chocolate. If you’re coming mainly for the visuals and photo opportunities, you might enjoy it more than the beverages. || author=Giovan Auxilio; rating=5; time=2026-02-23T04:15:47.637023850Z; relative=2 months ago; text=A mesmerizing spot so hypnotic it's like experiencing a hypnic jerk during the day! 😝😅🤣

Entrance Fee: P600/pax

Inclusions:

2 Drinks - Welcome and Exit Drinks

First Floor - you get to marvel at the animated aquarium where a whale shark is endlessly roaming within the walls of the cafe while friends, lovers and even families sharing idle chit-chats, sipping coffee or tea.

Second Floor - you are greeted by gigantic color changing balloons like a stalagmite and stalactite seamlessly overcrowding the area suffocating the life of a claustrophobic crazy animal. Hahaha

There were a few representations of the flora and fauna magically appearing every now and then. There was a waterfall flooding the area and even lightning and thunderstorms causing tremors and ruckus. Charot!

A kaleidoscope of bewitching shapes and forms in all their majestic colors seemingly held me captive while taking a step inside the scene. And there was a room filled with rotating balls and circular fruits as if trying to cause dizziness and spinning sensations.

sample_reviews:
author=dee; rating=5; time=2026-01-26T05:48:40.213810022Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Croissants here are top tier 😭🥐 Personal favorites are the Almond and Banoffee croissants - they’re honestly so good. Coffee is really solid too, but what surprised me most is that they have all-day breakfast meals, and they’re actually really good! The cafe itself feels super homey and cozy, the kind of place you’d want to linger in. Sadly, their Tapa was sold out today 🥹 so I’ll definitely be back for that. Highly recommended! || author=Ciara L.; rating=5; time=2025-10-13T18:55:22.604659253Z; relative=6 months ago; text=I've been seeing posts and ads about 5G (since the pandemic) and decided to try it here one day. I finally found the time to visit and was pleasantly surprised by the place. The coffee shop's exterior is unassuming and sort of lacks presence since it blends in with the neighboring houses, but that's part of its charm. When I entered the place, it felt like I was transported into one of the cafes in a fiction book I've read entitled, "Days at the Morisaki Bookshop".  I love how they maximized natural lighting and created a comfortable, homey atmosphere with their wooden accents and furniture. Their window-side counters/tables are perfect for those who prefer to dine alone and do their work. They also have varied seating options with couches. Their music playlist is nice and chill as well - it personally didn't disturb me even while I was working. Overall, the cafe is conducive for those who prefer a relaxing and peaceful atmosphere to study, focus, or work. Even when the place was fully packed, it wasn't noisy or disturbing. ☺️

🥐 Food and Drinks ☕
I'm personally not a big fan of croissants or pastries, but my friends have been raving about this coffee shop since they opened during the pandemic. We tried their classic croissant and pain au chocolat in their GMall branch with our family friend (pastry chef), and he kept on praising its perfect lamination, layers, and taste. I came alone to their original branch in Apas, Lahug, and got their freshly baked strawberry croissant and iced americano. I'm not a food critic, but I can say the croissant was light, moist, and delicious. It wasn't too sweet, and the fresh strawberries weren't sour. The creme filling inside was overflowing (in a good way) once I sliced the croissant. I tried flavored croissants in other bakeries, and they were usually underfilled or dry. This was perfect for me. Their iced americano was great as well -not burnt or bitter. It had notes of berries and chocolate. ✨
Price: Expensive, but portions are generous. Quality is great, so I think it's worth it ☺️✨

📮👩‍🍳 Service
Staff were courteous, polite, and warm. They served the food and drinks straight to my table, while I thought it was self-service. The only concern I had was when I asked what fresh goods were currently available, the cashier just pointed at the rack of croissants. I asked again what they were, and he said that those croissants were the only available ones without telling me what was available (since there weren't labels or a menu from what i remember). I asked him (3rd time) what the flavors were, and that was the only time he mentioned the available flavors 🥹😭. It's a small thing, but I kind of expected him to mention all the available flavors rightaway once asked. Nonetheless, they have okay service and provided a good experience. ☺️ || author=Eben Roets; rating=5; time=2025-12-28T04:09:36.880641199Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Micro Bakery at its best!! name: Miau Cafe
types: coffee_shop, cafe, food_store, store, food, point_of_interest, establishment
address: F. Cabahug St, Kasambagan, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Elise De la Pisa; rating=5; time=2025-10-22T10:07:55.633259172Z; relative=6 months ago; text=I really enjoyed my visit to Miau Café! The food was delicious, the staff were friendly and attentive, and their matcha drinks were so good. The ambiance is cozy and relaxing — perfect for unwinding.

I especially love the concept of the café where you can spend time playing with the adorable cats. They’re well cared for and make the whole experience even more enjoyable. I was actually too busy playing with the cats that I forgot to take photos of the food 😂 Definitely a must-visit spot for cat lovers and anyone looking for a chill place to eat and relax! 🐾💚 || author=James Jebulan; rating=5; time=2025-09-30T01:59:37.047991424Z; relative=6 months ago; text=The staff here were incredibly welcoming and respectful, and the food and drinks were fantastic! I really appreciated that the cats weren't roaming freely around the entire café; they have their own dedicated room where they can feel safe. Before entering the cat area, we were also given shoe covers to help prevent any bacteria from harming the cats, which I thought was a thoughtful touch. The cats themselves were friendly, and I loved that they have scheduled breaks to avoid becoming fatigued from interacting with visitors. Overall, a big shoutout to the team for genuinely caring about the well-being of the cats and not just treating them as part of the business! || author=Wyll Jie; rating=4; time=2025-09-18T18:30:28.110396124Z; relative=7 months ago; text=If you’re looking for a cafe that’s more than just about coffee, Miau Cafe is the spot to visit. The place is spacious, with a cozy atmosphere, and the staff are genuinely friendly and accommodating, which makes the experience even more welcoming.

We tried their Matcha Lattes, and I can say the flavor tastes authentic and rich — definitely a treat for matcha lovers. But what truly makes this cafe stand out is the opportunity to interact with their cute and adorable cats. It’s such a fun and heartwarming experience that perfectly fits the cafe’s theme and name.

For anyone who loves matcha and enjoys being around cats, Miau Cafe is definitely for you. It’s not just about the drinks — it’s about the cozy, unique experience that leaves you smiling as you leave. 🐾💚 || author=Crystal May Odoño; rating=5; time=2025-09-19T14:13:58.248211592Z; relative=7 months ago; text=the staff were accommodating and respectful, they also have good food and drinks ! i love the fact that the cats weren’t roaming around the whole cafe and they have their specific room para safe sila. they also made us wear shoe covers so that we don’t bring in any bacteria that may harm the cats when we enter the room to interact with them. cats are friendly too and they have a break time so that they don’t get fatigued from being with people all the time. kudos to the team for really taking care of the cats and not just using them for business. 😸💛 || author=Kyler Ybiernas; rating=5; time=2025-03-16T10:13:04.639908Z; relative=a year ago; text=They are on the expensive side when it comes to coffee but their drink was great and their ice cream croffle was delicious. The cats were the best thing on this cafe. I would suggest people to come early as the cats have an afternoon break from 2 to 3 pm and would be still tired from all the playing during the morning.

Before you can play with the cats you need to have at least ₱250 worth of receipt which is good for 15 minutes of playtime for 1 person. Also, you need to pay ₱15 for the face mask and foot socks which are needed before entering the cat area. You can also buy cat food for ₱25 each so you can feed the cats.

name: Bake & More

sample_reviews:
author=Jason Paul Benito; rating=5; time=2026-02-24T03:27:53.655485371Z; relative=2 months ago; text=I had such a great experience at Bake & More! The pastries and breads were incredibly fresh, soft, and full of flavor — you can really taste the quality in every bite. The shop itself is clean, cozy, and very inviting, making it a perfect spot to relax and enjoy a sweet treat. The staff were friendly, accommodating, and provided excellent service throughout my visit. I’ll definitely be coming back for more and highly recommend this place to anyone looking for delicious baked goods in Cebu! || author=Rene Thöne; rating=4; time=2026-01-18T01:03:19.608556863Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Very good bakery with sourdough bread, bread rolls and Brezeln. Very Bad : even dine in you receive one way paper cups || author=Balan V; rating=5; time=2025-12-03T15:16:41.702860534Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Bake & More is an excellent place for any kind of food—whether you’re craving Asian flavours or Western favourites—all lovingly managed by a true-blue Singaporean. Every Singaporean visiting Cebu should make it a point to stop by and savour the delightful creations at Bake & More. I’m certain you’ll find yourself coming back, not only for the delicious food but also for the warm hospitality of the owner and the friendly staff.

Bake & More is truly a must-visit for anyone in Cebu. This is where dining comfort finds its true meaning. || author=Vince Aguirre; rating=3; time=2025-05-31T11:04:04.259214Z; relative=11 months ago; text=I tried their laksa noodle soup, and while the broth had some richness, I wished it were even more so, given how Malay dishes are known for their bold flavors. What stood out to me were the toppings—the fish balls and assorted meats were delicious and really elevated the dish, preventing it from feeling too bland. I also found the noodles a bit tough; a slightly longer cook time could have made them softer and improved the overall experience.

We visited on a quiet Thursday afternoon in late May, and the atmosphere was peaceful. The restaurant is in an upscale neighborhood, and I must say, their coffee was excellent—fragrant, strong, and paired with quick service. || author=ariZona; rating=5; time=2025-11-30T13:16:38.139695959Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Food tastes great and crew on duty were friendly! Reasonably priced since they have huge servings. Will surely come back

name: Kvyn's Coffee Bar

sample_reviews:
author=Hester Anton Bacolod; rating=5; time=2026-03-03T02:13:10.259661601Z; relative=a month ago; text=This specialty coffee shop easily one of our favorite cafes in Cebu.

We genuinely enjoyed everything we had from the flat white to the specialty matcha-it brings back wonderful memories of our recent Japan trip.

PS: Their Basque Burnt Cheesecake is definitely a must try-the best in Cebu! || author=Milcah Reluya; rating=5; time=2026-02-21T06:48:38.301215325Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Really loved their matcha! They’ve got around 3–4 Japanese matcha brands to choose from, and oat milk is already the default (plus free sweetener!). The place feels warm and cozy, and can seat about 12–15 people. It’s right beside a gas station too, so you can easily gas up and grab your matcha fix in one stop. 🙂‍↕️🍵 || author=Liandro Miguel de Paula; rating=5; time=2025-09-08T03:35:03.697110479Z; relative=7 months ago; text=Had two of their flat whites and both cups were delicious - balanced and creamy. Spam meal and chocolate croissant were decent (a bit more chocolate would’ve made it better) and reasonably priced.

The owner was warm and welcoming, and the barista was friendly, too. Clean, minimalist space right by the main road that somehow still felt tucked away and relaxed. Great experience overall. || author=Espresso Yourself; rating=5; time=2025-11-01T11:08:38.477725313Z; relative=5 months ago; text=Kvyn’s Coffee Bar is more than just a place to grab a caffeine fix. This spot truly excels in combining expert craft with old-fashioned hospitality. Baristas like Mark and his colleagues are exceptionally accommodating and friendly, always carrying a bright smile. What really sets them apart is their deep familiarity with coffee, making every interaction informative. This level of service is mirrored by the owners, who are equally accommodating and consistently share a welcoming smile with every customer.

As for the drinks, they are outstanding. The Flat White is simply superb—creamy, perfectly balanced, and expertly prepared. For those who prefer a bold, straightforward cup, the Americano is brewed precisely to preference, delivering a clean flavor. It’s clear that every cup is made with care and attention to detail.

If you are looking for a place that serves exceptional coffee and treats its patrons like family, I highly recommend Kvyn’s Coffee Bar. || author=Chelsea V; rating=5; time=2026-03-10T03:12:28.243575846Z; relative=a month ago; text=Delicious coffee and croissant! Although we were hoping for a French toast, we loved the drinks and croissant and experienced such friendly service!! No vegetarian options other than pastries.

sample_reviews:
author=Joshua Munoz; rating=5; time=2026-02-04T12:37:57.303274489Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Small, cozy, strong wifi and good value. Had sea salt matcha latte and it was good and plenty.

Music a bit loud and space a bit cramped. Lucky i was there with few people so getting comfy seating was no issue.

Still, good place to study or get work done and a decent drink selection. What more could you ask for? || author=SITI ROSMAH MUHAMMAD; rating=5; time=2025-09-06T05:37:09.590794843Z; relative=7 months ago; text=Went there by myself yesterday early evening, the staff greeted me with their warm smile and vibes as I enter. Luckily, it wasn't crowded that time and the ambiance is relaxing despite the small place but the shop can still accomodate enough people but not too much, there's also table outside if you prefer that. The place has two aircons just like in the photos so no worries about getting suffocated with heat.

You can see the menu up close thru tablet in frontdesk that contains their signature drinks and selectable foods / snacks as well, I order one Iced Nutella and they served it after few minutes. It taste great considering it has a kick of Spanish Latte with Nutella paste within the glass. The WiFi strength is decent as well so it's a perfect place to study, chill out with friends, or maybe just having "me time", but make sure to order something and don't be noisy as common courtesy. The staffs there are very accomodating and they sent me off with smile and gratitude. || author=JenSen; rating=5; time=2026-02-04T07:20:02.156705257Z; relative=2 months ago; text=We chose this cafe as our meeting spot with the German Consul to pick up our translated documents, and it was the perfect choice. Highly recommended! || author=Jes Llego; rating=5; time=2025-06-18T11:46:18.314952300Z; relative=10 months ago; text=Small cafe. Only cookies and coffee available when I visited but is a good working cafe - strong wifi, sounds not as loud, with sockets, with T&B and board games available.

Can fit 23 people inside, 4 chairs al fresco. Open until 10pm. Had a spanish latte (with foam and powder) which had a caffeine kick.

Received my loyalty card and prices are from 150-200. Good location also. || author=John Michael Repompo; rating=5; time=2025-08-21T13:53:52.713380080Z; relative=8 months ago; text=Chill place, quiet and comfy. Music also suits the place. We have tried their Melted Peanut Butter Latte (hot) & Dahun Coffee (Iced) paired with clubhouse sandwich, which I highly recommend. I suggest trying out their specialty coffees, and their clubhouse sandwich is so good.

Cozy atmosphere inside that is well air-conditioned, designed with an expensive leafy plants that really fits the name of this cafe.

This coffee shop was a great discovery for us and will surely be on our favorite list.

Wide menus variety

sample_reviews:
author=Ms. Ratatouille; rating=4; time=2025-10-24T11:21:06.647940581Z; relative=6 months ago; text=Amazing scenery. Cats and dogs will be there to entertain you. Cats are amazingly providing outstanding cuddle  while waiting for our food.

The food is nice tho. 😆 || author=Jose Emmanuel Dy; rating=4; time=2025-08-04T09:05:10.248640830Z; relative=8 months ago; text=First of all, the atmosphere is superb. Cool einds, high up in the air, it's just beautiful especially early morning breakfast. I love the ambience.

The food. Food for me, is a little bit bland. I ordered the chorizo, and it was sub par for me. Eggs without salt so no taste there. The servings though, are an entirely different story. They serve BIG servings which makes our tummy super happy! We had the halo-halo but I forgot to take a picture. Kinda bit small also compared to the likes of chowking and whatnot.

Overall it was a fun and great experience for me and the family! Food could have been a lot better or tastier, at least for me, but again very recommended! || author=Sofia T.; rating=4; time=2025-05-30T02:38:48.324134Z; relative=11 months ago; text=Idk what the grilled fish was called but it was too salty. The night view is great. The CR isn't that great¿ I kinda don't like the dogs roaming around. || author=Maybeline Akashi; rating=5; time=2026-02-21T22:29:31.036498224Z; relative=2 months ago; text=I had such a great experience here. The cozy atmosphere made me want to stay longer and just relax. || author=Ella de la Victoria; rating=5; time=2022-10-25T03:28:10.256939Z; relative=3 years ago; text=I’ve been here 3x times now - twice for breakfast and once at night for bbq at their foodtrup chixboy.

For breakfast, it’s always been perfect! Budsikman - budbod, sikwate, mangga - perfect combo! They have silog combos too.
They also have healthy options like fruit smoothie bowls :) the best part is - it’s very affordable! Less than £200 for the meals.

The place is close to Temple of Leah so make sure to visit here before and then walk off the calories after.

The place is just beautiful specially with the wide view overlooking the city.

The staff are also very friendly and attentive.

If this place was close to my house i will be here everyday!!!

At night it gets busier and full blown food trucks are there to serve you bbq, seafood etc.

Love love love!

Absolutely fantastic!

author=Tola; rating=3; time=2025-12-09T10:14:40.490972294Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Been quite curious of this cafe for the past few months and since I’m just living nearby I finally made a try. I love the  latte and spanish latte but they only have one size for their drinks and for me I think it’s expensive compared to other cafes in the area. Payment options can be improved as well. The times I went there their card payment is not working and for someone who rarely uses cash it can be inconvenient. They do have QR though. Overall if you want a spot and escape the chaotic and very busy cafe in Ayala Malls this a go to place. Otherwise if you really wanted a good cup of coffee that’s worth the price, then think twice. || author=Jangmi; rating=2; time=2025-12-04T15:38:06.821509671Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Great place, the location, interior, and overall aesthetic are all wonderful. The staff is great, and it’s coworking-friendly, which I really appreciate!
I’m not a fan of the desserts and pastries, though. They’re displayed without covers, and since one side of the shop opens into a hotel lobby, it feels a bit unsanitary to me (just my personal preference). The cheesecake wasn’t worth the price, and it tasted more like sour cream than cream cheese. I couldn’t taste any Biscoff, the biscoff cookie on top was already soft, and honestly it didn’t taste fresh. The texture was almost jelly-like, and for 270 pesos the slice was quite small, you might as well make one yourself.

The pound cake was good, but again, small for its price. The matcha was mid. You can get better matcha elsewhere. I haven’t tried their coffee yet, so maybe I’ll go back for their croissant and coffee just to get the full picture.

sample_reviews:
author=Al Al; rating=1; time=2024-06-21T11:49:13.733201Z; relative=a year ago; text=Delectable Cheesecake, Disappointing Service - zero

The cheesecake itself was excellent - the filling had a perfect creamy texture and the flavors were well-balanced. It's clear they use quality ingredients and take pride in their baking.

However, the service experience really brought down the overall visit. The staff, especially the cashier, seemed disinterested and unwelcoming. They acted as if they were forced to be there, providing minimal interaction and making it quite difficult to place our order.

It almost felt like the cashier was having trouble communicating, though there was no signage indicating she was deaf or hard of hearing. Better accomodations should be made in that case to ensure a smooth ordering process for customers.

The overall atmosphere felt tense and hurried, which is a shame given how delicious the cheesecake was. I would recommend the owners look into providing more customer service training for their staff to match the quality of their baked goods.

While I would return for the cheesecake itself, the poor service makes me hesitant to do so.
