Here's the revised review with your note about the staff included:

---

**Marisse Patisserie – Busay**
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ (5/5)

Tucked in the cool, misty hills of Busay, Marisse Patisserie is the perfect place to cozy up with something sweet—especially on a rainy afternoon. After coming from Brickwood, we decided to stop by for dessert, and it turned out to be one of the highlights of the day.

As someone who’s very mindful of what I eat (and always counting calories), I tried my best not to give in—but this place is an irresistible guilty pleasure. We tried their cakes and croissants, and every bite was absolutely worth it. The cakes had a genuine, homemade quality, and the croissants were flaky, buttery, and baked to perfection. No regrets at all.

What made the experience even better was the exceptional service. The staff were warm, helpful, and never pushy. They genuinely listened to what we liked, offered thoughtful suggestions, and recommended some of the best items on the menu. Their relaxed and friendly approach made us feel completely at ease.

Prices are very reasonable for the quality, and the ambiance is charming—quiet, inviting, and ideal for relaxing while watching the rain outside.

Final thoughts:
Whether you're coming from a meal at Brickwood or just passing through Busay, Marisse Patisserie is a delightful stop. Delicious desserts, a cozy atmosphere, and genuinely kind staff and NOT A CROWDED PLACE make it a place I’d gladly visit again. Just be ready to indulge a little—you won’t regret it. || author=AJQ; rating=3; time=2020-02-09T09:49:59.034776Z; relative=6 years ago; text=It’s a quaint cafe in the middle 32 Sanson by Rockwell. The place is peaceful and relaxing || author=Herbert Kikoy; rating=5; time=2017-06-14T22:34:42.218Z; relative=8 years ago; text=If you’re looking for a haven close to perfection, Marisse Patisserie is the place to be. the cafe is small but kudos to their layout; every space was maximize and it had spacious tables. good for studying and catching up with friends.

service was fast and staff was pleasant. unfortunately, the hojicha wasn't my cup of tea. it had a bitter aftertaste that's the not very hard to miss. matcha chai was okay but the chai was too overpowering.

will def visit again to explore other matcha options || author=Ramie Shean Pradas; rating=1; time=2025-09-20T08:02:43.152566010Z; relative=7 months ago; text=As a big fan of matcha, I had high hopes — but this was honestly the worst matcha drink I’ve ever had.

We ordered the Iced Matcha Latte with oat milk. Right away, the color looked wrong — yellowish instead of green, which made me think they weren’t using real matcha powder. The taste was even worse: very fishy, like it had been pre-mixed days ago and left in the fridge.

Definitely coming back for another dish to try. || author=Carlo Andrew Olano; rating=5; time=2023-10-17T17:24:57.601180Z; relative=2 years ago; text=🌟 Crosswalk Bakery & Cafe: A True Delight for Foodies!🌟

Nestled in a charming corner, Crosswalk Bakery & Cafe is an absolute gem that's worth every visit. This place isn't just about desserts; it's a haven for all food enthusiasts.

My recent trip here left me genuinely impressed. As soon as you walk in, you're greeted by a cozy 60's diner vibe, decked out in soothing pastel colors that make it feel like a foodie's paradise.

I started with their burger, and trust me, it was no ordinary burger. Paired with their White Rabbit milkshake, it was a match made in heaven for my taste buds. The flavors were simply out of this world!

But the burger is just the beginning. Crosswalk Bakery & Cafe has a diverse menu that caters to every craving. From tantalizing ribs to the rich flavors of lengua, and if you're a pasta lover, their Kimchi Carbonara is a must-try.

And oh, desserts! Crosswalk is a true paradise for cake lovers. The Cebu Mango Nut Cake is a real standout – a heavenly slice of cheesecake topped with Cebu's famous sweet mangoes, with Rosquillos as the base and cashews adding the perfect crunch.

But that's not all. They offer a delightful variety of cake creations. From the Nutella Velvet Tub to the Chocnut Oreo Cheesecake, every dessert here is a masterpiece.

So, for fellow food lovers, it's time to gather your crew and explore Crosswalk Bakery & Cafe. It's where every bite is a delightful experience. You can find this gem at the corner of Don Jose Avila and Juana Osmeña Streets, and they're open daily from 10 am to 10 pm.

Don't miss out on this place – your taste buds will thank you! 🤤 #CrosswalkBakeryCafe #FoodieFinds #KalamiCebu

The coffee pairs perfectly with everything, smooth, warm, and comforting. A great place to relax and treat yourself! ☕🍰✨ || author=Manuel Villanueva; rating=5; time=2025-10-25T09:09:18.886707220Z; relative=6 months ago; text=Must-Try Dishes
Cafe Georg is known for serving up comforting and refined dishes with generous portion sizes. Customers frequently highlight the savory appetizers and decadent desserts.

Spinach & Cheese Dip: This is arguably the most famous appetizer, with multiple long-time patrons calling it "THE BEST!" It's a highly recommended start to any meal.

Mains & Savory: Popular choices include the deliciously marinated Lemongrass Chicken and the hearty Pork Chops with Garlic Herb Butter. Reviewers also recommend the Hickory Ribs (praised for their tender, melt-in-your-mouth meat) and the flavorful Penang dish. They also offer a variety of options for different preferences, including a Black Bean and Quinoa Burger for vegan and vegetarian diners.

Desserts: The cakes and pastries are a major draw, often cited as the perfect edible gift. Must-tries include the rich and decadent Kahlua Chocolate Mousse, the classic Carrot Walnut Cake, and the Avocado Pie, which one reviewer calls "to die for." || author=itsMarga; rating=5; time=2025-08-05T04:20:02.334293147Z; relative=8 months ago; text=My Pasta Haven 🍝
Cafe George has always been my go-to restaurant whenever I’m craving pasta. They offer a wide variety of dishes on their menu, but their pasta? Never disappoints.
Here are some of my all-time favorites:

🥣 Spinach & Cheese Dip – 10/10 My ultimate favorite! Creamy, flavorful, and perfect for sharing (or not!).
🥗 Caesar Salad – 10/10 Fresh, crisp, and generously topped with dressing and cheese—just how I like it.
🍝 Carbonara – 10/10 Rich, creamy, and perfectly seasoned. A must-try for any pasta lover.
🍖 Classic Pork Belly – 8/10 Tender and flavorful
🍗 Spinach Chicken – 8/10 a pasta with 2 spinach chickens on top
☕ Spanish Latte – 9/10 Smooth and comforting. The sweetness is just right—not overpowering.
Dessert: 🍫 Chocolate Decadence – 10/10 If you’re a fan of dark chocolate, this is it. Rich, intense, and satisfying. Feels like indulging in a chocolate cake and dark chocolate in one bite.

🍰 Cheesecake – 8/10
Creamy not overly sweet.

🌿 Ambiance – 10/10
The restaurant exudes charming old/vintage vibes—cozy, timeless, and perfect for slow, relaxing meals.

The cake base of the chocolate cake is a bit dense, but the ganache is wonderful, the almonds, if candied or roasted might add a more interesting texture.  Will try more cakes!!  I HAVE IMCLUDED EVIDENCE OF MY GLUTTONY. || author=Nicole Nuñez; rating=4; time=2025-09-05T19:48:12.651682900Z; relative=7 months ago; text=The small yet lovely and warm shop welcomed me and my mother on a cold, rainy day. What made it even warmer was the friendliness and hospitality of the owner and the staff. Super approachable and accommodating 💖

For dine in, we ordered their famous Classic Leche Flan, slices of their Chocolate Temptation cake (Best of Cebu 2023) and Coffee Crumble Cheesecake, and hot Café Americano. For take out (separate day), we ordered slices of their New York-Style Cheesecake topped w/ blueberry, Dreamy Ube Cake, and Rebellious Red Velvet cake.

Classic Leche Flan
• A creamy, rich, and melt-in-your-mouth goodness that is not overly sweet
• Really loved the taste of the brown syrup (also rich and not overly sweet), which makes the flan even more indulgent

Chocolate Temptation
• 'Though moist and soft, the chocolate cake base overpowered both the chocolate ganache frosting and the flan in terms of taste. Each component was of good quality, but I would have liked it more if all flavors were harmoniously present. If the choco cake base were lessened up a bit or if the choco ganache (as a filling) and the flan were added just a bit more, I would have devoured the whole thing in seconds. 🙈 Nevertheless, it was a nice treat. 🤎
• Pouring the sugar syrup of the Classic Leche Flan onto the cake (esp. the flan part) made it much, much better. It was so darn good! 😋

Coffee Crumble Cheesecake
• THE BEST I'VE TASTED FROM THE SHOP SO FAR 🥰 Currently one of my top favorite cheesecakes 🖤✨🤎 Planning to get this for my birthday (skl) 😁
• What I loved most about this cheesecake is that the flavors were friends, not rivals. 🤝 The coffee flavor is there but didn't dominate the whole thing. It blended so well with the cheesecake base, which I believe is not an easy thing to accomplish. 💛

Hot Café Americano
• A good cup of strong, quality coffee
• It didn't turn sour, unlike in other cafés or coffee shops. 🖤✨️

New York-Style Cheesecake topped w/ blueberry
• At this point, I gotta say they do make really good cheesecakes. 😋🫶
• The creamy, rich, light cheesecake went so well with the mild blueberry topping. 💛💜 Another harmony of flavors❣️

Dreamy Ube Cake
• The ube frosting and cake did not have the typical ube flavor that I'm used to (which is quite similar to that of the Selecta Ube ice cream), but the unique taste of ube was still there. 💜
• The creamy, smooth, light flan in the middle of the ube cake base made the cake even more decadent overall. 😋
• A great sweet treat, but perhaps too much so (mainly because of the sweet ube frosting) 😅

Rebellious Red Velvet
• The cake itself was a bit dense for my liking, and the cream filling was a bit less for the thickness of the cake. I also prefer cream cheese frosting/filling for red velvet cakes, but I couldn't taste the sourness in this one.
• Loved the cookie crumb crust 🖤✨️ quite an unusual ingredient for a red velvet cake, but the soft crunch brought a fun texture and taste 😋

Overall, I would recommend this shop to my fellow sweet tooths ❣️ If you want quality sweets and goodies that are relatively affordable and of adequate portions, you will love it here. ✨️ || author=Jen Ina; rating=5; time=2025-12-07T11:23:35.416364296Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Owner and staff are incredibly friendly and approachable. Love the dark chocolate cake with leche flan in the middle. The dark chocolate isn’t too sweet and ithe leche flan combo is just incredible. 🫶💗

author=RCG; rating=4; time=2025-11-27T15:28:58.479436224Z; relative=5 months ago; text=I absolutely love their yogurt ice cream! The texture is incredibly creamy, and the flavor isn’t too sour..  perfect even for those who aren’t usually into yogurt-based desserts. They also have a wide variety of toppings to choose from, and I love that you can already get a large cup with 4 toppings included. Their strawberries are especially delicious and sweet! definitely a must-try combo! 🍓

The serving size is generous, so you really get your money’s worth. Aside from the yogurt, they also offer fruit sandwiches. I wasn’t able to try them yet because they were already sold out during my visit, which just shows how good their food must be!

As for the location, it’s very convenient since it’s situated in IT Park Cebu, making it easy to stop by anytime. The only thing I hope they improve in the future is the space. The area inside is a bit small, and it can get crowded quickly. Still, it’s a place worth visiting, especially if you’re craving something refreshing and satisfying.

Overall, highly recommended! their yogurt treats are not only delicious but also fun to customize! 🥰🍨  Attached some of the photos I took and my take-out orders. || author=Kirsten Zoë Roos; rating=5; time=2026-01-02T10:06:06.710573968Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Yogurt was super delicious and it was a very short wait. Tasted super fresh and the toppings were also fresh (the fruit options). The other toppings offer a large range of sweet toppings and even some unexpected ones which would bring many customers like the mochi topping. || author=Rikka Marie Ramos; rating=5; time=2025-08-26T05:50:56.966272241Z; relative=8 months ago; text=A Must-Try in Cebu!
Definitely a must when visiting Cebu! We first tried it via Grab/Foodpanda, and the packaging was impressively secure—no spills at all. When we visited the physical store, everything was clean and well-organized. I didn’t mind the line at all since it moved quickly and efficiently.
The yogurt leans less on the sour side, but its creaminess and smooth texture more than make up for it. We absolutely loved their topping selection—especially how they only slice fresh fruit upon request, which keeps everything super fresh.
Really hoping they bring this to Manila soon. More branches, please! || author=JC; rating=5; time=2025-04-30T15:30:16.271355Z; relative=12 months ago; text=This is my favorite yogurt ice cream shop here in Cebu.

I've been here so many times and each photo I've uploaded is a separate visit. Where do I even begin?

I guess when they first opened their doors in 2024, I was genuinely curious about the kind of ice cream that they offered. I've tried a lot of yogurt ice cream in other stores before and it's always the same cheap, sour, artificial ice cream recipe.

This shop is different. They made the perfect yogurt ice cream recipe that tastes like vanilla and yogurt mixed together. It's neither too sour nor too sweet, it's just perfect. The flavor pairs very well with their fruit toppings especially with strawberry, hami melon, blueberry and mixed nuts. The ice cream also melts slowly so you can enjoy it for a longer time compared to other yogurt ice cream shops (I'm looking at you, llaollao. No more yogurt tinola soup with squished fruits from llaollao haha!).

They provide a wide selection of toppings but the general rule here is you can only select a maximum of 2 fruit toppings and 1 sauce, regardless of the size you ordered. Every size also has a limited number of topping choices but they serve most toppings in generous amounts.

Their matcha ice cream also has a very pleasant taste (after all, it's uji matcha), but keep in mind that they no longer serve matcha ice cream beyond the cutoff time (I think it was 6pm, not sure).

So far, these are my top recommendations:

1. Matcha base ice cream with strawberries, blueberries, mixed nuts, and something chocolate (either chocolate dip or KitKat is best)

2. Yogurt base ice cream with mango jam, hami melon, strawberries, and muscat (hami melon is not always available so I swap with mixed nuts, KitKat, or dragon fruit)

3. Mango yogurt frappe (the mango jam is sooooo good)

4. Strawberry yogurt frappe (my very first frappe and still one of the undefeated classics)

Here are some opportunities for improvement:

- The interior is quite small. There are three maximum tables and two to three waiting chairs inside the air conditioned room, and then the rest of the chairs and tables are outside. The place can get easily crowded especially during peak hours (weekends, Friday nights, and dinner time). I miss the time when the store was still new and only a few of us know about the place haha. Maybe you can open a second branch?

- The matcha ice cream has a cutoff time. This was not a problem before when you can order the matcha base beyond 6pm last year. I do not know the reason behind this change and I was surprised.

- The sandwiches look good but the bread loaf is actually dry, thin, and tastes like old paper. Maybe you can switch to a different bread supplier and I can try ordering sandwiches again?

- The frappes can be confusing because I thought all frappes have ice cream on top. Only the strawberry, mango, and blueberry frappes have ice cream. Please update your menu and categorize frappes with ice cream vs frappes with no ice cream.

Very good key points from this store:

- The staff are always very friendly, accommodating, and kind towards customers. I also met the Korean staff (they might be the owners idk) and they were also very kind and reliable. When you finish eating and walk out the door, they thank you for coming and open the door for you, just like before entering.

- Very good design and presentation. They seem to follow a common rule when preparing and arranging toppings on the ice cream. Even the frappes are nice to look at.

- Well-prepared and always clean. The tables are always clean because the staff are proactive in cleaning tables as soon as the customer leaves. There are good quality tissues on the counter. The staff wear glove, hairnets, and aprons. I never found a hair on my yogurt ever.

- They are now available on Grab!

- The air conditioning unit operates at a comfortable temperature. The music is very pleasant to the ears.

I already recommended this shop to my friends and coworkers. Please keep up the quality and I will stay as a "suki" here. Maybe, you can have a promo for repeat customers. || author=Gem Rodriguez; rating=5; time=2024-07-24T11:30:12.787413Z; relative=a year ago; text=I like the aesthetic of this cafe even if it’s a little bit small. The frozen yogurt is creamy and delicious it is highly recommended. There were a lot of toppings to choose from and I can say that the fruit toppings are high quality! Very fresh mango and sweet strawberry. Also the Korean owners are hands in to their business and smiling. Overall the service and food are excellent. 너무 맛있어!

sample_reviews:
author=Leila Uy; rating=5; time=2025-12-10T14:50:40.421382736Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Has a cozy and relaxing atmosphere perfect for casual dates or hangout with friends, great food and friendly staff, making it a solid choice for both sweet treats and light meals.

My to go to desserts soft, rich, and not overly sweet cakes. Just be sure to visit during off-peak hours to avoid crowds and potential delays. 🥰😋 || author=Angel Oliquino; rating=3; time=2025-05-27T02:09:27.430989Z; relative=11 months ago; text=We visited The Dessert Factory and had a really good dining experience! Although they are mostly known for cakes and sweets, we decided to try something different and ordered their salad — and it turned out to be really good. It was fresh, flavorful, and nicely presented. You can tell the ingredients were prepared well.

The service was also good. The staff were polite, attentive, and quick to assist us. They served the food on time and made sure we had everything we needed during our meal. It felt comfortable to dine there because they were welcoming, but not too intrusive.

The restaurant itself has a cozy and relaxing atmosphere. The interiors are bright and clean, and the place is perfect for chill hangouts or casual dates. Of course, we couldn’t leave without trying their famous desserts — and they definitely live up to the hype! Their cakes are soft, rich, and not too sweet — just right.

Whether you’re craving sweets or looking for a light meal, The Dessert Factory is a solid choice. Great food, great service, and a relaxing vibe. Will definitely come back to try more from their menu! || author=Danilo Dillo; rating=1; time=2025-11-16T07:56:51.874123551Z; relative=5 months ago; text=The Greatest Scam in the Restaurant Industry of Cebu City

I am writing to express my dissatisfaction with a recent promotional offer and the overall customer service at your establishment. I availed the promotion independently, expecting to apply my PWD/senior citizen discount as per RA No. 10754. However, I was informed that the promotion must be combined with my other food orders, preventing me from utilizing my entitled discount. Additionally, your policy of accepting only a single bill without separate payments further restricted my ability to benefit from the discount, which I believe contravenes RA No. 10754.

Furthermore, I encountered issues with the service. An additional cup of coffee was wasted due to inefficiencies in your delivery process, and the customer service provided was unsatisfactory. These experiences have significantly impacted my perception of your establishment.

As a result, I will no longer patronize Dessert Factory and will refrain from ordering dishes such as kinamatisang kawali in the future. I urge you to review your policies and service procedures to ensure compliance with applicable laws and to improve the customer experience. || author=AntonKZ; rating=5; time=2025-08-23T10:23:09.794496017Z; relative=8 months ago; text=Good chili con carne, good sweet and sour fish, good pork belly. Chill and quiet, not too many people and not too noisy around 18:00 || author=Denice Eloisa; rating=5; time=2025-01-08T10:56:53.062276Z; relative=a year ago; text=Our go to dinner spot after work. Big portions! My personal faves are their Chicken Parmigiana, Pork Belly and Sizzling Bulalo Steak. And oh, their The Factory Salad’s decent and affordably priced at 149 - good for 1 person.

types: bakery, food_store, food, store, point_of_interest, establishment

sample_reviews:
author=Diana Ross; rating=5; time=2025-12-30T11:56:55.839752884Z; relative=3 months ago; text=I like the place, it’s cold and cozy. I like pastries not too sweet and affordable. Though the iced coffee latte I got was too bitter. I wish there was an option to add more sweetness on it because it’s too bitter even I love iced americanos. But recommended cafe. || author=MENSEUNG; rating=5; time=2025-08-20T00:44:49.724106276Z; relative=8 months ago; text=If you're on the hunt for a new favorite spot in Mabolo that promises more than just a good cup of coffee, look no further than Kaffee Alde. This charming establishment truly stands out, offering a delightful experience that caters to both your sweet tooth and your need for a relaxed, comfortable environment.

From the moment you approach, one of Kaffee Alde's most noteworthy features immediately becomes apparent: its spacious parking. In a city where finding a convenient spot can often be a frustrating ordeal, Kaffee Alde provides ample space, taking away the stress before you even step inside. This thoughtful amenity sets a relaxed tone for your visit, allowing you to settle in without worry.

Stepping through the doors, you're greeted by an ambiance that strikes a perfect balance between cozy and chic. The interior is well-designed, offering various seating options that cater to different needs – whether you're looking for a quiet corner to work, a comfortable couch for a casual chat, or a spacious table for a group gathering. The lighting is warm and inviting, contributing to an atmosphere that encourages you to linger and unwind. It's the kind of place where you can comfortably spend hours, soaking in the pleasant surroundings.

But the true stars of the show at Kaffee Alde are undoubtedly their sweets and cakes. The display case is a veritable wonderland, showcasing a great selection that will make any dessert lover's heart sing. From intricate pastries to comforting slices, there's something to tempt every palate. What makes their offerings truly exceptional is that the cakes are not only cute but also incredibly affordable. It's rare to find such charming, well-made desserts that don't break the bank, making them perfect for an everyday treat or a special occasion. Each piece looks like a work of art, and the taste lives up to the visual appeal, ensuring a satisfyingly sweet experience.

Adding to the overall positive impression is the excellent service provided by the team. The staff were accommodating, exuding a warmth and genuine helpfulness that made the visit even more pleasant. They were attentive without being overbearing, quick to assist, and happy to offer recommendations, contributing significantly to the welcoming atmosphere. Their professionalism and friendly demeanor truly elevate the entire Kaffee Alde experience.

In summary, Kaffee Alde in Mabolo is a gem that beautifully combines convenience, comfort, and culinary delights. With its stress-free spacious parking, inviting ambiance, impressive array of delicious and affordable sweets (especially those adorable cakes!), and impeccably accommodating staff, it's a place you'll want to revisit again and again. Whether you're looking for a quiet spot to work, a cozy place for a catch-up with friends, or simply craving something sweet, Kaffee Alde is an absolute must-visit. || author=Caroline Balila; rating=5; time=2025-08-04T07:25:32.669661658Z; relative=8 months ago; text=They serve pastries, drinks and meals at a very affordable price. Very delicious too. Their shop is spacious and you can dine in. No wifi. They offer buy 1 take 1 on some of their items when they're about to close na. || author=JV Marti (JeiVii); rating=5; time=2026-01-22T22:56:55.062690096Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Went here and bought banana and marble loaves for takeout. Both tasted good, and the banana loaf wasn’t too oily, which I liked.

The place is neat and quiet, with a pleasant ambiance. The parking lot is also spacious.

The items aren’t too pricey but are still good quality. I’d definitely come back to try their other products, especially their drinks. || author=HJ Cruz; rating=4; time=2025-08-14T04:39:22.713305225Z; relative=8 months ago; text=Affordable pastries!!! This is their best seller and always sold out menu.
The store is located in Ayala Central Bloc, beside the Entrance and Exit. This is inside IT Park and very accessible in different mode of transportation.

They also offer hot and cold drinks and other flavor of cakes. || author=Cielo C; rating=1; time=2025-02-09T20:28:53.504316Z; relative=a year ago; text=Not recommended for pet owners. Pet-tolerant, not pet-friendly.

I last went here in 22 July 2023 with my mom, sister, and dog. We wanted to cool off on a hot afternoon. We ordered an avocado cream pie and 3 coffees, along with cold service water in reusable glasses. We were the only customers at first.

I asked the barista if I could have a disposable cup for water for my dog. Even if they had a large stack by the counter, she said she wasn’t allowed to give me one for my thirsty pet. I asked her who told her she wasn’t allowed? She said the owner.

I explained that 1) it’s disposable so it’s not a sanitary issue, 2) we are paying customers, 3) it’s a pet-friendly mall. She insisted it wasn’t allowed. I had to go to Metro to buy disposable cups for my dog to drink water. By this time, a couple had walked in to dine in, but they were also given reusable glasses and the store was still not busy.

My family and I were regulars at Treat Street. After this incident, we never went back. This probably could have been avoided had the staff been properly trained to explain why management restricted my request or if the cafe just put up a sign stating no pets allowed. || author=Khanna Blithe O. Cortes-Amores; rating=4; time=2021-12-08T00:13:44.542245Z; relative=4 years ago; text=Free water and the avocado cake is yummy! It is a bit sweet but the texture is really like avocado.

name: Hawker Chan - SM Seaside City, Cebu

sample_reviews:
author=Wyll Jie; rating=4; time=2025-09-18T11:31:32.831006914Z; relative=7 months ago; text=If you’re craving Singaporean-inspired dishes in Cebu, Hawker Chan at SM Seaside is worth a visit. The place is clean, cozy, and welcoming — not too spacious, but it doesn’t feel crowded either, which makes dining more comfortable.

We ordered their Medium-Sized Chicken and Pork Platter along with the Beef Brisket Noodle Soup. The platter is actually a good choice if you’re dining with friends or family since the medium size is already filling for 4 people. My personal favorite from the platter would be the fried dumplings — crispy, flavorful, and definitely something I’d order again. The Hainanese chicken was tender and tasty, although I did notice a slight odor that I wasn’t too fond of. The pork, on the other hand, was savory and cooked just right.

As for the Beef Brisket Noodle Soup, the broth was flavorful and comforting, but a bit too greasy for my liking. Still, it’s something noodle lovers might enjoy if they prefer a richer soup.

Overall, the dining experience was fine — not extraordinary, but satisfying. Hawker Chan delivers a warm and casual vibe, perfect for a quick meal with friends or family when you’re at SM Seaside. || author=Carlo Andrew Olano; rating=5; time=2025-08-14T16:34:31.459125589Z; relative=8 months ago; text=We had lunch at Hawker Chan in SM Seaside City Cebu yesterday, and it turned out to be a really filling and satisfying experience. Since we arrived past 12 noon, we had to wait around 10 minutes before getting a table. Luckily, they have seats for waiting, although at first the staff thought we were just resting. Once I asked, they accommodated us right away.

What I love here are their set meals and trays. Honestly, I think this place is underrated. At first glance, ₱200–₱300 per meal might seem pricey, but when you realize the set already includes meat, rice, a drink, noodles, dessert, and even soup, it’s actually a bargain.

The Hainanese Chicken can come with egg, veggies, or even laksa on the side, making it really filling. And if you’re into roasts, this is where Hawker Chan shines. Their soy chicken, BBQ pork, roast pork, and roast duck are some of the best in Cebu in this price range.

For me, Hawker Chan strikes that perfect balance between good price and excellent food. It’s the closest you can get to the hawker centers of Singapore without leaving Cebu. Definitely worth the visit if you’re craving authentic Singaporean flavors. || author=Alliza Chavez; rating=5; time=2025-08-01T02:14:25.881297358Z; relative=8 months ago; text=Great food. Ordered their Set A meal. Rice was packed with flavors - I could eat that alone, it was THAT good. Chicken was tender and NOT dry (we love a moist chicken).

The BEST part of this meal is the Sesame Bun. We did not expect the RICH FILLING inside. I'd go back there just to eat that again. || author=Isabel Ortega; rating=4; time=2025-10-24T06:41:48.054690276Z; relative=6 months ago; text=Had lunch here yesterday and it was good, service was good as well. name: Silogan Ni Gian
address: Tower 1, 8 Arlington Pond St, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Dino Batain; rating=5; time=2023-08-21T15:20:12.203507Z; relative=2 years ago; text=This is definitely one of my top choices when I'm hungry and craving delicious yet affordable food. When I'm looking for a quick and satisfying meals, then Silogan ni Gian is my go-to place. I really enjoy the meals here, and you should too! || author=rich ashley; rating=5; time=2022-06-18T23:14:34.215312Z; relative=3 years ago; text=Silogan ni Gian is a crowd favorite.
I remember I introduced some of my friends to eat here and they got hooked. Reasonably priced and really really good food.
Filipinos love silog. What is silog? Rice and Egg. (Plain rice and sunny side up/runny yolk/over easy -- is the best way to do it. -- ok garlic rice is also a wonderful alternative 😅 shout out to the garlic lovers)

And silog is best paired with? All kinds of meat!

PORKSILOG is my favorite!!! Bone in Porkchops seasoned perfectly, tender yet gives a good bite and silog. Im salivating as im typing this now.
I always add extra porkchop as 1 will never be enough.
If you want sawsawan (sauce that enhances the flavor when we dip something in it) they provide it with onions, calamansi (filipino limes) and chilis plus your concoction of vinegar and soy. Ugh! So basic but IT IS THE BEST!

I also add bacon and chicken skin to my order 🤣
But they have baconsilog
Chickenskin silog
Sisigsilog
Hungariansilog
____ silog 🤣

Tons of variations, it depends on whatever you fancy really.

The best part? Reasonably priced!!!
Worth every cent.

Idk how many branches they have and how some other branches look like as I had mine delivered through grab food.
Dined in some of them -- theres electric fan (comes in very handy as you will enjoy the food and if you have it spicy and hot, youre gonna sweat 🤣😅, a sink where you could wash your hands, monobloc tables and chairs, spoon and fork (yes we know how to use them apart from using our hands).

Anywayyyyy

Starfactor 5/5
Food solid 5stars
Price 10 stars!!
Service 5/5 no fuss
Ambiance 3/5 bahala na kay LAMI! || author=Louis Springer; rating=5; time=2025-04-21T10:38:04.712728Z; relative=a year ago; text=Silogan ni Gian is a breakfast gem in Cebu City. We tried the corned beefsilog and sisigsilog—both were flavorful, generously portioned, and hit the spot perfectly. The prices are budget-friendly, and the whole experience is quick, simple, and satisfying—ideal for a no-fuss, hearty start to the day. The staff was warm and welcoming, and even though it was a warm morning, the fan kept things comfortable. Highly recommended if you’re craving a classic silog meal done right! || author=Wolfgang “Captain.DavyJones” Santifaller; rating=5; time=2023-11-23T02:04:58.056797Z; relative=2 years ago; text=Good food, good price and very lami.

The restaurant serves Hong Kong–style street food and the dishes are packed with flavor, especially their dim sum and noodle dishes. Everything we ordered came out hot and freshly cooked, and the portions were generous for the price. Many people recommend items like hakaw, siomai, fried rice, and other dim sum, which are known for their rich taste and good serving size. The place can get busy because a lot of people come here to enjoy the food, but that just shows how popular it is. Overall, it’s a great spot in Cebu if you’re craving flavorful Chinese or Hong Kong-style comfort food that is both delicious and affordable. || author=Chipper Chihuahua; rating=5; time=2026-02-15T12:41:14.785815223Z; relative=2 months ago; text=the atmosphere feels very cafeteria-ish. but it sort of adds to the street food authenticity vibes. the queue is long during rush hours but the food is definitely worth it. the laksa was exquisite, served on a mini stove to keep it boiling and you can request to add more hot water. good for sharing unless you have a huge appetite. the dimsum and other fried foods were also amazing. instead of rice, we opted for the HK style fried noodles and it goes so well with the side sauces they offer. || author=Dominique Varga; rating=5; time=2026-02-18T07:41:03.725614503Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Every time I come and visit Eat Fresh I always get the original laksa. Will never get tired of the food here it’s definitely something I will always come back to. Tried almost everything in the menu already and so far these are the ones that catch my pallet.  Great service too, all waiters are accommodating most especially if its not lunch rush or dinner rush. 10/10 food! || author=Layla P.; rating=5; time=2025-11-19T16:23:10.983032287Z; relative=5 months ago; text=I'll definitely come back for more 🤤

All the dishes we ordered were incredibly good. My favorite was the hakaw, which was steamed to perfection and I love their chili garlic sauce that goes perfectly with it.  Also, don't miss their steamed custard bun, it's perfectly steamy and sweet.

The service was also exceptional. The servers were accommodating and they know their dishes so well, so trust their recommendations.

The atmosphere is not cozy and I don't think it's meant to be cozy at all, but it has a certain charm to it. There's an air of nostalgia and familiarity, it's almost reminiscent of a school cafeteria or a foodcourt. || author=Aaron Nuñez; rating=5; time=2025-09-29T03:55:00.701128752Z; relative=7 months ago; text=What a wonderful culinary experience, it truly felt like being transported to the bustling food streets of Hong Kong! The dishes here are bursting with flavor and variety, making it a spot worth returning to again and again.

I absolutely loved the Char Kway Teow (a must-try!), the Three Mushrooms with Vegetables, and of course their Jumbo Siomai which was hearty, flavorful, and so satisfying.  Pair these with their delicious milk tea, and you’ve got yourself a winning combination.

I also tried the Salted Egg Chicken Rice, which was good, though personally it didn’t stand out to me as much as the other dishes. That said, the menu is packed with so many choices that there’s always something new to explore and enjoy.

Overall, Eat Fresh is a place full of vibrant flavors and authentic Hong Kong-style comfort food, definitely worth coming back for!

sample_reviews:
author=Clydale Bentulan; rating=5; time=2026-02-22T09:09:44.393921847Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Saw this suggestion from threads and I can say “This is the place I want to go back to” 🫶🏻❤️ huge servings for a good prices. Mi gorend was savory and requested runny egg. First time trying Thai Bagoonh fried rice and it was sooooo good!!! Chicken satay was okay but wished it has the peanut sauce.

Coffee is soo goood!! But we didn’t much like the milo drink, too sweet and wished we have requested to make it less sweet. Banana pancake is so delicious too!! (Forgot to take a picture of it)

We requested to take out the left overs and they were so kind. We tought they will just put it in a paper bag or plastic but it is in microwavable tupperwares. Thank u so much!

Overall experience is 5/5 ! || author=Raine; rating=5; time=2025-11-01T05:06:45.821006715Z; relative=5 months ago; text=Really nice place. Food is really good too at an affordable price point.

Place is a bit hot, but overall comfy and quiet. Menu selection is great and gives you a lot to choose from.

Got their Special Pad Thai and it was so good. The fried pork and shrimp dumplings were so good too!

No photos but I did order their regular Com Ga Nuong and Sambal Fried Rice for take out and those were really good too! Kinda wished I would've gotten the overload instead tho to try out the sausage.

Also ordered their Roti Gluay Milo and Martabak for dessert and these did not disappoint. Had no idea what they would taste like but it was a good blend of sweet and salty (Martabak had chocolate, cheese and condensed milk inside).

As for the drinks, had the iced tea with my meal and it was good. Got their Milo Godzilla and Milo Dinosaur for take out and they were good but I'd suggest drinking these asap.

Overall very satisfied and excited to try their other dishes! || author=Dale Aldrin Maniego; rating=2; time=2025-10-23T15:18:36.444761876Z; relative=6 months ago; text=Food - I would say I'm disappointed since I expected that it would be at least be decent and similar to the original taste.
1. Sambal rice - hmmmm I know sambal since I was a kid, the sambal rice here tasted like chilli oil and soy sauce, definitely not suited for that price range in my opinion (It was more similar to sinangag actually)

2. Tom Yum Goong - This is a total fail sadly. It tasted far from tom yum actually (aside from the soup not having any prawns). It was not aromatic at all as well (No Kaffir leaves and lemon grass aroma) and to make it worst, it was really salty (salty enough that you'll think about your kidney after tasting it a few times)

3. Thai Green Mango Salad - Not sure if this should taste like Som Tum coz if it does then it tasted far as well but I would say that it tasted good actually, just not accurate?

Place - Place is nice and cosy.

Service - Attentive and fast so kudos on that.

Overall if you're looking for authentic indo, thai, vietnam food, sadly, I would not recommend this place. Even the price doesn't justify the taste 😅😅. || author=Mike Paul; rating=5; time=2025-11-23T18:28:44.647155900Z; relative=5 months ago; text=Crazy underrated place!
Food was really good and affordable for the quality & serving 👌💯
Ordered the Pad Thai & Thai Bagoong Fried Rice but highlight for me was the Pork & Shrimp Dumplings at 99php 🤯
Also loved the Roti Gluay!🤤
Will definitely come back next time! 😁

Came back for the 2nd time and everything was still good 💯
I liked the Mi Goreng than Pad Thai tho || author=Zia Low; rating=4; time=2026-02-13T15:25:44.498781993Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Has a vegan tofu banme and tofu salad both very nice with fast service. name: Ned Nanay's Grill
address: Don Mariano Cui St, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Lean Gomez; rating=5; time=2025-11-14T04:19:59.211925091Z; relative=5 months ago; text=Ned Nanay’s Grill has truly become our go-to barbecue place. Although the location sits a bit inside from the main highway and the store sign isn’t very eye-catching, it’s still easy enough to find once you’re familiar with the area. The restaurant offers both an indoor, air-conditioned dining area and an outdoor setup with plenty of electric fans, and surprisingly, both spaces are clean, comfortable, and pleasant even during busy hours.

The barbecue is the real star of this place. Their distinct, flavorful sauce brushed generously on the meat makes the barbecue so delicious that you can skip the usual toyo, calamansi, and suka if you want. It’s savory, slightly sweet, and smoky, and easily one of the best barbecue flavors in Cebu. Aside from grilled items, they also serve a variety of Filipino dishes. We tried their sisig, which was tasty, buttery, and satisfying, although noticeably oily but since we were craving it, we didn’t mind at all.

Prices are reasonable and fair for the quality and flavor, though portions tend to be on the smaller side; still, it’s worth it for the taste. The ordering process is simple and organized: you pick your barbecue at the counter where the uncooked selections are displayed, check their menu for other dishes, get your number stick, then head in to find a table. They’ll serve everything once ready. As far as I recall, they might have a small service charge. Best to double-check at the counter to avoid surprises.

Service is consistently good; the staff are friendly, approachable, and always smiling. Their crew also clears tables quickly and accommodates requests when needed. For payment, you can settle your bill at the cashier window or politely ask the crew for assistance.

If you want flavorful, comforting Filipino barbecue paired with a relaxed, unpretentious dining atmosphere, Ned Nanay’s Grill is definitely worth the visit. || author=Viet Huong Nguyen; rating=5; time=2025-04-29T09:46:33.696885Z; relative=12 months ago; text=Affordable and tasty place for grilled skewers (mainly chicken). They also have some other common Filipino dishes on the menu.

We had grilled skewers, veggie chop suay and halo-halo. All were good and very well-seasoned, though tbh a tad salty and sweet for our taste but thats a common Filipino food thing.

You order at the front right near the entrance, get a table and wait for your food.

Service is quick and quite friendly, tho some struggled a bit to communicate with foreign tourists. || author=Robin Culpa; rating=5; time=2025-02-15T04:57:07.782198Z; relative=a year ago; text=Went there for a dinner. I had different kinds of barbeque. I also had lomi. Price is really affordable and the taste is so delicious. It's a good alternative to enjoi having dinner with family, friends or with colleagues especially after work. There are a lot of food options for you to choose from. A lot of seafoods for grilling. The place is also well maintained. || author=Francesco Marini; rating=5; time=2023-12-14T16:12:26.855660Z; relative=2 years ago; text=Had an amazing time, the food is great and the service is excellent. Prices are very reasonable and the quantities are generous. Surrounded by locals, this is a great place to try Filipino cuisine. The atmosphere is also very friendly and the food was served fast. I definitely recommend it! || author=May N; rating=5; time=2023-03-08T01:51:51.202812Z; relative=3 years ago; text=Great bargain. Highly-recommended. Delicious local cooking, affordable with the seafood and meat options. They also have the streetfood style cooking from the familiar intestines to the usual longganisa and tocino. Dine here if you have time to wait for the dishes. Minimal service charge.  There's a takeout option if you want, most of the passerby do.

If you ask the staff, they'll take your picture when dining. There's an aircon area and an open-air area. Also has TV and karaoke. Tried their spicy grilled squid; has a chewy feel and natural sweetness to it. The rice platter has 5 cups worth of rice, good for groups. The chicken breast dish on a barbecue stick was aromatic and tasty. In the evening, the drinks are all canned or bottled - tried the bottled calamansi and it's fine for the price.
It's strategically located near most apartelles in Cebu City, it's good to visit if you want to see people, plus it's open past 8pm.

name: Casa Verde
address: 8WH4+F84 The Walk, Abad St, Apas, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Mon Ortiz; rating=5; time=2025-10-13T15:19:05.487325009Z; relative=6 months ago; text=Good food, nice restaurant ambiance, great value for money. || author=Miriam Orque; rating=5; time=2025-02-23T13:49:13.291074Z; relative=a year ago; text=Situated in Cebu IT Park there is plenty of paid parking space. Lots of public transportation as well to reach this restaurant. Food has the same taste through the years. A must visit when you are in Cebu City. || author=Geomariefer Español; rating=4; time=2024-08-29T15:53:06.483628Z; relative=a year ago; text=Food still reasonably priced. Accepts card as pyment. Steak was delicious. All time favorite ribs was excellent too. Pay parking available. Located beside Mercury Drug. Nice staff and accomodating. New green and wood interior in their extension room. You can call them for reservation. || author=Cam (黄秀贤); rating=5; time=2025-07-20T03:48:02.237880575Z; relative=9 months ago; text=Casa Verde has long held its place as one of Cebu’s most beloved homegrown dining spots, and for good reason. Known for its generous portions, comforting American-style dishes, and student-friendly prices, it continues to draw crowds of locals and tourists alike.

If you’re looking for ribs or steak that won’t break the bank, Casa Verde is one of the best bets in town. Their signature Brian’s Ribs— fall-off-the-bone pork ribs glazed in a sweet, tangy barbecue sauce— is a standout, offering incredible value for its size and flavor. It’s the kind of dish that keeps you coming back, perfect for sharing or indulging solo if you’re really hungry.

Their steaks, while not fine-dining cuts, deliver more than expected for their price range. Cooked to your preference and served with classic sides like mashed potatoes and buttered vegetables, they scratch that meaty itch without straining your wallet. Even the burgers, pastas, and desserts are consistently crowd-pleasers.

The atmosphere is casual and cozy— unpretentious but warm, with a touch of vintage Americana charm. Whether you’re dining at the original Ramos branch or one of their mall-based outlets, the experience is consistently satisfying.

All in all, Casa Verde is a Cebu staple that proves you don’t have to spend a fortune to enjoy hearty, flavorful food. It hits the sweet spot between affordability, portion size, and taste— a rare combination that continues to make it a favorite among families, students, and barkadas. || author=Cheng Ho; rating=3; time=2018-07-04T08:52:20.641Z; relative=7 years ago; text=The place is still affordable for its type of food and generous servings.

We were disappointed as the Brian's ribs were no longer as delicious as before. It tasted bland, rubbery and just simply lacking.

The servings were no longer as generous too.

The death by chocolate cake is still as yummy as ever though. Smaller in size too. :'(

My friends and I went there just to enjoy a meal together. When we arrived, the staff were welcoming and the vibe seemed nice at first. However, while we were eating, we noticed that some of the crew members were constantly looking at our table.

One of my friends was wearing a sleeveless top, and several male crew members were openly staring at her. It wasn’t subtle at all. One crew member even called other staff from the kitchen to come over and look at our table. That made us feel extremely uncomfortable and disrespected.

When I reacted because of how obvious they were being, we overheard one of them say, “Awa sukoa niya oy,” as if our discomfort was something to joke about. There was even a moment when a female crew member covered another crew member’s eyes because he was staring too much, and they were just laughing about it.

We are teenagers and students. We came there to eat and enjoy our time, not to be stared at and made uncomfortable by the staff. No customer, especially young students, should feel that way in a restaurant.

I hope management takes this seriously and reminds their staff to act professionally and treat all customers with respect. || author=tagrani; rating=3; time=2026-03-07T03:15:29.218217941Z; relative=a month ago; text=My boyfriend and I really like ribshack but this branch was quite slow. name: Kanto Street Ramen
types: ramen_restaurant, japanese_restaurant, restaurant, food, point_of_interest, establishment
address: Don Jose Avila St, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Lean Gomez; rating=3; time=2025-10-27T05:23:23.653995485Z; relative=6 months ago; text=Kanto Street Ramen is undeniably popular. Every time I visit, whether on a weekday or weekend, it’s always full. Expect a short wait before being seated, and if you’re in a group, it may take longer since tables are limited. Sometimes, you even have to share tables with strangers due to the high volume of customers. The setup is completely open-air and located by the roadside, so there’s no proper cover. If it rains, some tables and chairs can get wet, and since it’s outdoors, it’s normal to encounter bugs or even the occasional cockroach on the floor. Electric fans are installed, but they aren’t strong enough to keep the area cool, especially on humid nights. Bringing your own handy fan is a good idea.

The ordering system is straightforward: you pay at the counter, get an order number, find a seat, and wait for your number to be called to claim your food at the stall. Self-service is expected due to the limited crew. On our visit, there was a Kuya assigned to handle the seating queue and clean the tables, which helped maintain some order despite the busy environment.

In terms of food, the ramen is decent for its price. The broth is flavorful enough and the serving size is just right for budget ramen. Toppings are minimal, but acceptable given the cost. However, not everything hit the mark. The gyoza was a letdown as the bottom was more burned than the usual while the top and filling were still cold, which affected the overall taste.

Overall, Kanto Street Ramen offers an affordable ramen fix for those who don’t mind the street-style dining experience, the crowds, and a bit of discomfort. If you’re after ambiance or consistent food quality, this might not be for you but if you’re looking for budget-friendly ramen and are willing to compromise on comfort, it’s worth a try. || author=Aaron Nuñez; rating=5; time=2025-07-13T12:29:54.395233810Z; relative=9 months ago; text=Kanto Ramen is the kind of place that doesn’t try too hard—and that’s exactly its charm. It’s low key, unpretentious, and quietly comforting, like a warm bowl on a weary Sunday.

This isn’t your high-end, Tokyo-level ramen. It’s the kind that sits by the roadside, made with local ingredients but still manages to capture that nostalgic Japanese flavor. For the price, it’s surprisingly satisfying—a budget-friendly indulgence that warms both heart and wallet.

Perfect for lazy weekends or late-night cravings, Kanto Ramen is proof that good food doesn’t have to be fancy. Just honest, flavorful, and made for comfort. || author=Elly Lunéva; rating=5; time=2025-04-03T01:05:41.784608Z; relative=a year ago; text=One of the best ramen spots I’ve tried — flavorful, rich, and very affordable.

The place is usually full after 6 PM, and wait times can reach 30–40 minutes, but it’s worth it.
Lots of locals eat here, which is always a good sign. Just a heads-up: they’re closed on Mondays — but with that kind of daily crowd, it makes sense they need a break.

Pro tip: come by 5-5:30 PM to avoid the rush. || author=Cam (黄秀贤); rating=5; time=2025-07-18T12:08:52.670249260Z; relative=9 months ago; text=In the ever-evolving Cebu food scene, where new restaurants constantly vie for attention, Kanto Ramen Cebu has quietly— and deservedly— earned a loyal following. It may not boast sleek interiors or high-end presentation, but what it offers is something far more memorable: flavorful, soulful ramen that hits the spot without breaking the bank.

Kanto Ramen is a sidewalk-style ramen joint that embraces its unpretentious charm. Humble, no-frills, and comfortingly local in its setting, it’s the kind of place you find yourself returning to not just for the food, but for the familiarity and warmth it brings. Among all its offerings, the Tonkotsu Shoyu Ramen stands out as a personal favorite— and in my opinion, one of the best in Cebu within its price range. The broth is rich and deeply savory, with a well-balanced shoyu base that complements the creamy depth of the tonkotsu. It’s a bowl that feels carefully made, without the fanfare.

Every slurp feels indulgent, yet never heavy. The noodles are firm, the chashu tender, and the soft-boiled egg is done just right. It’s the kind of ramen that doesn’t pretend to be more than it is— affordable, honest, and deeply satisfying.

Kanto Ramen Cebu has carved out a snug little place in my heart. It has become my go-to whenever I crave something hearty, familiar, and affordable. In a city where prices continue to rise and quality sometimes falls short, Kanto Ramen remains refreshingly consistent. It may not come with the bells and whistles of upscale ramen houses, but for what it is—and at the price it comes— it’s simply unbeatable.

Whether you’re a student on a budget, a late-night wanderer, or a ramen lover who values flavor over flair, Kanto Ramen Cebu is one place that absolutely deserves a spot on your list. || author=TheTraveler; rating=5; time=2025-06-03T11:22:58.235989Z; relative=10 months ago; text=Food served hot and prepared in front of you, waited approximately 10-15 minutes, longer queue if you came in during rush hours (around 6/7 pm) I accidentally spilled my food (my fault)/and staff were kind enough to assist me right away, they also offered to pour soup again 😅. Friendly and approachable staffs. Music in background (not too loud, nice). I have tried lots of ramen and this one is on top. I order tonkotsu shoyo ramen ₱159.00 (not sure for the spelling) its creamy it looks exactly with whats advertised. i recommend this shop 🙂

sample_reviews:
author=Jack Manlosa; rating=3; time=2026-02-27T12:54:13.648321246Z; relative=a month ago; text=Orange Karenderia offers a pleasant dining experience with a clean, cozy ambiance that makes it comfortable for casual meals inside the mall. The place has a modern take on a traditional Filipino eatery, making it inviting for both families and solo diners looking for familiar comfort food in a nicer setting.

Food-wise, the dishes are a mix of hits and misses. The grilled tuna, while flavorful, comes in surprisingly small portions considering the price, which may leave some diners wanting more. On the brighter side, the vegetable dishes stand out — the talong (eggplant) and other gulay options are well-seasoned, fresh, and genuinely satisfying. These are easily some of the best items on the menu.

The calamari and kawali are enjoyable and cooked properly, with good texture and taste, though they don’t particularly stand out compared to similar dishes elsewhere. They’re solid choices but not memorable.

Service is decent, and the overall atmosphere is relaxed, making it a comfortable spot to sit down and enjoy a meal without feeling rushed.

Overall, Orange Karenderia is a good option if you’re craving Filipino comfort food in a pleasant setting, especially for vegetable lovers. However, portion sizes for some dishes — particularly the tuna — could be improved to better match expectations. || author=Leslie Diaz; rating=5; time=2024-11-20T08:57:00.889402Z; relative=a year ago; text=Located in SM City Cebu, Orange Karenderia serves delicious local food. name: Amaretto
address: Mantawe Ave, Mandaue, 6014 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Robbie Ruby; rating=4; time=2026-03-04T00:41:01.051743812Z; relative=a month ago; text=For those seeking an elevated yet relaxed dining experience, Amaretto offers the perfect balance of elegance and comfort. This standout spot in Mandaue redefines casual dining with its seamless fusion of Asian and Western flavor. Each plate is beautifully presented and carefully prepared, highlighting quality ingredients and balanced flavors. The crab panipuri and kinilaw in mango and pomelo were so refreshing and delicious!

What truly makes Amaretto shine is its attentive service. The staff is warm, professional, and responsive, ensuring guests feel well taken care of throughout the dining experience. Despite the elegant ambiance and high-quality offerings, the pricing remains decent and not too pricey, making it accessible for casual nights out as well as special occasions. || author=Taylor Campbell; rating=4; time=2025-11-17T04:05:09.577806068Z; relative=5 months ago; text=Had bruschetta, sous vide pork belly, sea bass kinilaw, tiramisu + signature amaretto cocktail and watermelon smash punch. Bruschetta was 10/10. Pork belly was 7.5/10, - very tender but flavor is lacking. It needs some sort of acid to bring out the flavor and balance it. Sea bass kinelaw was 9/10. It was a little too spicy (and I like spicy) and if they had served it with tortilla chips, the salt and the crunch would have put the whole dish over the top. Amaretto cocktail had espresso, it was very smooth and well balanced. I'm not a big coffee drinker so I probably wouldn't order it again, but if you love coffee you'll likely love it. Watermelon smash punch was delicious and refreshing. Tiramisu was unfortunately not good, maybe 5/10. Wife and I both love tiramisu but this was not it. 🙁

We'll be back for sure, but hopefully management makes some improvements to the menu before then. Lastly service and atmosphere were both great! || author=Kie Jones; rating=5; time=2025-11-11T09:01:46.895974535Z; relative=5 months ago; text=i really love the place and the food here. everything felt cozy and well-prepared. we started with bruschetta, which had crispy toasted bread topped with fresh tomatoes, herbs, and olive oil. it was light, fresh, and a perfect way to start the meal.

for the main dishes, we ordered the nested char siu chicken and beef raguot. the char siu chicken was tender and flavorful with that sweet and savory glaze, while the beef raguot was rich and comforting, like something slow-cooked with care.

we also had bak kut teh soup, which had tender pork ribs and a warm, herby broth that felt really soothing. the nduja pasta was creamy with just the right level of spice that made it stand out.

for dessert, we ended with tiramisu, which was soft, creamy, and had a nice balance of coffee and sweetness. for drinks, we had lemongrass punch and blue lemonade — both were refreshing and went perfectly with the food.

the staff were very friendly and approachable. they were attentive the whole time, always checking if we needed anything. you won’t feel intimidated at all because they make the place feel warm and welcoming.

we actually expected the bill to be higher since we ordered a lot of dishes for just two people, but the price was very reasonable. if you plan to go, make sure to reserve a table to avoid any waiting, especially during dinner time. || author=Kawin Cheong; rating=2; time=2026-01-03T02:01:25.104366731Z; relative=3 months ago; text=The quality of the food doesn’t justify the price. You could get better tasting food for the same or even a much lower price. I must admit that their presentation is good. Also, sanitary issues. I found a pest on my meal and they added it to my bill. Only gave me a tiramisu as compensation (not even that good). || author=Sole Wanderer; rating=1; time=2026-02-18T02:18:39.660642829Z; relative=2 months ago; text=I walked in alone today. The waiter welcomed me, then immediately whispered to the “manager.” Moments later, I was told they were fully booked — even though I could clearly see empty tables everywhere, including the bar. No signs outside said walk-ins weren’t welcome. It didn’t feel like bad timing. It felt personal. Was this discrimination?

Edit: Yes, it was Valentine’s Day — but I wasn’t even offered the option to wait while customers were literally wrapping up their meals. Fully booked… or just selective? The instant “thank you” after turning me away said more than enough. I genuinely hope the staff never has to experience this kind of treatment, whether here or in another country.

Customer tip: Dress like your meal budget starts at ₱5,000.

sample_reviews:
author=Jolie Ann Cartuciano; rating=5; time=2025-10-30T08:40:39.272572263Z; relative=5 months ago; text=I never expect the place to be cozy and elegant. Great for family gatherings and meetups. We got the room for 50 pax. Food was surprisingly super tasty. The package included a 4-hour use of the function room, a sound system, a projector (so you can have karaoke or play videos), and elegant table setting and decors. The staff was super kind! Really don't have anything to complain about. Will definitely recommend to our friends :) || author=Jill Travenner; rating=5; time=2025-06-10T18:20:22.807633467Z; relative=10 months ago; text=We had such an amazing time celebrating Mommy Luz’s birthday at Mabolo Gallery in Envy of Garden! 🎉 It was a night filled with laughter, great food, and unforgettable memories with friends.

The venue was just perfect, ideal for small to medium gatherings as it can comfortably accommodate around 20 to 30 guests Whether it's a birthday, an intimate get-together, or even a small corporate event, this place has the right balance of elegance and comfort.

One of the standout features of the night was the Galaxy-themed lighting setup. It gave the entire space a soft, mystical glow that added a demure and dreamy ambiance. The lights made the whole place feel extra special, it was like celebrating under the stars!

We couldn’t resist taking photos all around the venue. From the beautiful indoor setup to the charming outdoor area , everything was picture-perfect. The lighting both inside and outside created the perfect backdrop for photos that we’ll surely treasure for a long time.

Another great thing about this place is that they also have a restaurant on-site. making it convenient for events with food and drinks included. The staff were welcoming and attentive, ensuring everyone was comfortable and enjoying the celebration.

Overall, Mabolo Gallery at Envy of Garden is truly a hidden gem — perfect for those looking for a stylish and versatile event space. We had so much fun celebrating and creating memories with friends, and we wouldn’t hesitate to book this venue again in the future.

Thet offer a free wifi for the guests.

Highly recommended! 🌟 || author=Julienne; rating=5; time=2025-03-25T09:30:48.097298Z; relative=a year ago; text=We found Garden of Envy by chance and we were so happy we did! We were only there for dinner but our waiter for the evening let us know about their buy 1 take 1 cocktail deal that was on at the time, which was fantastic. All of their cocktails that we had were great! We ended up staying for a long time because we were enjoying the drinks a lot. The bartender at the time, Jun, told us about his specials, which included a clarified cocktail with mango and an infused cocktail - can’t remember what else was in them, but they were both fantastic and the presentation was awesome!

The food was fantastic too, it looked so good we forgot to take photos, but we had the lumpiang shanghai, lechon kawali and chicken with mash and mushroom gravy. Would recommend all of them. We were sad that we found the restaurant on our last day in Cebu, but we’ll be definitely coming back when we are in Cebu again! Thank you again to Sir Jonel our waiter and Sir Jun for making our experience great. || author=Vienna Rose Jumao-as Cabantan; rating=5; time=2026-01-29T03:17:15.266448018Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Hello!!! name: La Buona Italian Restaurant
address: Advent Business Center, 139 Acacia St, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Les Jaco; rating=5; time=2026-02-16T07:24:36.167465166Z; relative=2 months ago; text=It was my first time dining in a fancy Italian spot, La Buona.

The place felt really intimate. It’s tucked inside a compound with a few old houses around pretty sure it used to be an ancestral home that got turned into a restaurant. The vibe was cozy, super comfortable to dine in, and very cold (in a good way). The staff were chill and very accommodating.

Prices were pretty reasonable. We had truffle pasta, which was cooked perfectly, just how I like it. I’d been craving it for a while! We ordered scallops, pizza, truffle pasta, tiramisu to name a few.

Would I recommend it? Definitely. Will I come back? Of course! || author=Rae Pueblos; rating=5; time=2024-06-15T08:12:29.178203Z; relative=a year ago; text=Nice place. I kept passing by the area but didn't know there was a really good authentic Italian restaurant nearby. Everything was so good, from food to drinks. The staff were also so attentive and friendly. Good food, good service, good time. All the food we had was amazing, but I personally love the carbonara the most! The prices are a little on the expensive side, but it's definitely worth the price. || author=Alan Fu; rating=5; time=2023-05-26T11:46:11.634022Z; relative=2 years ago; text=This is the most authentic Italian Restaurant in Cebu. Their Tiramisu with Ice Cream should be the best I tasted in Philippines. They do not use cheap lady fingers so the texture and the bite is full!

Another thing I enjoyed is the clams, it was super fresh and thought it was oily but actually it was the juices of the clam, they use good olive oil.

Met the owner and the son too, drank limoncello together, will def be back! || author=KC; rating=5; time=2024-05-13T03:28:08.384128Z; relative=a year ago; text=There is no other AUTHENTIC ITALIAN restaurant in Cebu! This is the best!!!

Good customer service
Their waitress confirmed with our orders properly
Quite pricey but taste is AMAZING! We literally wiped out all our orders.
We got 2 pizzas, 3 kinds of pasta, 1 baked scallop and 2 tiramisu (to die for!!!)

They also gave us complimentary sweets for Mothers Day.

Highly recommend! Definitely coming back for more! 😋 || author=CM A.; rating=5; time=2020-11-19T01:58:53.897608Z; relative=5 years ago; text=This place is a hidden gem in Cebu that people don't know of. The servings are quite big which is perfect for sharing for 2-3 people. I highly recommend ordering the scallops for appetizer, pork ribs for main course and the gelato cake for dessert. You will not regret! 😋

Ala carte options are also available though. If there's anything that truly elevates Blu Bar and Grill it's their impeccable service. We recieved absolutely warm and topnotch experience from their staff which was the real highlight from our dinner.

Aside from the stunning view of the Cebu skyline, the was equally great particularly the desserts. Their signature Tiramisu was notorious for being the best and I will agree that this is the best Tiramisu in Cebu. Mains were okay, they were not as memorable but still great. The ribs were more on the bony side but was really tasty. I also really liked their bread and the seared Tuna was really enjoyable.

Blu Bar is a perfect choice if you're craving for a great meal with a stunning view and superb service. || author=Matt Bailey; rating=2; time=2026-02-17T03:00:08.859874372Z; relative=2 months ago; text=First the food was great

So I booked for valentines, and payed in advance at there request a total of 9976 peso for a 5 course meal with wine pairings. First we had to wait an hour for the first course, we were brought drinks menu's during this time, but no one stayed to take the order and you couldn't get anyone's attention, the one time someone did come she directed us to let one of the others members of staff know. Second when we finally got our food, we didn't receive our wine pairing after me telling them twice that we had paid for wine pairing so I had to chase what I paid for, even then we still didn't get our last wine pairing during desert, at which point, I gave up with chasing. Then as we were leaving, we were asked if we had paid, annoying since by this point I had said so 3 times, plus they should have record of it. At this point I showed them the email between me and them with proof of payment. It annoyed me to be questioned about payment when hadn't provided everything I paid for. Honestly they are disorganised and caused stress on what was supposed to be a romantic dinner with my girlfriend. Like I said the food was the only good part

name: Dungawan Garden Restaurant

sample_reviews:
author=Sean Martin Sanchez; rating=4; time=2026-01-13T17:20:26.117084908Z; relative=3 months ago; text=If you are looking for an overlooking restaurant near Tisa in Labangon, this is one of the good place to dine and hangout.

The manager/supervisor seemed cranky.  Hmmmm.  No service water.  Ordered food and it arrived reasonably on time, however, there was not enough space on the table and the waiter was not helpful in arranging the food and when we asked if he could get another table, he said there was none available.  Mind you, the said party of 20 did not arrive yet.  Even before we left, they did not arrive.  Very disappointing service.  The view was marvelous but will not come back here again.  Service was really bad and food was so-so.  Oh and they have a service charge which was not indicated in the menu. || author=Gayle Guy; rating=1; time=2026-02-16T17:05:10.403294923Z; relative=2 months ago; text=We chose this restaurant to celebrate my son’s birthday on a weekday, and there were only a few diners at the time. Unfortunately, when we tried to order, many items on the menu were unavailable, so we had to select alternatives.

We were informed that the food would take 30–45 minutes to prepare, which we initially understood since we were enjoying the scenery. However, the main issue was that the dishes were served one at a time, with roughly 30 minutes between each plate. We arrived at 6:00 PM, but the last dish wasn’t served until 10:00 PM.

What was meant to be a joyful birthday celebration unfortunately turned into a very disappointing and frustrating experience.

They even charge a service charge wherein we were not happy with their service. I wont recommend this restaurant especially if you are hungry. Lol || author=Andreah Alunan; rating=1; time=2026-02-14T14:28:21.431528281Z; relative=2 months ago; text=We dined here on Valentine’s Day, and unfortunately, the experience didn’t go as smoothly as we hoped. Everything started off okay since we arrived at 5:30PM earlier that everyone else, but the wait for our food took about 2 hours (Served around 7:30PM) To be fair, it was Valentine’s Day, so we expected some delays—but that still felt too long.

What made it more frustrating was the billing process. We had to line up just like everyone else, and the reception area was extremely crowded. People waiting for tables were mixed in with those trying to settle their bills, which made the space chaotic and uncomfortable.

We also noticed that the manager wasn’t around during the rush, and only the cashier was handling concerns. It felt like there was a lack of proper management and coordination. At one point, a waiter was called, but she didn’t seem to respond at all. We also overheard someone walk in asking about a reservation and being assured there was a table ready without worry, which made it seem like there were favorites or inconsistent handling of guests.

Overall, it was disappointing, especially for a special occasion like Valentine’s Day. We understand it was a busy night, but better crowd control, communication, and management presence would have made a big difference. || author=jaimy smith; rating=1; time=2026-01-03T09:28:43.082497384Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Absolutely terrible experience.

We waited 1 full hour for the food and there was zero customer service. Even ice took 20 minutes to arrive to completely unacceptable.

We clearly instructed the server to serve everything together, but they ignored us. Every dish came out one by one with long gaps, ruining the entire meal.

To make it worse, the pasta was undercooked, and the bread was burned. Honestly, I can’t believe food was served like this. No apology, no explanation, no sense of urgency from the staff.

This place wasted our time, money, and patience. Never coming back. Avoid this restaurant.

Didn’t really feel like eating but loved their steak and grazing board bets paired with wine that was recommended as well. The restaurant has an open layout where the dining area is centrally positioned, with clear visibility of both the bar and the cooking station. While seated, I could clearly see staff in these areas, and I became increasingly uncomfortable due to the staring and visible reactions coming from behind the counter and kitchen area.

At one point, there was noticeable laughter and loud noises from the cooking station while staff were looking in my direction. Whether intentional or not, the behavior felt unprofessional and unwelcoming. As a guest, I expect to feel at ease while dining, but instead I felt watched and singled out.

I hope management reflects on this feedback and ensures that all guests are treated with professionalism and respect, regardless of who they are. || author=Khrys Burden; rating=5; time=2025-08-31T11:13:32.064549139Z; relative=7 months ago; text=This is me and my dad's second time here and we enjoyed our experience.

We ordered a Caesar Salad, Spaghetti Carbonara, and Dry Aged Ribeye. Everything was excellent and the steak especially was perfectly cooked (we had it medium-rare) and seasoned well. We also had a beer and a classic mojito (which is not on the menu but you can ask the bartender to whip up for you).

As for the ambience, we were pleasantly surprised at how bright the restaurant is now. On our first visit, it seemed quite dim to the point we had difficulty reading the menu.

What stood out for us was the impeccable service from our waiter, Oliver. He accomodated our requests and explained everything in a concise manner. He was also quite attentive and pleasant.

One more thing to note, we were informed that since it is the end of the month, they had no stock of some of their cuts of steak. Oh, and we also took some food home so it was like we spent for 3 meals.

sample_reviews:
author=Katii Ann; rating=5; time=2026-01-08T10:05:56.314853481Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Woods Smokehouse is one of those easygoing spots that just feels right the moment you settle in great location, a solid city view, and a relaxed vibe that makes you want to hang out a little longer. The ambiance is comfortably chill without trying too hard, and while the food is definitely on the expensive side, it's honestly worth it for the quality and flavor. One of the most charming surprises was a lovely pregnant cat casually hanging around inside, which somehow made the place feel even more welcoming. The view is already nice, but you can tell it would be especially beautiful in the morning when the city wakes up. Overall, it's a laidback bar and grill with good food, good energy, and just enough character to make it memorable. || author=Kyle Matthew; rating=4; time=2026-02-21T00:04:49.002486508Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Other than the rain while dining on their overhead patio, it's perfectly okay. Servers were attentive, food was great, but no mitigation efforts in case it rains. Parasol or a retractable roof maybe? || author=leah Montermoso; rating=4; time=2023-02-07T21:17:43.649022Z; relative=3 years ago; text=This is my first time to dine here and here are my thoughts about the place. As far as the place goes, I love how accessible it is, its just along the road to your way to Busay. Its not to far from JY Square though. You can easily see the place because of their big Carved Cement Wall Signage (you can't miss it). When you get inside the interior is not that amazing as there some fixing on the furnitures that needs to be done they said it's because of the typhoon Odet but despite all these its still a good place to relax your mind with a very nice view of the city as it has an overlooking view. The place is not too big just enough to accommodate a number of people which for me is wonderful because it kinda keeps the intimacy ambiance of the place. For the food, I'm having mixed emotions although we only ordered 4 dishes. I love the smoked bacon, it is very flavorful. I also liked the sizzling pork belly with the ginger-garlic sauce. The Chicken that we got I believe is overwhelming because of the Spices but its still okay. I don't like their Carbonara that much as its not that creamy and the sauce has the tendency to harden if you don't eat it right away. The staffs are the best. We are assisted by two of their staff and both of them are so accommodating. They answered all our inquiries. They are very patient and I love how they always put a smile on their face. Parking do not worry they have a parking area but don't be too hype about it coz its not that big remember that they are located along the road but yes they do have a parking. So this is my review about the place, I may not have given this place a 5 star but I still recommend for you to try it. God bless everyone, enjoy travels. || author=William Dorrington; rating=5; time=2025-11-20T14:19:21.666251602Z; relative=5 months ago; text=A phenomenal find that we only discovered when driving by. Beautiful beers, beautiful views, fantastic service. Would recommend to any locals or travellers! || author=jayjesse02; rating=5; time=2019-01-28T09:46:14.367730326Z; relative=7 years ago; text=The restobar overlooks the city. The food was DELICIOUS. I recommend the smoked bacon. The bacon is paired with some toasted bread slices. We ordered rice just because. :p If you fancy pasta - hot and really spicy pasta - choose the spicy grilled sausage.

Customer service was also good.

Additional information:
- Car park
- Restrooms
