They serve some delicious bread and butter while preparing the food. We ordered starters and they all tased very well. Afterward we ate main courses and they were also good. There was no room for desert,  after eating all the dishes. We were really full!

They have a good selection of wine, so we ordered a bottle.

Staff and service was all really good. || author=Georg Schulze; rating=5; time=2025-08-07T12:45:08.454800175Z; relative=8 months ago; text=Just perfect!!!
We came for the Expo2025 and found this tiny place near to our hotel.
8 seats, live cooking, 15 courses for only 300€ for two including two bottles of great wine!
They got a Michelin star this year - unbelievable great food! || author=Glen C; rating=5; time=2026-01-09T15:01:17.125337288Z; relative=3 months ago; text=La felicidad es buena comida.

Enye stands as the epitome of gustatory excellence as one of Cebu’s Michelin-recognized dining destinations. Where every dish is a celebration. Curated by Chef Chele González, the menu delivers a true fiesta in every bite, blending Spanish mastery with local soul.
We sampled the dry-aged Lapu-lapu, a fish native to Cebu, thoughtfully prepared in two distinct styles:
Pescado a la Espalda showcased the fish grilled to perfection, highlighting its natural sweetness with crisp skin and delicate, flaky flesh. In contrast, Pescado en Adobo offered deeper, more complex flavours, with the fish gently infused in a rich, aromatic adobo sauce that paid homage to classic Spanish technique.
We also indulged in two paellas that beautifully captured Enye’s range and finesse.
The Paella Marisco del Senyoret was a refined seafood paella, generously layered with prawns, squid, and shellfish, all delicately peeled for effortless enjoyment, allowing the saffron-infused rice to shine. Meanwhile, the Paella Negra delivered bold intensity, its inky black rice enriched with cuttlefish ink, offering a briny depth balanced by subtle smokiness.

Much like a traditional fiesta, the spread was impeccably paired with St Hallett’s Cabernet Sauvignon from South Australia, its structured tannins and dark fruit notes complementing the robust flavours of the meal.
Dessert was a fitting finale: the indulgent Burnt Basque Cheesecake, rich and caramelised, alongside churros served with a togarashi-spiced chocolate sauce, adding a playful heat to the familiar sweetness.
At Enye, dining is not merely a meal,it is a celebration of flavour, culture, and craftsmanship.

author=Armando Salayo Jr (Manman); rating=4; time=2025-12-06T02:03:07.481008713Z; relative=4 months ago; text=I was expecting for a wow experience as I’ve read online that this place serves really good food. It was alright. Ok service and ok place to eat quick.

However, I was concerned to see that the food selection counter doesn't have covers, which feels unhygienic for a self-service setup. Unfortunately, both my friend and I ended up with an upset stomach later that evening, which makes me worry about their food safety standards. While the concept is great, I really hope they consider adding covers to the ingredients to ensure everything stays fresh and clean for the customers. || author=Jangmi; rating=4; time=2025-12-09T14:39:43.579095966Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Food is good, warm and comforting. I ordered their shao mai rice bowl, which had tasty, meaty siomai and a well-seasoned flavor. With the egg, it was filling, and the free soup made it worth the price. I also tried the wonton soup, which was good as well. It has the same white-based broth; the wontons were meaty and complemented the soup nicely. There were a lot of them, maybe 8–10 pieces, so it’s good for sharing.
I also tried the malatang. They use the same base soup, with the other versions just being spicy. The selection of veggies and meat for the hotpot was okay—mid, but decent. Their pig trotters rice bowl is also good. The portion matches the price, and flavor-wise, it wasn’t too sweet (I personally would’ve liked a bit more sweetness). Their meat buns are good too, the bun is crispy outside and soft inside, and the meat filling goes well with it.
They definitely have a lot of options. If you’re craving Chinese food, this comes pretty close. || author=Raven; rating=5; time=2025-12-02T05:17:32.828146048Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Great food in Cebu, in particular if you you’re not used to non-western food. Very yummy combination of skewers and a mini hot pot. || author=Lilan Seña; rating=1; time=2026-03-09T05:11:38.023073431Z; relative=a month ago; text=I loved their szichuan malatang so much that I didn't mind the changes they made in their pricing compared to when they started (based on weight instead of bowl size), the slight changes in the toppings (sorry, but the removal of the coriander affected the overall flavor for me), nor the rude behavior of their staff. But last night was the last straw. Nothing big, but it made me decide that dining there is no longer worth it. 1) the szichuan soup base was completely different. I think there was less garlic and less spice, or maybe they changed the recipe entirely. It's not the same flavor that I loved. 2) their dry szichuan malatang was horrible. It looked so unappetizing and flavor was not on point as well. 3) staff are just unprofessional. I like going late at night when it's cooler and there's less people. I keep in mind that 12.30am is their last call, and 1am is their closing time when i come over. Yet, every single time, they make us feel rushed. Last night, we arrived quarter to 12am, then got our food around 12.10am. At that point, the cashier was already putting the chairs on top of the available tables. A few minutes later, a guy was mopping the floor behind us and blocking the way so we have to go around for the door when we go out. We finished our meal around 12.30am, which is supposed to be their last call. I, like any filipino worker, depise having to extend for unpaid hours. But this is too much. Last call is not closing hours. Sweeping and/or mopping around the customers is rude (unless someone spilled something). What happened to basic customer service? I won't be coming back anymore.

name: Yappari Steak Okinawa Steak House

sample_reviews:
author=Geoff Sese; rating=5; time=2025-11-26T13:19:50.200559581Z; relative=5 months ago; text=First time trying out this steak house, going in with no expectations at all but I'd say that it left a good impression to me.

Food is great, I do like how the steak are cooked and is still sizzling hot when served. The hot plate was still sizzling even up to my last bite of the steak. It was juicy, not that too hard nor too soft, just enough for the bite and to slice. Although it took quite some time for the ordered to be served, they did gave a egg soup of some kind to dig in while waiting and the taste was alright.

Drinks were good and presented well, first time drinking this kind of juice and pretty nice that it is a local brand and tasted really great.

I've checked and saw that there are already branches in Manila so I might try for another bite back home! || author=Chipper Chihuahua; rating=4; time=2026-01-22T15:09:53.782654718Z; relative=3 months ago; text=ordering is done at the counter and payment is upfront. the steak is uniquely served raw and on hot lava plates so you can cook it however you like. i suggest you ask for an extra plate to rest your steak on if you don’t want it well done. i wish you could choose to have only one type of sauce, maybe i’ll try that next time. not much exhaust, it can get smokey when there’s too many people dining at the same time. || author=Kleinberg Fletcher Tabel; rating=5; time=2025-10-04T17:44:37.866006352Z; relative=6 months ago; text=Hailing all the way from Okinawa, Yappari Steak brings a truly authentic Japanese steakhouse experience right to Cebu. What started as a humble concept from Japan has now become a dining sensation across the city — with branches at Tops, SM JMall (appropriately called “the Japanese Mall”), and the ever-bustling IT Park.

The star of the show? The Yappari Steak, grilled not on your usual flat-top, but on lava stones sourced from Mount Fuji itself. It’s a bold promise — and the experience truly delivers. The steak arrives sizzling, raw on the edges, inviting you to cut it into bite-sized pieces and grill it to your own liking. The moment the meat hits the hot stone, you hear that satisfying sizzle and smell the rich aroma of premium Australian beef — tender, juicy, and full of flavor.

You get to choose from three sauces: garlic soy butter, spicy yakiniku, and pepper steak sauce, plus a dab of wasabi for that authentic Japanese kick. Whether you dip or pour, each bite bursts with umami. The set comes with rice and soup — simple, balanced, and incredibly satisfying.

The staff could be a little warmer, but service is efficient. The interiors are casual yet cozy, making it feel like a piece of Japan tucked within Cebu’s skyline. Prices vary depending on the cut and portion, but for the quality, it’s worth every peso.

If you want to experience the art of steak, Japanese-style, this is one for the books. Yappari Steak isn’t just a meal — it’s a performance on a plate. || author=Jennifer Wallen; rating=5; time=2026-03-09T07:00:37.824623759Z; relative=a month ago; text=The beef steak and chicken steak were delicious. We’ve been several times over the last few weeks and love it. Served on sizzling slate. Love the garbage salad and macaroni salad. Staff is always friendly. If you like red wine, they sell it by the glass for only 150 pesos! || author=Aileen; rating=5; time=2026-01-20T08:39:20.722751538Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Yappari Steak serves soft, well-cooked meat that’s flavorful and satisfying. The sauces complement the steak perfectly, enhancing the taste without overpowering it. Portions are just right for the price—enough to leave you full and happy without feeling too heavy. Overall, it’s a great spot for a quality steak meal that delivers good value for money.

name: Mendokoro Ramenba Cebu
address: F&B 12 Garden Row, Ayala Malls Central Block IT Park Lahug Ayala Malls Central Block IT Park, Lahug, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Jose; rating=5; time=2026-03-06T17:12:36.013872362Z; relative=a month ago; text=I’ve had lunch and dinner at this place a couple of times, and as always, the taste is good. Even though the price is a bit high, it still feels reasonable for the quality. The staff are friendly and professional. They even provide paper napkins to protect your clothes from soup splashes, which is a thoughtful touch. So far, I’d highly recommend the Tsukemen, and of course the ramen. I can’t wait to visit again soon. || author=MyLifeWithTheSun; rating=5; time=2025-10-15T09:33:53.866511058Z; relative=6 months ago; text=My go-to ramen spot in Cebu! Every visit to Mendokoro Ramenba feels like a treat — from the savory aroma that greets you to the cozy ramen bar setup where you can watch the chefs at work.

My favorite is the Shoyu Ramen with extra egg. The broth is rich, flavorful, and perfectly balanced with that deep umami taste that lingers in the best way. The noodles have just the right bite, and the chashu practically melts in your mouth. The soft-boiled egg is always cooked perfectly — creamy yolk and all. I can usually only finish half, so it’s best shared with someone. That said, I can see how some might find it a bit overwhelming — too creamy or too fatty for their taste, especially if you prefer lighter ramen styles like shio. Personally, I love it, but it’s definitely on the indulgent side.

The dumplings (gyoza) are great too — crisp on one side, soft on the other, with a juicy, flavorful filling. The serving of five is generous; three or four pieces would be just right for me.

The ambiance deserves a mention as well. It’s got that minimalist Japanese ramen-bar charm — warm lighting, clean lines, and the lively sound of ramen being prepared right in front of you. The staff are friendly, service is fast, and everything feels efficiently run. It can get busy during peak hours, but the overall experience is consistently good.

Many other diners share the same sentiments — praising the rich broth, excellent service, and authentic taste. Some find the ramen a little pricey or heavy, but most agree it’s worth it for the quality and flavor.

Overall, Mendokoro Ramenba Cebu is one of the best ramen spots in the city. Come hungry, or better yet, come with a friend to share — because once you taste that Shoyu Ramen, you’ll definitely want to come back for more. || author=sziang sziang; rating=4; time=2026-01-03T13:40:22.220582263Z; relative=3 months ago; text=The miso ramen broth is very thick. It feels like eating the taiwanese pork rice sauce with thick sauce pork and thick sauce ramen. You'll need to pay first at the counter with menu on the wall. The attendant will keep up selling you add on. They will automatically serve you ice water when you are seated so not necessary to order drinks. All the seating if I remembered correctly is bar counter with bag hooks below the table. The setup is cater for quick lunch crowds. Two tonkatsu miso ramen added egg with soda drinks comes up to 1.6k php. || author=Umi Umi; rating=5; time=2025-10-26T06:00:45.971052859Z; relative=6 months ago; text=I ordered the lechon ramen which is off-menu, it was really good. The broth was thick, it was served with a little fresh sauce on the side for the lechon.

Also tried the Hiyashi Chuka. Well served, super nice. Noodle a little thick. || author=Jean Hun; rating=5; time=2025-12-06T04:07:24.258788661Z; relative=4 months ago; text=If there’s one thing I’ve learned from dining around Cebu, it’s that restaurant ratings can be wildly misleading. Having eaten at multiple Michelin-starred establishments, I’d like to think I have a decent benchmark for quality — and this ramen spot truly surprised me.

The broth is exceptional: rich, aromatic, and perfectly balanced without being overly salty, allowing the umami to shine through. The noodles are just the right thickness and cooked to that ideal, satisfying chew. Even the bamboo shoots stand out — thinly sliced, free of any unpleasant odor, and complementary to the overall flavour profile.

A genuinely impressive bowl of ramen, and easily one of the best I’ve had in Cebu.

It is a little pricier compared to other wing joints in the city, but you can tell the difference in the preparation of the chicken. The staff served us quickly which was a plus since we were already quite hungry from walking around. Overall, it was a good experience for a solid, no-fuss meal when you want to avoid the massive crowds at the food park. || author=J B; rating=3; time=2025-12-10T11:11:38.568323420Z; relative=4 months ago; text=I don’t know, maybe my expectations were too high for a fast food franchise like this

The IT Park venue looks the part, just behind the bus station in front of Sugbo Mercado

I had never visited one of these brand franchises before - specialising in “hot wings and ribs’

Hit the Lime BBQ wings - the flavour was bad. The line was some kind of sweet artificial lime you’d expect from a green syrup at a fun fair or something. And just too sweet. The wings themselves were ok, meaty and tasty enough, but the disuse was just nasty

The spicy ribs - again the sauce was just too sweet. The meat was dry and tasteless although the ribs were “meaty”

Typical non existent Filipino service bit that what you’d expect at any fast food joint so at least consistent

The venue is decent enough, and co comment location

Three stars || author=Rihanna Abella; rating=1; time=2026-01-24T12:40:45.699189608Z; relative=3 months ago; text=The food itself was good and we really enjoyed it. However, our experience was affected by the long waiting time. It took around 40 minutes for our order to be served even though the place wasn’t that busy.
When the food arrived, we noticed that the extra rice we requested wasn’t included. My brother politely asked the staff/cashier (the one who took our order) why there was no extra rice, but she responded in an unpleasant tone, saying “tanawa dira sa resibo” instead of apologizing or explaining properly. My brother felt shy so he just went back to our table. Later on, my mother went to order again since gi kulangan shas rice, and she asked why the extra rice wasn’t included even though she mentioned it several times. The staff replied in a defensive and irritated way, saying she already repeated the order many times even though she didn’t. Instead of saying sorry, it felt like she was the one getting mad.
Overall, the food was good, but the customer service needs improvement. I hope the staff can be trained to be more polite and accommodating to customers, because good service really matters as much as good food. || author=Michael Voit; rating=3; time=2025-07-03T13:26:17.057253568Z; relative=9 months ago; text=I saw the name of the place New York Buffalo Brad’s and thought that I was gonna get to have authentic buffalo wings. sample_reviews:
author=Joseph Anin; rating=1; time=2026-03-07T17:22:24.857292938Z; relative=a month ago; text=Unfortunately this is the first time I had diarrhea from eating at a buffet restaurant. The food I decided to eat for the evening was beef stroganoff and roast beef. The stroganoff meat was tough and difficult to chew. As for the roast beef it was a little better in which I probably ate about 8 slices. But the next morning I had diarrhea and used the toilet 5 times within the day. We are currently trying to resolve this matter with their management and I will update the progress.

On a side note the staff are friendly and accommodating.

Update:
Feb 18, 2026 - no response from Seda management

March 8, 2026 - The management has chosen to ignore our complaint. Dining at Misto feels like wrapping yourself in a warm blanket of comfort food and kindness. The atmosphere is delightfully cozy, with soft lighting and relaxed interiors that invite you to settle in and savor the experience.

🥗 **Flavors for Every Mood**
The menu offers an ample spread—whether you’re in the mood for hearty mains, light bites, or local favorites, there's always something that hits the spot. Each dish arrives with thoughtful presentation and bold, satisfying flavor.

🍨 **Halo-Halo Heaven**
Their halo-halo deserves its own spotlight. It's layered with just the right mix of sweet, creamy, and icy goodness—a must-try masterpiece that takes the traditional treat to new heights.

🤝 **Service with Heart**
The staff truly elevate the experience. Attentive, courteous, and always ready with a smile, they create a dining vibe that feels both professional and genuinely warm.

🛋️ **Come for the Food, Stay for the Feeling**
Whether you’re popping in for a quiet lunch or a celebratory dinner, Misto offers more than just a meal—it’s a place where comfort, flavor, and Filipino hospitality come together beautifully. || author=Potato Monster; rating=5; time=2025-06-17T16:46:18.001982404Z; relative=10 months ago; text=Such a good buffet located in one of the busiest areas in Cebu. For 999php (Father's Day event price), you can have a variety of dishes in one place.
They all have kinds of meat like roasted beef, lechon,  pork humba, grilled seafood, steamed fish, chicken adobo, and more. They also have pinoy dishes like Bam-i, kare-kare and desserts like puto, cassava cake and halo-halo. Salad station is available too and they have a lot to choose from like the leafy greens and fruits. If you're in a mood for pasta, they also serve pasta dishes too. My absolute favorite for the whole dining experience would probably be the desserts. Shockingly, they serve such sweets that you wouldn't normally see in a buffet like Opera Cake and Croquembouche. They taste so good and clean. || author=Belle; rating=2; time=2025-03-31T14:01:32.017605Z; relative=a year ago; text=I should have not expected too much because the food was mediocre to say the least. The presentation is nice though. The buffet area was placed against the wall so it was not crowded.

There's a simple salad bar, a sushi bar and maki I had tasted like it was out for a while and the rice was a bit hard. There's nilaga but it tasted like chicken tinola, fried fish, deep fried pork, a beef dish. They have a halo halo bar but only have chocolate ice cream available.

The only 3 things I enjoyed are the red velvet cake cake which was very moist, ube Spanish bread and dark chocolate pudding. We also had coffee but it's not included in the buffet, you will have to pay extra if you want it.

We called for a reservation but they also accept walk in. Staff were okay, they can be more friendly, some were a bit standoffish.

For the food, we ordered:

🍝 Penne Seafood Pesto – 10/10
Absolutely delicious! The pasta was cooked perfectly, and the pesto had a rich and flavorful taste. We would definitely come back for this dish again.

🍕 Pizza Prosciutto e Arugula – 7/10
While we were excited about this since it’s an Italian restaurant, the pizza was a bit different from what we expected. It had a thin crust (which was nice!), but there were only a few slices of prosciutto on top. Still good, but could be improved.

🥩 Beef Salpicao – 8/10
The beef was tender and tasty. It’s a good dish overall, just not the standout of the night.

🍰 Tiramisu Alla Norma – 10/10
The star of the evening! The tiramisu was perfectly balanced.. not too sweet, with a rich blend of coffee and cream. We highly recommend trying this!

Overall, we had a great dinner! || author=Monessa PH; rating=4; time=2026-01-15T07:35:08.843678956Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Cozy, homey vibe with great food that are a treat to the tastebuds. I tried the pasta with cebú chorizo. Extraordinary. The place is really lovely. And the guy serving is super attentive. I will come back || author=Elena Park; rating=4; time=2025-08-14T07:36:15.792446521Z; relative=8 months ago; text=It’s a place I visit occasionally because the food is delicious. Although the space is small, it’s quiet spot that’s good for enjoying a meal. overall, the food is fresh and tasty. However, some dishes ca be but salty, so recommend requesting “less salt” for Items with a sauce.
Bread is served before the main dishes, it usually comes out 15-20mins after order. To avoid a gap in the dinning experience that may affect the enjoyment of the main dish, it would be better if the bread were served immediately after ordering.

For reference, non-heated dishes like salad take about 20-25mins to be served, and hot dishes take around 30-35mins, so it’s not particularly fast. I suggest being patient and enjoying relaxed pace. Overall, the staff were friendly and provided service at a level above average.

We had a soothing arroz caldo and tasty Lomi on our first night, and then Lomi and another dish (I can’t remember) on the second night. || author=Mark Delph; rating=4; time=2025-06-29T09:47:25.673084735Z; relative=10 months ago; text=I'm excited to share my recent dining experience at Merienda by Pan de Manila, conveniently located in IT Park, Cebu City!
This contemporary restaurant offers a fantastic blend of Filipino and international fare, and their expansive menu truly has something for everyone – from delicious sandwiches and pasta dishes to beloved traditional Filipino classics.
My partner opted for the Bangus Breakfast with eggs, a classic Filipino favorite that was prepared perfectly and thoroughly enjoyed. I decided on the Chicken Pot Pie, and I must say, it was an excellent choice! The pie boasted a truly delightful crust – flaky and golden, just as it should be, encasing a rich and savory filling.  We also enjoyed the cucumber lemonade.
Beyond our specific orders, the overall ambiance of Merienda by Pan de Manila is inviting and modern, making it a great spot for a casual meal, a quick coffee, or even a more substantial dining experience. The service was efficient and friendly, adding to the positive impression.
Whether you're craving a taste of home or something new and exciting, Merienda by Pan de Manila is a wonderful addition to the IT Park dining scene. We thoroughly enjoyed our meal and highly recommend checking it out! || author=Uly Catalan; rating=2; time=2026-01-17T01:25:58.038096065Z; relative=3 months ago; text=I do not recommend this place.

The food quality has gone downhill. It no longer tastes like what is advertised. Every visit feels like a gamble. Inconsistent and disappointing.

The service is worse. Staff are inattentive and clearly untrained. They do not listen and they do not anticipate basic needs.

We asked for the coffee to be served first. It never came. All the food arrived except the coffee. To make it worse, our main dishes were served with no plates and no rice. We had to ask repeatedly for things that should have been automatic.

The place is also painfully loud. Blenders running nonstop. Staff shouting across the floor. You can barely hear the person sitting in front of you.

They are clearly understaffed, and it shows. Everything feels rushed. No ownership. No care.

This is what happens when a business puts volume and sales ahead of customers. Merienda by Pan de Manila feels commercial, careless, and poorly managed. I will not be coming back.

name: 88th Avenue

address: Lot 686 Gov. M. Cuenco Ave, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Antonios Papadimitriou; rating=4; time=2026-02-24T08:15:08.143008295Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Overall I enjoyed my stay. I slept well, and shops, restaurants and pubs are at walking distance. Ayala mall is also close by. Staff was helpful and the location is good.
I had to change room as the AC and shower pressure was not ok. I got a bigger room for the same price. The beds are too soft for me. Apart from this it was an good stay || author=DNE RESOURCES HR; rating=1; time=2026-02-18T14:38:32.800651429Z; relative=2 months ago; text=This hotel is absolutely ridiculous. The staff may be friendly, but the hotel itself is dirty and poorly maintained. The balcony is not worth the price at all it’s way too small for two people who actually want to sit and relax comfortably.

The WiFi is unbelievably bad. If you work from home or need a stable connection, do NOT stay here. It’s a complete nightmare. The walls are paper-thin you can hear everyone gossiping, arguing, laughing, and talking loudly. If you value peace and quiet, this place is not for you.

The cleaning is a joke. How do you mop a floor without even sweeping it first? That’s just lazy and unacceptable. They also don’t clean your room unless you call and beg for it. For people staying long-term, they barely provide anything just cheap plastic items.for garbage  or dont even provide it not unless you ask for it!!!!!!  No tissue even!!!The elevators are hot and uncomfortable.

The TV setup is equally frustrating. You have to struggle with the remote just to get Netflix working properly. It shouldn’t be that complicated.

And don’t even get me started on their so-called “bar package.” One noodle, two tiny bottles of water, a twin-pack coffee, and one small local chocolate bar for 650? That’s daylight robbery. Completely overpriced and embarrassing.

On top of that, they’re calling you in the middle of the night just talking nonsense and disturbing your sleep. It’s completely unprofessional and unnecessary.

Overall, this place is overpriced, poorly managed, and not worth it. I absolutely do not recommend this hotel. || author=Togo; rating=1; time=2026-02-21T10:13:28.819811104Z; relative=2 months ago; text=I would like to share my honest opinion.

1️⃣ Immediately after check-in, when we entered the room, the door would not close properly. It stayed half open, and after a while, an alarm started ringing — as if the guest had done something wrong.

2️⃣ When I tried to take a shower, only cold water came out. My child endured it, but clearly something was wrong. After contacting the front desk, I was told that the hot water system must be switched on every time before use. If that is the case, why was this not explained at check-in? Has anyone considered how a guest feels discovering there is no hot water after already undressing?

3️⃣ We were looking forward to using the jacuzzi, but the rooftop area was completely dark and unusable.

4️⃣ The indoor pool was filled with cold, untreated fresh water. It was too cold to enter. Who exactly is expected to use a pool in that condition?

5️⃣ In the gym, two out of three treadmills were out of order.

6️⃣ A deposit was required, but my card was charged twice. Fortunately, I noticed it. Are guests expected to double-check every transaction to avoid being overcharged?

7️⃣ At checkout, I was suddenly told that one towel was missing and was asked to pay for it. The price was quoted inconsistently — first 800 pesos, then 900 pesos. I requested a detailed list and a follow-up email, but I have received no response.

Conclusion:

This was the worst hotel experience I have had in nearly 50 years of traveling. Four stars? Absolutely not. At best, it deserves two and a half stars.

The only positive point: the nearby tenant serving breakfast was excellent. || author=Princess Val Durado; rating=1; time=2025-11-07T08:42:09.980684368Z; relative=5 months ago; text=After Typhoon Tino, I needed a place to stay so I thought of 88th Ave since I stayed here after Typhoon Odette also. So I inquired directly via Messenger. As posted on their Facebook page, they have an on-going promo of 40% off on all room rates. My friend - availed the same promo around September this year which he paid only 2,500 a night for a Studio. I booked the same room and I was charged full room rate which is 4,000 a night. I asked about the promo — and they responded it doesn’t apply because it will depend on the room availability. Which doesn’t make sense to me, why promote 40% off on your page? And I checked Booking.com — it’s only 3,200 a night. 🙄 I should’ve booked there instead of direct booking. It would’ve saved me another 3,200. Especially right now under the state of calamity, every peso counts.

I’m not being cheap, I just want to pay fair and square. Overall, I feel like it is taking advantage of the situation. We’re still checked in currently as we desperately need a place to stay. I hope management reads this! Please do not promote any discounts on your page if you’re not willing to give those discounts! Disappointing. || author=Colton Bell; rating=2; time=2025-10-19T23:38:52.613713138Z; relative=6 months ago; text=We thought this hotel was okay when we arrived. However, on the first night when we were ready for bed, we opened the bed sheets and it was dirty inside with other peoples hair and stains. We called the reception to change the sheets which they did. They asked me 4 times if we had used the bed yet which I replied every time we had not used the bed yet. The real problem was however, they tried to charge us extra money to change the sheets. When I objected, the staff started saying that I was the problem and they charged us to change the sheets because we should’ve asked as soon as we entered the room at 2pm. The outside of the bed was clean so we didn’t think under the sheets would be dirty. Some of the staff were very rude as well when discussing the situation in Bisaya. They did not realize that I speak Bisaya too and quickly left when I started speaking to them, leaving the poor receptionist to handle the situation alone. I will not be coming back here and will advise my friends and family not to visit this hotel.

I would definitely visit again when I’m in the area. || author=Antonio Medina; rating=2; time=2025-10-31T09:14:29.128823481Z; relative=5 months ago; text=Sliced fat IS NOT MEAT
Grill is never CLEANED
Ice tee is included, but they never offered a BEER (they have it, and I wanted)

The FAT I cut out of the meat they charged 350 pesetas as LEFT OVER (manager removed it after my complain)

This is a bad reputation for Koreans to call it BBQ. I know it not like that in Korea

I’ll NEVER enter there again || author=Ella de la Victoria; rating=3; time=2025-01-04T20:32:21.628435Z; relative=a year ago; text=Review for Samgyupsalamat Central Bloc

We visited Samgyupsalamat Central Bloc for lunch as a family (2 adults and 2 kids), and while the food and service were good, the experience was let down by the restaurant’s ambiance and maintenance.

Food & Pricing:
The unlimited meat and sides are priced at 599/head (minimum of 2 people), which is fairly reasonable. The meat was served almost instantly, and the grill was prepped for us, making it easy to get started. However, not all the food was fresh—the fried chicken was cold, and the rice was barely warm.

Service:
The service was excellent and super quick, which is always a plus. The staff was attentive and efficient in clearing plates and ensuring we had what we needed.

Ambiance & Cleanliness:
This is where the experience fell short. The restaurant was quite warm, making it uncomfortable, especially while grilling. I also feel that the exhaust system isn’t functioning well as the whole restaurant just smells of smoke.

The floors were another issue—sticky and slippery, which is both unpleasant and an accident hazard, particularly for families with kids.

Verdict:
While the food and service were good, the overall dining experience could be significantly improved with better ventilation, temperature control, and regular maintenance of the floors. I’d recommend it for the food, but be prepared for some discomfort with the ambiance. || author=Rona Grace Quintas; rating=5; time=2025-02-03T16:26:36.533720Z; relative=a year ago; text=What stood out to me is the staff. They were very attentive and were quick in getting our refills. We didn't even need to request to have our grill replaced with a new one. The food was delicious but the side dishes could've been more diverse. Overall, the place had great ambience. It was not too crowded so we didn't have to wait long to be seated || author=Jem Banza; rating=1; time=2025-12-18T06:56:55.688555396Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Samgyupsalamat Central Bloc Cockroach Infestation

We got to cooking the meats and already ate the japchae when a cockroach randomly crawled on top of the table. Tried to kill it and I found this bunch of cockroaches directly under the table where we cooked the meats.

There's a lot of customers eating there at the time and I doubt that only our table got infested. I informed the staff immediately and I thought they're gonna do something about it like do pest control. When we passed by a few minutes after, they are still accepting customers while there are cockroaches under the table.

It's so badly infested that the cockroach even got to my stuff and I only noticed when we're at Ayala Center already.

Prior to this, the customer service is already bad. There is no one manning the dining area. Their crew would just huddle at the cashier booth and talk among themselves.

Just think twice if you plan on having samgyup here.

name: Ato-ah
address: 38 Park Avenue, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Jennifer Sibi; rating=3; time=2025-12-23T08:09:27.887068609Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Atmosphere 4 stars: Interior design, lighting, furniture, cutlery, impeccable.
Service 4 stars: Most items in the menu were not available. We need to tell the waiters that it was warm in our area and to please turn on the aircon. We asked for warm water from one of our guest and we got room temperature water, so we asked again for a little warmer and we got really hot water. You need to crane your neck and wave to the waiters attention. (I know I may sound entitled, but there are other non-Michelin select restaurants that has attentive staff)
Food 2 stars: Food presentation and execution was excellent. The textures were top notch perfectly cooked, but it lacks the depth of the Cebuano flavor that I was looking for. Other than that, some of the food were served at almost room temperature. Kinda bit disappointing, especially with this Linarang soup which was supposed to be piping hot.

Dishes that didn’t disappoint.
Scallops
Kinilaw
Barbeque

This is just my personal opinion. My taste buds might be different from others. || author=Anton Antonio; rating=5; time=2025-11-29T15:12:15.447883615Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Ato Ah is a great lunch spot and an easy five star experience. It may not have a Michelin star, but it is proudly recognized in the Michelin Guide for good cooking, and the quality of the dishes makes that very clear. The restaurant has a relaxed and spacious setup with high ceilings, floor to ceiling windows, and a semi open kitchen that adds a lively, homey feel, but the real highlight is definitely the food.

The menu is concise and focused on Filipino favorites, especially Cebuano specialties. Portions are well sized for solo diners or small groups, and their affordable lunch sets make this a reliable go to for nearby office workers. During our visit, the dining room was a bit warm because of the lunch crowd, but once the food arrived, the flavors took full attention.

The recommended lumpia was the standout for me. It was perfectly crisp on the outside, packed with flavor on the inside, and easily the dish I would order again without hesitation. The liempo was also a hit. It was tender, juicy, and had a satisfying mix of smoky and savory. The pancit was just okay, not the strongest dish of the meal, but still enjoyable enough to finish.

Overall, the food at Ato Ah carries the experience. If you are looking for well executed Filipino dishes in a bright and inviting space, this is a place worth returning to. I look forward to trying more of their specialties next time. || author=ensorcelled.; rating=5; time=2026-01-16T05:45:34.469154890Z; relative=3 months ago; text=My friends and I went here to catch up after a long time, the place was really nice. The atmosphere was cozy and the service was stellar. The staff were very attentive and accommodated our requests as promptly as possible.

The food options were great too. Loved the familiarity of dishes since everything was named as what it is, none of that fancy fusion food names, we got a total of 7 dishes.

The serving size is okay, would be good enough for 2-3 pax per order but for more I think it'd be a stretch?? Flavor-wise everything was well-seasoned and stuck to the true flavor of the Filipino dishes. Some dishes could've been better, I think the Humba specifically lacked for me.

We loved the lumpia (very reminiscent to Jabee lumpia shanghai), the pancit and the scallops too (albeit the silly plate to serving size ratio haha)

Enjoyed our time here and would definitely love to come back again. || author=derrick lluch garces; rating=5; time=2026-02-13T18:06:33.573329848Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Had a lovely dinner with fam here.
We made reservations online to guarantee a spot because the place isn’t very big.
Ample options in the menu which were all very yummy and each order was good for 2-3 people.
The bagoong rice was crazy delicious!
I didn’t know they were michelin-rated(deserving ❤️).
They had singers when we went and those two were very good. It might be hard to hear your companions during their set; heads up. || author=Vanessa Garcia; rating=5; time=2026-01-29T15:10:31.615083670Z; relative=2 months ago; text=As a Cebuano, there’s a deep sense of pride in calling ATOAH ours. A Filipino restaurant that honors CEBUANO CUISINE while thoughtfully modernizing it, ATOAH respects tradition and executes with clarity. It presents Cebuano dishes with balance, intention, and a modern voice — without losing its soul. This is the kind of food, care, and discipline that deserves global recognition. Confidently, proudly, and without bias, this Michelin Selected recognition is truly wel deserved!!

Staff were affable, too. || author=ay; rating=5; time=2025-08-17T07:59:45.613832026Z; relative=8 months ago; text=Review:
I really love bread and often visit bakeries, but croissants are my absolute favorite. When you search for the best croissants in Cebu, this café always comes up. I usually go to their branch in IT Park, but this time I visited their shop on the 2nd floor of Ayala Mall for the first time.

The atmosphere was very pleasant — quiet, with people working on their laptops or simply enjoying coffee. Since the café is connected to the department store, there’s a steady flow of people, but it still feels cozy. The staff welcomed me warmly with a smile and were very accommodating even with several orders.

This time, instead of bread, I tried one of their desserts, and I highly recommend the Basque cheesecake. Of course, their croissants are also delicious, and the coffee is very good too. Prices are a bit higher compared to other places, but the quality of the coffee makes it worth it.

They also seem to have a wide range of meal options, so it could be a nice spot for lunch as well. I’ll definitely come back again! || author=Christine Jonah Abaño; rating=4; time=2025-12-27T11:26:08.340957986Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Living out for what they are known. Tried the Okinawa Latte 💯👌🤩 The Filipino Rice 🥣 with a twist is worth trying too aside from the bread and pastries. Since still here in Davao, would like to try the egg tart🥰 || author=Errol; rating=1; time=2025-03-17T10:17:53.696980Z; relative=a year ago; text=Great food, coffee, tea & pastries all the time!
Abacookie is my all time favorite & the half chicken!

As of March 2025
As I was enjoing my coffee in another shop, I have decided to just share the feedback I had been holding for days now.
I’m writing to express our deep disappointment with the way we’ve been treated at abaca ayala, over the past few days. As long-time loyal customers—visiting this branch in Ayala almost daily and frequenting Abaca Seaside or Paseo on Mondays, Fridays, and weekends—we never expected to be made to feel unwelcome at a place we’ve supported for years.

Lately, our visits have been awkward, with staff seemingly trying to keep us from sitting in our usual outdoor spot, where we have always preferred to stay. Initially, we were asked to move to another table, which was just beside our usual one, with vague and inconsistent reasons such as:
1.	Staffing issues – We were told no one could attend to that area, yet our table is actually closer to the door than the one they wanted us to move to.
2.	Dust concerns – The claim that our table is dusty due to nearby construction, while the adjacent table was supposedly dust-free, seemed absurd.
3.	Better service – We even tried offering solutions, like having a self-service system where customers could pay and order at the counter, but no one seemed interested in listening. Instead, we kept hearing, “Because the management said so. There is nothing we can do.”

We attempted to address this with Jody, Solamit, and Matthew, but unfortunately, our concerns were ignored. What’s even more disheartening is the way we were treated last week—being booted out like criminals or beggars from a coffee shop we have supported for years. A server named Mike approached & requested us to transfer saying “mo inform lang mi nimo sir na gipalagpas lang mo namo the past few days but dili na gyud pwede palingkoran diri na table” The experience was not only upsetting but also embarrassing.

We’ve always admired Abaca for the quality of its food, pastries and coffee—hands down, some of the best! But at the end of the day, no level of quality can make up for a lack of respect for loyal customers. It’s disappointing to realize that customer service, loyalty, and valuing people don’t seem to be a priority in your mission or core values.

Needless to say, I have changed my high rating to thw lowest possible as our experience no longer reflects the quality & service we once appreciated. i truly hope that mgmt re-evaluates how it treats its long-time customers as no business thrives by driving away the very people who have supported it for years!

author=Marie; rating=4; time=2025-12-18T11:31:20.679549223Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Recently I went to Kuroda, which I often see at SM. The prices are very low even though it is located inside a mall. I ordered it knowing that I didn't want it to be cheap or bad.

I went to Japan three times. What I found out when I went there was the difference in taste between Japanese food at Sumo-san and Tokyotokyo and in Japan. That's not to say that Kuroda is bad.I think people who have been to Japan and eaten things like Yoshinoya, Yayoiken, and Tonkatsu no Saboten will understand this. The same goes for Coco No. 1. Their service is also exceptional. I was surprised that we did not have to wait to be seated on a Saturday afternoon. Comfort rooms are also available inside the resto. If you love tea, try the hibiscus tea! It is caffeine-free, sour, slightly sweet, and refreshingly good! Abaca's egg tart is a legend. Slightly sweet, crust is flaky, custard is soft. The ugly macaron is underrated. This is my go-to indulgent sweet at Abaca. Chocolate from top to bottom, sweet, creamy, slightly bitter. They are usually packed especially on afternoon weekends so please manage your expectations. || author=Jonathan S. Ng; rating=5; time=2025-08-23T07:54:44.144702114Z; relative=8 months ago; text=ABACA Cafe at Ayala Center Cebu delivers fantastic but very strong coffee that showcases their expertly crafted blend with a lovely taste that coffee enthusiasts will truly appreciate. The bold strength demonstrates quality beans and proper roasting techniques that create a memorable caffeine experience.

The naked waffle stands out as exceptionally fluffy and delicious, achieving the perfect texture that makes each bite light yet satisfying. This simple preparation allows the waffle's quality to shine without unnecessary embellishments.

The breakfast quesadilla impresses with its generous mix of meat and vegetables, creating an incredibly heavy and filling dish that transforms a simple concept into a substantial meal. The abundance of quality ingredients makes this a standout brunch option that satisfies even hearty appetites.

The salmon eggs Benedict proves equally delicious, showcasing proper hollandaise technique paired with quality salmon that elevates this brunch classic to restaurant excellence.
For desserts, the Portuguese egg tart is good while delivering authentic flavors, and the chocolate cream puff is delicious with rich, creamy filling that provides a satisfying sweet finish.

I really liked the attentive staff who provided excellent service throughout the meal, and the lovely view from the dining room added an extra dimension to the experience, creating a pleasant atmosphere that enhanced the overall enjoyment.

Overall, ABACA Cafe represents a great coffee shop that rivals - if not emerges as the victor in a fight with - Wildflour (for you natives of Manila). This comparison speaks volumes about ABACA's quality, as Wildflour sets the Manila standard for premium cafe experiences.

ABACA successfully combines exceptional coffee, quality brunch preparations, delicious pastries, attentive service, and scenic views to create a comprehensive cafe experience that can compete with Manila's best establishments while bringing that level of excellence to Cebu. || author=Lisa J; rating=5; time=2026-03-04T03:19:02.367856612Z; relative=a month ago; text=Great flat white, as well as cold brew. Highly recommend it if you're craving authentic Filipino cuisine! 100% worth it! || author=Nicole Nuñez; rating=5; time=2025-09-07T16:34:08.018141981Z; relative=7 months ago; text=As usual, the food was great❣️ My family and I ordered their Bangus ala Pobre, Gerry's Special Inihaw na Bangus, and Crispy Kangkong. We were told by the staff that the food will be ready within 20–25 minutes, but (a good "but) the Crispy Kangkong arrived within just 5 minutes or less. ✨️ We were also served a small plate of potato chips (with gravy) on the house as an additional appetizer. 💛

Bangus ala Pobre
- The grilled fish was tender (not raw), flavorful, and a bit juicy.✨
- My favorite were the slightly burnt parts of the fish. They were the tastiest. 😋 Also loved how crispy the sides were. 👌
- What made the dish even more delectable were the garlic bits and grilled sides (carrots, turnips, tomatoes, shallots, and green beans). 😋

Gerry's Special Inihaw na Bangus
- Similar to the Bangus ala Pobre, the fish itself was well-seasoned and tender. ✨️
- The creamy, sweet, savory, and slightly sour egg ensalada (egg, shallots, tomatoes, and scallion strips) significantly elevated the overall taste of the dish. 😋 A perfect complement❣️

Crispy Kangkong
- True to its name—very crispy ✨️
- The sweet shallot-tomato(?)-mayo on the side was a perfect dip as it added a depth of flavor to such a simple dish. 💚🤍 It also helped with the greasiness and blandess of the plain kangkong chips. ✨️

So far, the staff in this particular branch are consistently providing quality service as they have been accommodating, attentive, and friendly ever since (similar to that of their Ayala Central Bloc branch but notably even more so). Will definitely dine here again. 💖

There was a constant machine noise like from an overly active air conditioning that is so loud that you can hear nothing else. I'm glad that I ate alone, because I'm pretty sure I couldn't have managed to talk with anyone in this noise.

There are what looks like ai-generated pictures on the walls, which doesn't add anything impressive to the atmosphere.

The seating itself is nice and spacious, and the backs are tall enough that each "booth" feels private enough. || author=B R; rating=3; time=2026-01-05T13:29:50.100958720Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Nice atmosphere and good service, food a little pricey for what it is.

-> Good: Beef Pares - Soft and tender meat, flavorful sauce and chili.
-> To be improved: Roasted Chicken wirh Chimichiurri - Rather dry, no Chimichurri identified
-> Okay: Waldorf Salad - Nothing special, small portion || author=Lean Gomez; rating=4; time=2025-07-22T00:12:22.379070228Z; relative=9 months ago; text=We visited Bamaka Dine and Drink for a late Saturday lunch to celebrate our friends' birthday, and while the place was unexpectedly quiet for lunch hour, it worked in our favor. The atmosphere was cozy and intimate, making it feel like a private dining spot, perfect for casual gatherings or catching up with friends. The interiors have a modern, artsy vibe that adds a nice touch to the overall dining experience.

The staff were warm and accommodating from the moment we arrived. They were more than willing to assist with our small birthday celebration and even helped us with a few personal requests, which we really appreciated. Service was attentive but not overbearing, just the way it should be.

We ordered a variety of dishes to share, and here's a quick rundown:
* Classic Carbonara – Decent, but not particularly memorable. The sauce could have used more depth and creaminess. Portion was on the small side for its price.
* Honey Mustard Chicken Wings – Flavorful with a distinct sweet and tangy twist. The wing parts were large and juicy, which we appreciated.
* Buffalo Chicken Wings – A bit underwhelming in flavor. It lacked the heat and tang expected from a classic buffalo wing.
* Spinach and Gorgonzola Pizza – This was one of the highlights. Flavorful, with a nice balance between the richness of cheese and freshness of the spinach. Highly recommended!
* Lechon Kawali with Pinakbet – An interesting twist that fused homestyle Pinakbet with crispy lechon kawali on top. While it felt a little off-theme from the rest of our orders, the lechon belly was perfectly crispy and the veggies were cooked well.
* Gambas Aglio Olio Pasta – Light but flavorful. The garlic and shrimp worked well together, although a hint more chili would have elevated it.
* Seafood Ceviche – While I’m not a fan of raw dishes, my friends who tried it said the combination was refreshing and well-balanced.

The food overall was enjoyable, though a few items could use refinement in either flavor depth or portion size, especially considering the prices, which lean toward the higher end for a casual dining place. That said, presentation across the board was well done, plated attractively and consistently.

All in all, Bamaka Dine and Drink is a charming hidden gem with a strong potential. It’s perfect for quiet catch-ups or intimate celebrations. We’ll definitely be back to try more from their menu, especially their steak and drinks next time.

author=Khrys Burden; rating=4; time=2025-09-28T11:38:06.596879965Z; relative=7 months ago; text=Our overall experience at this place was very mid. We had dinner on a sunday night and were seated promptly.

As for the karaage, its kind kf hard to mess karaage up so it was good as expected. The chahan was for my dad so I didn't get to taste the rice but the shrimp on top were overcooked.

📞 For reservations, call us or send a DM today! @seda_hotels || author=Maxwell Jimenez; rating=5; time=2022-05-08T08:58:52.149134Z; relative=3 years ago; text=Misto's Mother's Day Lunch Buffet Review

First time trying out the extensive buffet of Misto post-pandemic. The choices are unmistakably Misto. Mains were mostly Filipino cuisine and even had a street food section for your fishball and tempura fix. I started light with some roll assortment (I think the Maki station is the one I missed the most with buffets). The rolls were tasty and deceiving (One roll had an orange translucent exterior which I mistook as fish but was actually some sort of raddish).

Being the voracious carnivore that I am, I got a huge serving of the roast beef and lechon belly. Both were seasoned well and didn't really need the sauce (which were a bit bland for the beef). They also had a noodle  and pasta section and a grazing section.

The dessert section was quite extensive with three types of whole cake, mini desserts and of course some kakanin and a halo-halo section for that Filipino touch. Everything were great except for the chocolate cake which had more cocoa content so it had that deep dark chocolate bitterness to it. The standout dish for me was the mango panna cotta. The mango toppings was topped with some calamansi infused syrup with gave the relatively simple dessert a fresh kick to it. It was so good it was the only dish I made a second round for.

Overall I always trust that most of Mistos' offerings are great tasting dishes and they did not disappoint with this buffet. Service was still quick and warm despite the busy mood. || author=miguel miguel; rating=5; time=2023-07-15T02:57:17.535223Z; relative=2 years ago; text=my first time in this "new" hotel since taken over by ayala. it used to be marriot hotel.  first of all, i got free parking which was good. the atmosphere was very familiar because they didnt change much. concierge were up on their toes to help me with my helmet. since we were able to reserve a table for everyone, sitting was not an issue. getting to the buffet area was a bit crowded due to the diners. you have bacon, chorizo, potatoes, eggs, native desserts like budbud, bread, noodles, breakfast and name it i guess they have it. an enjoyable experience for a get together with out of town family members. || author=Gay H. Parilla; rating=5; time=2023-05-30T21:28:14.851154Z; relative=2 years ago; text=I had a wonderful time at Misto in Seda Hotel Ayala Center Cebu last Sunday on the occasion of mother's day. Well, I really was not expecting a  grand celebration but my family brought me there.

They had a special lunch treat for mums, a band singing classic music. The melody complemented their very delicious buffet. Although, the choices of food were not really that wide but they had the famous filipino lechon on a Sunday with a variety of salad - greek, caprese and salmon to mention a few and they also had penne pasta, a variety of fruits, drinks and of course my favorite desserts 😍

The ambiance was so relaxing and the service was excellent.

When you are in Ayala Center area, Misto at Seda is a must try buffet venue for picky eaters 💖

author=Rajega; rating=3; time=2025-11-01T02:39:13.695512043Z; relative=5 months ago; text=When we arrived the place was empty, we let ourselves in and moments later they gave us a menu. We ordered Salisbury angus steak, Brazilian ribeye, mushroom soup, linguini, and  pork chop, along with a pitcher of four seasons juice.

The atmosphere felt pretty gloomy with only us there, the air filled with Meghan Trainor songs accompanied by the chatting of the servers.

The Salisbury angus steak didn't meet expectations, it tasted bland and lacked seasoning, the mashed it came with tasted better.

I told them to cook the brazilian ribeye steak medium rare, it was juicy and it tasted decent, albeit it was a bit rubbery.

The pork chops didn't have much flavor but it tasted fine, it was hard to cut though.

The linguini was dry, like it came out of an oven, looked premade and just reheated. It was a little bit on the salty side.

The mushroom soup tasted good, honestly the thing I enjoyed the most, too bad it's serving was pretty small.

The service was fine, they got us anything we asked for (pepper & hot sauce) but they did charge us with a 106 peso service fee.

It was a decent experience, would I come back? Probably not, but it wasn't too bad, I could see it thriving if the prices weren't too high, but in it's current state, there's no reason to choose it over other restaurants in the building offering the same things but better at the same price. || author=Aaron Nuñez; rating=3; time=2025-11-28T06:34:17.997082954Z; relative=5 months ago; text=Brizket Steak House gave us a pretty straightforward dining experience. name: Majestic Ayala Center Cebu
address: 4th Level, Garden Area, ayala center cebu, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Lean Gomez; rating=5; time=2025-10-27T03:25:31.268998334Z; relative=6 months ago; text=Majestic at Ayala Center Cebu truly impressed us, especially with its new and spacious location. It’s easy to find within the mall, and the expansion from their old spot is very noticeable, more room, better ambiance, and a generally more comfortable dining experience. We arrived as a big group without a reservation, and although we had to wait briefly outside so they could prepare a long table for us, the staff handled it efficiently and politely, which we appreciated.

The restaurant is clean, well-lit, and comfortably cool thanks to efficient air conditioning. The menu is straightforward and easy to understand, making it simple to choose both classic favorites and new dishes to try. We ordered a variety of food, and overall, the experience was extremely satisfying.

Some standout dishes were the Bird’s Nest Soup and Sweet and Sour Pork, both consistent Majestic favorites with rich flavors. Their Fried Rice had the right texture, not too hard or overly oily. A major highlight was the Patatim with Bread. Though its appearance may be a bit overwhelming at first because of the fatty cut, the taste completely wins you over. The meat is fall-off-the-bone tender, and the sauce is incredibly flavorful. The Birthday Pancit was also well done, generously topped with meat and vegetables.

Other dishes like Calamari, Fried Chicken, Lumpia, and Lechon Kawali were solid and satisfying. Nothing overly unique but comforting in that familiar, classic way. Their Siomai continues to live up to Majestic’s reputation, flavorful, juicy, and a must-order.

With the number of dishes we ordered, it was understandable that serving everything took a while, but the wait was reasonable and the food arrived warm. Portions were generous and fairly priced, especially for group dining.

Service was another strong point. The servers were friendly, attentive, and quick to accommodate requests. They even sang enthusiastically for our birthday celebrant, making the moment extra cheerful and memorable.

Overall, Majestic remains a go-to spot for gatherings, celebrations, and satisfying Chinese cuisine. Would absolutely dine here again! 🍜🥢 || author=Joseph E Besin; rating=2; time=2026-02-17T10:33:54.280486049Z; relative=2 months ago; text=We dined as a group of 16 people to celebrate my daughter’s birthday on February 16, 2026, during lunch. When we placed our order, the waiter informed us that the food would be ready in 30 minutes. However, we ended up waiting about an hour, and the dishes were served in a staggered manner, which affected our appetite, even though most of the food tasted good.
One of the dishes was overly sour and salty. In addition, when we were almost finished eating, only one steamed pompano fish was served, while the second fish and the kangkong had still not arrived. Due to the significant delay, we decided to cancel the remaining orders. Initially, the server informed us that cancellation was not possible, but after requesting to speak with the manager, our request was finally granted.
We would also like to note that the air-conditioning was not sufficient, and the dining area felt uncomfortably hot during our visit.
For group dining reservations, it would be helpful if the staff suggested pre-ordering to customers to improve service efficiency and ensure a better dining experience. At the moment, because of this experience, I would not recommend the restaurant to my friends and colleagues.
If the management would like to discuss this matter further or hear my suggestions, I would be glad to speak with them. || author=Kyle Vincent; rating=4; time=2025-09-28T10:53:35.505921238Z; relative=7 months ago; text=Good food, and plenty of serving , needs more improvement of food serving and making it more delicious to be consumed that you'll surely miss.
Overall it's good also the price is just right but I'm hoping that they improve their food quality in the near future. || author=Daniel Tjen; rating=1; time=2026-03-03T05:02:43.005897127Z; relative=a month ago; text=The place is nice in ambience, however the staff lack care in both their service and communication skills with the customers. We waited for over an hour for a table whilst i can see the table available inside. No update on what time we can go in or how long the food will take to come out.

Train your staff to be more energetic and respect the customers. You don’t need anything fancy, just give your energy. Smile at your customers and have a happy greetings that you want them in your store.

Overall food wasn’t bad but the staff really disappointed the whole experience || author=Gary; rating=5; time=2025-11-29T11:29:37.554439136Z; relative=4 months ago; text=A friend and I ordered the Lemon chicken, some white rice and some Plain Siomai. author=Jeremy W; rating=2; time=2025-06-12T15:27:34.203254Z; relative=10 months ago; text=Based on reviews, we had average-good expectations but we were left quite disappointed by the food. The ribs were dry and much too sweet, the rice wet and soggy. The fish in the fish tacos were cold and not freshly fried. Luckily the sauce and salad were good enough to cover for the fish. The nachos was also very average.
There was also a cockroach crawling around on the floor in the main dining area.
Overall a below average meal and definitely will not be back. || author=G A; rating=1; time=2025-04-20T11:52:36.692841Z; relative=a year ago; text=I recently visited a well-known steakhouse in Cebu that I used to enjoy, but this visit was extremely disappointing. When I arrived, I noticed that several staff members were occupying customer tables, chatting and relaxing instead of attending to guests. It already felt unprofessional.

Once seated, I was surprised to see many menu items—especially steaks—marked as “N/A.” These weren’t just side items, but some of their main signature dishes.

I ordered David Dean’s Tenderloin, specifically because it was one of the few dishes still available and it was supposed to come with mashed potatoes (which I prefer over rice). However, what was served was a steak placed directly on top of plain white rice. I was not informed about any changes when ordering.

When I asked the staff, they simply said mashed potatoes were not available and that they had substituted rice without informing me. No apology, no compensation, just a flat-out “That’s how it is.” I was still charged the full price of 475 pesos.

This level of service is unacceptable. The quality has dropped significantly over the years, and staff training seems nonexistent. Tourists and locals alike deserve better. I won’t return, and I strongly suggest others think twice before going. || author=Keyeana Vizcarra; rating=4; time=2025-03-11T09:04:55.211975Z; relative=a year ago; text=Big servings great for groups!

author=Chelle T; rating=4; time=2026-02-09T22:33:10.192716640Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Made reservations for 6pm, got seated right away. Ordered mainly appetizers and sashimi to try. They were out of tuna when we arrived, since the sashimi is limited to tuna or salmon, we were a little disappointed.  Then as our dinner ended, we saw a plate of sashimi being walked to a customer, we asked our waiter if they have tuna now, he said they just delivered but he failed to mention it to us, we then ordered two plates. The fatty salmon sashimi and sushi were great. Tuna was a bit underwhelming. The skewers: chicken liver, pork and beef cubes were flavorful. The beef a particular favorite. Crunchy mix tempura, fried veggies, shrimp and calamari was a surprise especially when we couldn't distinguish until we bit into it. Our friend liked his katsudon and we liked the fried rice although it would have been nice to eat it with the skewers for balance. The textures were great, small pieces of egg, shrimp, veggies per bite and not salty. Service was fine, we asked for items and they provided. || author=Jorgen Frohlich; rating=5; time=2026-03-07T13:47:00.954410601Z; relative=a month ago; text=Watami is one of the better Japanese restaurants in Cebu.

You probably want to sit inside with air conditioning in the daytime. But in the evening the outdoor area is lovely.
The service, although very busy, was welcoming and professional.
There are several restaurants in this area of the mall and it does have a slight atmosphere of food court.
The portions are rather small But mostly delicious especially the squid, and I recommend this restaurant. || author=Adam Menkes; rating=5; time=2026-01-22T07:53:53.849655687Z; relative=3 months ago; text=I rarely give 5 stars. Ordered set 3 for 999 and additional 99 for the tea however I was hoping that it would be a tuna grilled but pork belly and giserve but I didn’t mind at all. Hopefully they get it right next time. The staff are approachable and friendly, the place is not really cooled despite sa ac but its still cozy. Worth a try || author=Andi Susanto; rating=5; time=2025-09-27T16:09:55.095095297Z; relative=7 months ago; text=First time trying this place and I’m impressed. I ordered the chicharon sisig, lechon kawali, puto with dips, halo-halomazing, and garlic rice. Every dish was flavorful and well prepared. The chicharon sisig was crispy and savory without being too oily, the lechon kawali had a great crunch with tender meat, and the garlic rice tied everything together. The puto with dips was a nice, light side, and the halo-halomazing was a refreshing finish. Overall, delicious food and a satisfying first visit. I’ll be back. || author=Aaron Nuñez; rating=5; time=2025-12-30T07:43:43.280236181Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Hukad at Ayala Center remains one of Cebu’s go-to family restaurants when it comes to honest, comforting Filipino food.  As part of the Golden Cowrie group, they carry the same reputation locals trust, familiar flavors, generous servings, and dishes that feel like they belong at a shared table.

We ordered a spread that covered all the Filipino comfort food bases: adobo bisaya that hits with its savory depth, pork sinigang with a properly sour and soul-warming broth, crisp and dependable lumpia shanghai, rich vegetable kare-kare with its nutty sauce, and sisig that’s satisfying without being overwhelming.

Nothing here tries too hard, and that’s exactly the charm. The food is consistently good, meant for sharing, and best enjoyed with friends and conversations that linger. Hukad isn’t about reinvention, it’s about preserving what works and doing it well, every single time.

If you’re looking for a place that reliably serves Filipino favorites in a relaxed, family-friendly setting, Hukad continues to earn its place at the table. || author=Joshua Dan Naval; rating=1; time=2026-02-11T21:17:04.327878934Z; relative=2 months ago; text=We are dining at Hukad Ayala center cebu on February 11 2026 8pm. We ordered Beef paris, Pancit, unli garlic rice & Ice tea. Ice tea arived after few minutes, with bowl & plates no spoon, fork & tissue we have to ask after our pancit is serve. But we forget to tell that we don't eat pork therefor we ask them to wrap it for take out and order another one without pork. the new pancit is serve at 9pm but the beef paris is not yet serve, therefore we ask, what they said the mistaken to serve at the wrong table. therefore we start to eat with the pancit & rice only after 1hr of waiting (we did not notice the time talking to each other), the beef paris is serve after 15-20 minutes later. then when we ask for additional rice because its unlimited it takes a while also. after we finish our last bite they ask to pay the bills without me calling & clean the table before we leave (the cleaner ask politely though).
We are finished almost 10 pm & leave the store UNHAPPY. They did not even compensate for the inconveniences, they apologize but not proper apology. NO TO HUKAD AYALA CENTER CEBU ANYMORE! BTW we are the last customer to leave on that day.

Over-all, we had a great birthday party celebration.

As for the shakes, they were a hit! Creamy, not overly sweet, and the perfect way to end the meal.
Ambience? Cozy and cute, very chill spot for a dinner.
Still curious about the pancakes, though — maybe next time!

Rating: 10/10
Would come back for the food and finally try the pancakes! || author=Cam (黄秀贤); rating=5; time=2025-07-12T11:26:35.261997658Z; relative=9 months ago; text=Fuwari Pancake is a delightful spot that brings a taste of Japan to the heart of the city. While the name may suggest a singular focus on pancakes, the restaurant offers far more than just fluffy sweets— it’s a hidden gem for authentic Japanese comfort food.

One standout dish for me is their Taru Taru Chicken meal, which truly delivers in both flavor and presentation. The chicken is perfectly fried to a golden crisp, juicy on the inside with a light and satisfying crunch on the outside.

The dish is accompanied by well-prepared sides that round out the experience— each element thoughtfully plated and seasoned just right.

Their other Japanese offerings are also worth exploring. From the texture of the rice to the richness of the sauces and the precision of the flavors, you can tell that care and authenticity are at the heart of their kitchen. It’s comfort food elevated with a refined Japanese sensibility.

Whether you’re dropping by for their signature souffle pancakes or craving a satisfying Japanese meal, Fuwari Pancake is a charming and reliable choice. The ambiance is cozy and welcoming, with friendly service that enhances the dining experience. A must-visit for those who appreciate good food, quiet charm, and a little culinary escape to Japan. || author=mmm kii; rating=5; time=2026-01-15T09:25:00.727770594Z; relative=3 months ago; text=I had an amazing experience at Fuwari Pancake! I came here based on all the positive reviews, but I honestly have no idea what those people were eating — because my experience was completely different.
The food was incredibly disappointing. Portions were small for the price, and the quality just wasn’t there.
The sushi was terrible — I ordered the unagi roll, and it tasted like it was made with low-quality eel. The eel sauce was watered down and had a weird, unpleasant taste.
The okonomiyaki was a complete joke. It was tiny, bland, and far from authentic. You can see in the photo — that little circle is supposed to be 20 grams of pork, but there was maybe 1.5 grams at best. Basically, it was just shredded cabbage with a slice of cheese, an overwhelming amount of dried fish flakes, and hardly any pork at all.
The only halfway decent item was the fried rice, but again — way too small for the price.
Overall, this place is absolutely not worth it. name: Phat Pho Ayala Terraces
address: Biliran Rd, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Jae Yoon; rating=5; time=2026-01-11T10:15:07.049698014Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Straight up, this places knows how to make viet food. 2 dishes ive had here now.  The beef pho, and the bun bo hue.  The broth on both of them were amazing.  The bun bo hue in particular, which has been disappointing at every restaurant in my city,  this one here gave me a reminder of the first one ive ever had, that made me fall in love with it, home made by a friend. The broth was rich, flavorful, and spicy. The meats were tailored more for non locals, minus the blood curd.  For me, it was great. Others might prefer a more traditional version.  The only negative, the service seems to run a bit slow. I am so happy I found this place.  Its unfortunate I will be moving on in my travels. || author=Bryan Mcdonald; rating=3; time=2025-12-18T10:25:22.347591583Z; relative=4 months ago; text=Im amazed at how mediocre most food is in the Philippines.  This is a pretty nice resturant in the nicest mall in cebu and is say it's the most average Pho I've ever had.  Maybe it's the subpar ingredients available,  but just bland  no spice or really any taste at all.  Very disappointed. || author=maria luisa pages; rating=5; time=2025-09-09T06:12:34.511652144Z; relative=7 months ago; text=Rainy days always call for comfort food, and Phat Pho hits the spot every time. It’s the best place to go when you’re craving something warm, hearty, and still on the healthier side. Their pho is like a hug in a bowl — satisfying without the guilt, and just the kind of soup that makes you feel better whether you’re fighting off a fever, a cough, or simply the blues. The ambiance is cozy yet vibrant, making it perfect for solo meals or catching up with friends. Phat Pho proves that eating healthy doesn’t mean compromising on flavor. Always a go-to. || author=Pie Cañete; rating=5; time=2025-08-08T02:00:26.640478890Z; relative=8 months ago; text=Eating at Phat Pho felt like being back in Vietnam. The pho was rich and comforting, the flavors were spot on, and everything tasted fresh and real. It’s the kind of meal that brings back travel memories with every bite. || author=Angel Oliquino; rating=4; time=2025-05-11T16:52:06.675358Z; relative=11 months ago; text=We recently dined at Phat Pho around 1 PM, right in the middle of the lunch rush, and despite the wait for a table, the experience was absolutely worth it! The moment we stepped in, we were greeted by their friendly and welcoming staff—everyone was polite, attentive, and made sure we were comfortable from start to finish. Their excellent service really stood out.

The restaurant is clean, bright, and well-maintained, with a relaxed ambiance that makes you want to stay a little longer. Even though it was busy, the atmosphere didn’t feel chaotic or cramped, which I appreciated.

Now for the food—the pho was amazing Flavorful, rich, and comforting—everything you want in a good bowl of pho. It was served hot and fresh, with generous portions and quality ingredients. We also tried their halo-halo, and we were pleasantly surprised by how big it was—it’s definitely good for sharing (perfect for two people!). It was refreshing, creamy, and had a great balance of sweet and icy textures.

The serving sizes in general are very reasonable, especially for the price. Everything we ordered felt like it was thoughtfully prepared.

I’m definitely excited to go back and explore more from their menu. There’s a lot of variety, and if everything else tastes as good as what we had, I know I’ll be in for a treat.

If you’re a fan of Vietnamese food or simply looking for a great spot to dine with friends or family, Phat Pho is a must-visit. Be prepared for a short wait during peak hours, but trust me—it’s worth it!
