name: Abaca Baking Company® (Restaurant) - Base Line Center
address: Juana Osmeña St, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Papa Ace; rating=5; time=2025-04-30T00:33:06.993579Z; relative=12 months ago; text=A Hidden Gem for Latin American Flavors!

Abacá Baking Company’s Latin American Dinner Buffet (every Friday and Saturday, 5:30–9:30 PM at Baseline Center) is a must-visit for food lovers. The variety was impressive, from savory empanadas and slow-cooked meats and rich, flavorful stews. The atmosphere was cozy but lively, and the service was friendly and attentive.

Their dessert station also stood out, especially the leche flan and cakes. Everything was well-prepared and full of authentic flavor. For the price and quality, it's a fantastic weekend dining option. Highly recommended! || author=Xavier Blaise; rating=3; time=2026-02-19T08:08:34.632260272Z; relative=2 months ago; text=When I first walked into the restaurant, I honestly wasn’t even sure it was a restaurant — the interior felt quite cold and impersonal, which didn’t create the most welcoming first impression. That said, it was around 3pm, and after two hours on the ferry we were very hungry, so we decided to sit down anyway.

I have to say that the prices are among the highest I’ve encountered so far in the Philippines — possibly only comparable to places like the Hard Rock Café in Manila. I ordered a fairly simple Western dish, mac & cheese, but unfortunately it was disappointing and lacked flavour. My partner chose a Filipino dish, which was noticeably better.

One point worth mentioning is that I had to change my original order because the first dish I selected — priced at around 700 pesos — was not available as they were missing some of the vegetables. At that price point, I would have expected the full menu to be available.

We also weren’t able to access Wi-Fi. While I understand this may not be a restaurant primarily aimed at tourists, the location is still in a tourist area, so offering basic connectivity would be a welcome improvement.

Overall, the experience wasn’t terrible, but given the high prices, I would have expected a warmer atmosphere and more consistency in both food and service. || author=neil suacillo; rating=4; time=2025-06-07T11:41:55.344148Z; relative=10 months ago; text=They have a homey dining atmosphere. The place was quite neat, and the spacing between buffet side tables was just right—not too crowded, not too sparse.

The theme during our visit was Mexican cuisine, and they offered a good variety of dishes—possibly even a full range of traditional Mexican options. They also serve fresh shrimps, scallops, oyster and other sea food options while grilled beef and pork slices as additional choices, which added a hearty touch to the meal. To top it off, there was a generous selection of Abaca pastries and desserts that perfectly rounded out the experience. Will most likely come again || author=Anthony Divinagracia; rating=5; time=2024-09-03T03:09:25.492873Z; relative=a year ago; text=Stopped by this small cafe with some family members to have something to eat before heading to a ferry going to Bohol. The ham and cheese croissants were delicious. Their coffee is also great as well. The crew in this branch was kind and accommodating. I didn’t feel they were in a hurry even though we came in a few minutes before closing.

name: Sambal by Spice Fusion
address: Level 2, Alcoves Wing, ayala center cebu, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Kleinberg Fletcher Tabel; rating=5; time=2025-09-21T17:05:30.541590792Z; relative=7 months ago; text=I haven’t tried Spice Fusion yet, but I finally got to visit its sister restaurant, Sambal at Ayala Center Cebu, with my family—and I must say, it’s a gem! What makes Sambal exciting is how it brings together flavors from all around Asia and makes them work beautifully on one table.

For lunch, we ordered four dishes, one dessert, and a drink—and every single one was a hit. The Indian-inspired roti stuffed with savory beef was soft, fluffy, and surprisingly perfect when paired with Filipino comfort food like crispy pork belly and Shanghai meat rolls. The Singapore fresh lumpia deserves a special mention—it’s probably the best version of fresh lumpia I’ve tried. To round out the meal, we had refreshing green tea and finished with the mango massi, a tropical twist on the classic dessert that was simply delightful.

Beyond the food, the service really impressed us. The staff were warm, attentive, and quick to accommodate. The restaurant itself feels inviting—clean, well-organized, and with an ambiance that works whether you’re dining casually with family or catching up with friends.

I honestly don’t get the negative reviews floating around. Our experience was the exact opposite—it was flavorful, satisfying, and great value for money. If you’re in Cebu and want a taste of Asia on a single table, Sambal is absolutely worth a visit. || author=Juan Mariano; rating=3; time=2024-08-19T07:15:09.010107Z; relative=a year ago; text=Third-time visit. The food serving is decent and good for sharing. The price matches the food taste.  Inconsistencies- some dishes though promising on the menu fell flat in execution. Casual ambience is okay.

On the other hand, honest feedback for the service. It’s quite on the low edge. Management should make their staff attentive and keen to details (we had to follow up again on the takeout order after we paid the bill and noticed the staff seemed fazed that we had a takeout order which was already sitting and waiting to be served)

Overall, it’s a convenient option if you’re looking for a quick meal while shopping. || author=Errol; rating=4; time=2025-03-05T13:06:31.205373Z; relative=a year ago; text=For years, I’ve been wondering what happened to Spice Fusion, as they used to be in SM City. I only recently found out that they have rebranded as Sambal, now located in Ayala Cebu.

I’ve been craving their Golden Crispy Pork, which I consider their bestseller and a must-try. The last time we dined at Spice Fusion was before the pandemic, so it had been way too long.

When we visited Sambal, the first thing we noticed was the poor to no signal inside the restaurant. Of course, we ordered the Golden Crispy Pork along with Yang Chow Fried Rice. This time, we also tried their Battered Chicken and Laksa. We were pleasantly surprised by the Laksa—it came in a huge serving, probably good for 4-7 people! Since there were only three of us, we ended up taking some home.

We’ll definitely visit again another time! || author=Brent Go; rating=4; time=2022-03-21T16:35:27.030651Z; relative=4 years ago; text=Just like Asian Fusion, wonderful dishes and wonderful flavors!
I had the experience of having burnt curry with the Roti and a cockroach on my beef rice claypot, but the staff replaced them. I think there are too many staff and lack of actual customer service because they have more than 12 employees yet it took us time took call the attention of one. When they took my plate with the cockroach on it, I was not given another plate and utensils nor an apology for experiencing that one. They have a 3 percent service charge, yet they have a lot of staff not doing their best, so that's saying something with how management was done.
Another note on the curry: consider creating another batch as safety when current curry is burnt. It took us more than 30 minutes for the curry to arrive. I took the effort to wait that long just to finish that small bowl of curry goodnes.

Food was perfect. There is room for better customer service || author=Glenn Bermejo; rating=4; time=2022-05-10T11:35:33.411505Z; relative=3 years ago; text=Sambal Restaurant by Spice Fusion is a Malaysian restaurant which uniquely combines Malay, Indian and Singaporean influences, named after a chili sauce or paste, typically made from a mixture of a variety of chilli peppers with secondary ingredients such as shrimp paste, garlic, ginger, shallot, scallion, palm sugar, and lime juice which is applied and that sets the tone of their wonderfully delicious dishes. The food resembles Filipino dishes in many ways, but with a distinctive twist. Their food was delicious and appetizingly presented. The serves though, and there were a lot, we helpful but not very attentive. You have to summon them for service. The place is small, a little too cramped for comfort. Nevermind the large group in the back of the room which was boisterously loud. This place does not take reservations. It’s strictly first come first served.  It was empty when we came but was filled to capacity halfway through our meal.

The food is prepared by someone who thinks they can make japanese food by slathering mayonnaise and ketchup on it.

I ordered Volcano maki and they served me something that looked like a pretend sushi with drizzle of ketchup and mayonnaise and told me that it was volcano maki.

They served me a pseudo maki that doesn't look remotely like the one they had on the menu.

Also, they lack sanitary practices when preparing the food. Because japanese food is mostly raw, they were supposed toobserve the highest form of hygiene. What they do is that they slice all food on the same plastic chopping board that looked very dirty.

They were chopping fish, mango, etc, on the same dirty chopping board.

I got food poisoning when I went back to the hotel.

Better head down to Rustans and buy one of those premade Sushi rolls. They are way cheaper.

name: Barikata Ramen Bar
types: ramen_restaurant, japanese_restaurant, restaurant, point_of_interest, food, establishment
address: L4, 8W94+H97 ayala center cebu, Biliran Rd, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Aleksandr Kushnirenko; rating=1; time=2026-03-04T15:58:52.580397293Z; relative=a month ago; text=My dinner at Barikata Ramen Bar was one of the most disappointing restaurant experiences I’ve had in Cebu.

Service:
The staff were cold, inattentive, and completely disorganized. I ordered a beer and they forgot to bring a glass. What makes this even more absurd is that I was sitting literally one meter away from the bar counter, directly in front of the bartender. Despite that, I still had to repeatedly try to get someone’s attention. The waiters were constantly walking around the restaurant, yet nothing was actually happening. Even after asking for a glass, I still had to wait while they seemed to have plenty of time to casually chat with the manager.

Food:
Unfortunately, the food didn’t make up for the poor service. I ordered several dishes and everything was mediocre and forgettable. The biggest issue was the Chicken Nanban. When I cut into it, the chicken inside was red and clearly undercooked. I’ve attached a photo so people can see it for themselves. Undercooked chicken is not just unpleasant — it is a serious food safety concern. On top of that, the dish was mostly batter and skin with very little actual meat.

Billing:
I also noticed a discrepancy on the bill. The fries were listed as ₱80 on the menu but I was charged ₱100 on the receipt. It’s a small difference, but charges should match the advertised menu price. The bill also included an automatic 5% service charge, which feels very questionable considering how inattentive the service was.

Overall:
Between the poor service, mediocre food, undercooked chicken, and billing discrepancy, this was a very frustrating experience. I hope management takes this seriously, because serving undercooked chicken should never happen in any restaurant.

Unfortunately, based on this experience, I cannot recommend this place. || author=Pompeii Skindog; rating=4; time=2025-11-22T02:40:10.867811275Z; relative=5 months ago; text=It was my first time visiting this particular Japanese restaurant, and I’m genuinely impressed with how everything turned out. I’ve tried many Japanese places before, but this one stood out in its own unique way. The moment I walked in, the ambiance felt inviting clean interiors, cozy lighting, and a warm atmosphere that made the dining experience even better.

The food didn’t disappoint at all. Every dish tasted fresh and well-prepared, and you can really tell that the ingredients used were high-quality. I ordered some of my usual favorites so I could compare, and they delivered beautifully ramen with rich and comforting broth, sushi that was soft and fresh, and their katsudon that had the perfect crisp-to-tender ratio. It gave me a familiar Japanese taste but executed with its own signature style.

The service was also worth mentioning. The staff were friendly, polite, and quick to assist. They were attentive without hovering, which made the whole experience feel comfortable and pleasant.

For a first visit, this restaurant definitely made a strong impression. If you’re craving Japanese food that’s flavorful, satisfying, and worth every peso, this place is honestly a great pick. Looking forward to coming back and trying even more from their menu! || author=Peter Quinineza; rating=5; time=2023-06-18T05:37:48.289387Z; relative=2 years ago; text=Loved the place. Although this is a ramen bar but ambiance is modern. Ramen was good. Price is reasonable. And serving time was okay. Staff were attentive as well. Table spacing is good. author=dookie xave; rating=5; time=2026-02-23T23:17:21.332410070Z; relative=2 months ago; text=A Cebuano classic that continues to stand the test of time. 💛

I recently came back to MyJoy and what struck me the most is how it still delivers the things that made many of us grow up loving this place, real meals, great variety, and that familiar comforting taste.

My personal highlights:
🥗 Mango salad & fresh salad options — something you don’t usually see in a fast-food setup, and a big plus if you want a more balanced meal
🍝 Palabok — rich, flavorful, and very nostalgic
🌮 Tacos — still one of their signature must-orders
🍧 Halo-halo — the perfect classic Cebu treat to end the meal
🔥 And of course, their rice meals like the grilled pork belly, very filling and sulit

What makes MyJoy unique for me is that it’s more than the usual fast food , you can have Filipino comfort food, grilled dishes, salads, and desserts all in one place. It’s a complete, busog, and budget-friendly dining experience.

The space feels familiar and welcoming, and there’s something special about seeing different generations still eating here from students to families.

In a city filled with new and trendy spots, MyJoy remains a true Cebuano original.

Still comforting. Still sulit. Still ours. || author=Shawn Ray Villamor (4G7SRV); rating=4; time=2025-08-10T04:34:17.969602353Z; relative=8 months ago; text=This is my first time and her second time eating in "My Joy". I was skeptical at first but it turned out to be a satisfying lunch. I don't like salad but it may turn out to be good for other people.

Food was served quickly and was still warm.

Pork belly 8/10 - Was warm and tender, the portion was just right and the barbeque sauce it came with a familiar taste (if you normally order barbeque outside)

Spaghetti 7/10 - Comparing this to jollibee's spaghetti, the sauce is lighter and not so strong compared to McDonald's. It's somewhat in the middle.

Fried Chicken 6/10 - The chicken was crispy, tender and well cooked. It's kind of like a home-made fried chicken but the flavor could be slightly improved. The size of the chicken could've also been slightly bigger. The sauce it comes with does compliment it but I would prefer that delicious, juicy pinoy fried chicken and gravy combo that we're all used to.

btw the iced tea was great. I still don't know why it's called 'shake' tho lol. Anyway 🤷🏽‍♂️

The place is clean, the staff were smiling and the atmosphere could definitely be associated with the word "Joy". || author=Adrian; rating=2; time=2025-07-10T07:46:37.930639298Z; relative=9 months ago; text=Beef taco served cold beef was like a sauce not ground beef...taste was not good. author=Jojo Tiongko; rating=5; time=2025-12-29T11:01:58.834663697Z; relative=3 months ago; text=If it’s your first time at Kubkai Khan Ayala (Ayala Center Cebu), grab a bowl first and don’t hesitate to ask a waiter for help—they’ll guide you on the best meat/veggie/noodle combinations and how to balance the sauces so your stir-fry comes out perfect.
Kubkai Khan is a solid go-to if you want fast, filling, and customizable Mongolian-style food in a comfortable, clean space. The flow is simple: choose your meat, vegetables, noodles, and sauces, then the staff cooks it hot and fresh right in front of you—so you get a meal that’s made to your taste every time.
What stands out here is the variety and value. There are plenty of mix-and-match options, the cooking station moves efficiently, and the crew stays on top of refills and service even when it’s busy. It’s also great for groups because everyone can build their own bowl, from mild to spicy.
If you’re looking for a Mongolian buffet in Ayala Center Cebu that’s convenient, satisfying, and easy to enjoy with friends or family, Kubkai Khan Ayala is a dependable choice. || author=Errol; rating=3; time=2025-04-23T20:07:02.785849Z; relative=a year ago; text=Hungry?! author=MyLifeWithTheSun; rating=4; time=2025-12-09T01:59:21.296559132Z; relative=4 months ago; text=We recently dined at Lugang Café. The food was okay—nothing particularly bad, but nothing that really stood out either. The ambiance was nice enough, and the place had a steady crowd, which is always a good sign.

What affected the experience, though, was the service. One server dismissed us when we tried to ask something, saying she would get back to us later, but unfortunately never did. It left us a bit disappointed, especially since service can really shape the overall dining experience.

Would still consider coming back to try other dishes—hopefully with a better service encounter next time. || author=Cebu Food Critic; rating=4; time=2025-09-30T12:23:08.699894981Z; relative=6 months ago; text=First time trying Taiwanese cuisine. They are located in SM Seaside mall in Cebu. The restaurant in fairly new and has a viewing window were you can watch the chefs making  dumplings.
The staff seemed uninterested once we were seated. See a lot of the staff nesting, talking amongst themselves.
We went through the extensive menu and asked the waitress for assistance, when she did. Still I was hoping for more information than she gave me on the dishes.
Ordered 4 dishes. Pork dumplings were spicy, great and plentiful. Mapa Tofu was also spicy and good. The pork barbeque was better than I anticipated. The final dish was Machang forgotten by the staff ( had the flag down a different server to get them to fullfil our orders) and I wish we never ordered it. Some sticky brown rice pouch thing stuffed with mushrooms was nasty.
Overall I would try again, lower my service expectations andmaybe there buffet. || author=anne Bequilla; rating=5; time=2025-11-11T04:07:45.936422528Z; relative=5 months ago; text=love the feast all you can in Lugang. XLB has been my long time fave and its good that we get to have an unli serve here. though, the waiting time is a bit long when you order for 2nd round or 3rd round due to a lot of customers. price is right for me || author=Sora; rating=1; time=2025-11-11T23:46:36.902206502Z; relative=5 months ago; text=I was excited to try the restaurant because it looked nice, but the experience wasn’t satisfying. The food was too salty, and it overpowered the natural taste of the ingredients. It was also quite expensive for what was served, so I felt it wasn’t worth the price. The place has potential, but they really need to work on improving the flavor and portion quality.

DTF is way better. || author=Jennifer Beck (Jenni); rating=1; time=2025-10-18T08:54:57.136785637Z; relative=6 months ago; text=Not worth your visit! Take it from my experience and eat somewhere else, save your time and money!

I decided to try Lugang Café shortly after its opening because of the hype i saw on social media. I really love Taiwanese food and especially wanted to have unlimited xiao long bao. I was drawn to their ₱688 unlimited promo (dim sum, appetizers, rice, noodles, soup, and iced tea, or ₱888 with one additional main dish) sounded like a great deal, and I was genuinely excited to experience it.

First impressions were actually good. The restaurant looked inviting and classy.  Despite it being quite busy, we were seated immediately. I appreciated that they asked about food allergies upfront — that’s not something every restaurant does. Ordering was simple and organized, similar to the system at Din Tai Fung, where you write down your selections. I was especially looking forward to the unlimited xiao long bao, and the server mentioned it would take about 15–20 minutes and it did not feel we were waiting that long for the first order to come through. When we asked for ginger or extra ice for the drinks, it was brought quickly, though getting the servers’ attention overall was a bit challenging.

Unfortunately, the food experience went downhill from there.
We upgraded to try a main dish for the  ₱888 promo and ordered the spare ribs in plum sauce. It turned out to be a super disappointing dish. It lacked the rich, tangy plum flavor I expected and was unexpectedly topped with powdered sugar, yes powdered sugar, making it taste more like a dessert or a donut rather than a savory entrée. It felt like a joke to me.

The garlic buttered shrimp was one of the few dishes that met expectations — well cooked and flavorful. The pork wonton in chili oil was enjoyable but lacked the depth and spice I associate with this dish. The tofu with preserved egg and pork floss was an interesting experience, a little out of my comfort zone but okay.

The rest of the food, however, was either underwhelming or unpleasant. The xiao long bao and dumplings had an off-putting, musky odor — the kind of smell I can only describe as “sweaty,” possibly from rendered fat or unclean steamers in such a high-turnover setting cooking environment (being unlimited dim sum). I usually tolerate food I don’t enjoy, but this time, I couldn’t even swallow it. Had to spit it out. The fried rice and corn soup were unremarkable. The noodles never arrived despite three polite follow-ups with servers. The Vietnamese egg rolls were burnt, hard, and lacked freshness. By this point, I had lost my appetite. Also have to mention, the servers kept bringing us things we did not order, which seemed like they did not know their table numbers that well, or they just have a poor management system in terms of organization.

What left a lasting negative impression wasn’t just the food — it was also the service at the end of the meal. Even as it was so busy, several servers repeatedly stared at our table without ever approaching to ask how things were. A simple, direct check-in (“How’s everything/How‘s the food?”) would have made a big difference. Instead, the passive looks made me feel uncomfortable and unwelcome to dine here. Very unprofessional and its just not how you treat your customers!

I also noted their no-leftovers policy, which I understand for unlimited promos, but it added unnecessary pressure to finish food I genuinely couldn’t enjoy.

Overall, this experience was disappointing on multiple levels — from food quality to service. I came in excited and left feeling let down. For the same price point, I could have had a far better experience elsewhere (Din Tai Fung for similar price, or Binondo‘s Best has far out way better xiao long bao if that is what you are looking for), especially when comparing both food and service quality. Just don‘t fall for the hype and unlimited promo.

Food 1/5
Service 1/5
Atmosphere 3/5

sample_reviews:

You really do get what you pay for.

The miso soup was disappointingly diluted. Watery, bland, and lacking depth. The three shrimp tempura looked cute but had zero crunch, which defeats the point of tempura. The salad was drowned in what tasted like familiar Kewpie sesame mayo, nothing memorable. Dessert was the most puzzling. A gelatinous chocolate pudding that honestly looked like dinuguan. Not appetizing at all.

The so-called wagyu steak looked promising on the plate, but that’s where it ended. It didn’t taste or feel like wagyu. The texture reminded me of tofu pretending to be beef, swimming in soy sauce.

I wouldn’t come back. || author=Karelle Juarez; rating=5; time=2025-09-23T13:54:05.897240931Z; relative=7 months ago; text=Was craving for authentic Japanese food post-Japan trip and came upon this restaurant in Seaside.

name: Chili's SM Seaside, Cebu

sample_reviews:
author=Andreah Alunan; rating=5; time=2026-01-27T14:56:12.902652601Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Chili’s never disappoints! The food is always delicious, and the service is top-notch. I especially love the “All For Me” meals, they’re perfect for a satisfying solo feast. Definitely a go-to spot for a great dining experience! || author=Anne Ann; rating=3; time=2025-11-14T23:20:21.492760670Z; relative=5 months ago; text=We went to Chili’s because a few of my friends said the food at their SM City Cebu branch was delicious. They also mentioned that it’s a bit pricey but worth it. Since the SM Seaside branch is closer to us, my friend and I with our two kids decided to have lunch there.

We ordered three of their ₱380 set meals, where you can choose 1 appetizer, 1 main dish (we added ₱150 each for our main dish), and a drink — iced tea or lemonade. We got the chips and salsa, loaded boneless chicken (added ₱75), house side salad, boneless BBQ chicken, countryside pork belly, grilled salmon, Ribeye steak with rice and drinks.

The drinks came first, followed by the chips and salsa. We really liked the salsa — it went perfectly with the chips.

When the rest of the food came, everything looked yummy, The boneless bbq chicken is yummy cooked well and savory except for the sides ; but most dishes were too salty. The pork belly was tough and rubbery , and both the rice and salmon were also salty. I usually enjoy salty food, but this time it wasn’t good. We also ordered their ribeye steak with rice for ₱2,050, but the presentation wasn’t appetizing, and the steak was tough and dry — definitely not worth the price. We didn’t even finish our food.

While we were in the middle of chatting, a staff member suddenly asked how our food was without saying “excuse me,” and we had just started eating, so we couldn’t give any feedback yet.

On the positive side, the food arrived quickly.

For us, we don’t think we’ll come back again. || author=Q Villanueva; rating=4; time=2025-12-09T07:56:24.729614701Z; relative=4 months ago; text=we tried the P380 meals and it was worth for it's price. Will come back to try other combo meals. || author=C G; rating=4; time=2025-09-24T11:54:22.749427419Z; relative=7 months ago; text=Came here for the margarita drink -unli drinks - came out a bit tipsy and kinda disappointed.

The strawberry also doesn't have any kind of strawberry taste. My friend got the mango strawberry mix which was delicious, so order that one.

I tried my best to finish it all, but lesson learned to be proactive with the bartender.

The classic nachos was a hit tho! Love it!

They have other drinks I still want to try, but maybe not the unli drinks anymore. || author=Christopher Oon; rating=4; time=2025-10-17T11:24:29.597170364Z; relative=6 months ago; text=Nice place with good food and accommodating staff. name: Seafood and Ribs Warehouse SM Seaside Cebu
address: 3rd floor, SM Seaside City Cebu, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=G; rating=1; time=2024-08-18T16:07:14.835485Z; relative=a year ago; text=If you're planning a date, avoid this place.

Food:
Battered chicken (285)
- not fully cooked, still has blood
- smells like it was cooked in burnt oil
- no seasoning, tastes bland
- sauce is just plainly ketchup

Ribs (295)
- taste like frozen meat, smells rancid
- sauce tastes like ketchup with sugar and pepper, nothing special
- It was shown on the menu that there was mashed potato as a side dish but we were not served with the mashed potato. Instead, just a small portion of rice. WE WERE NOT INFORMED.

Service/Staff:
- we got served LAST, and the staff were not attentive despite that there was a line and the customer before us got accommodated FIRST
- 1hr waiting for the food to be served, until the mall closed
- the bill and the food was served together, the waiter did not even ask if we need anything, we did not have our drink at the moment, but when we requested for the drinks we were told that there was no available iced tea and the waiter suggested the canned drinks, however after he took our order the waiter was called by the other customer and requested for an “iced tea” which they got served even though the waiter told us it was not available
- we lost our appetite so we asked for the food to be packed, not expecting they would literally just put in a plastic like it was for your pets to eat at home

Cleanliness:
- cockroaches roaming everywhere on the table
-  spoon and fork were not washed properly, the fork still has a grain of rice, and the spoon has sauce
- plates were not dried or wiped and basically there’s still water on the plates along with the food

Basically, not worth the price and time. They charged too much for the food that tasted below average. Better to eat in the streets like a food eatery than go here.

We will not go back here anyways, it ruined our appetite.

DO BETTER. || author=Philip Raymond Lim; rating=1; time=2024-12-29T06:47:20.055464Z; relative=a year ago; text=Food cooking might be ok but, cockroach alert!!!! A cockroach creeped out from my food. Can I give zero stars????

It’s cockroach everywhere and then some cockroach flew on my dining table!!! Killed one in front of me and another one surfaced. I’m never coming back. Disgusting. Just barf!!! 🤢🤮😷

Paging DOH alert! Unsanitary for everybody. || author=Leo Roffus Divinagracia; rating=5; time=2024-08-04T18:31:55.437051Z; relative=a year ago; text=Seafood and Ribs Warehouse in SM Seaside is renowned as one of the best "PALUTO" spots in Cebu. Despite the top-notch quality, the prices are very reasonable, making it accessible to many. The welcoming and attentive crew ensure a comfortable and enjoyable dining experience. The friendly and lively atmosphere makes it perfect for gatherings with family and friends, adding to the overall enjoyment of the meal. || author=Carlo Andrew Olano; rating=5; time=2022-03-17T14:26:43.927035Z; relative=4 years ago; text=If you love feesh seafood cooked to your liking at an affordable price then I can recommend Seafood and Ribs Warehouse. Service - 2/10👎

We entered the restaurant because it wasnt filled. The service throughout was POOR.

Firstly, They served our food so LATE, they mentioned it would take 15 Minutes and yet it almost took 30 minutes and a follow up to the waiters for our food to come. Mind you, THE RESTAURANT WASNT PACKED.

Secondly, we were neglected in our time in Hey Wok. We ordered 3 Extender Promo Meals, which included a Quail Egg Siomai, Steamed Rice, and Iced Tea.

They served tables before us WHEN WE WERE FIRST. They failed to also mention that They Ran Out of ICED TEA.

Additionally they didnt even serve us any drinks until we were done with our meal. Totally uncalled for a restaurant.

We were really dissapointed, we gave Hey Wok a shot. Wish we never did.

Food - 3/10👎

The Hainanese Rice with Bokchoy was good but it mentioned it would come with Bokchoy, It was just rice and chicken with complete absence of Bokchoy...

Quail Egg Siomai and Fried Wontons were good.

The Empress Roll was had a weird aftertaste and had a mild fishy flavor

The Fried Chicken was good but too oily, BUT some were UNDERCOOKED and had blood in it. So a big No No

Steamed Rice was ok, I liked the beefy flavor even though I ordered a Pork Steamed Rice....additionally the onions gave it a good flavor as well that isnt present in other alternatives

The Sesame Milktea we ordered HAD A BUG ON THE TOP FOAM, and we arent even sure this is the Sesame Drink. We think the waiter just lied probably cause they didnt even have it.

Hakaw and the Iced Lemon Black Tea wasnt good, due to the fact they didnt even serve us it when we ordered it. What a complete JOKE. We told them to cancel it nalang since we finished our meal and they didnt seem to plan on serving it to us. They had the audacity to still include it in our Bill, taxpayers pay for ghost projects, I guess its ok for us to pay for GHOST FOOD💀

Overall would not recommend. Could have gone to the more well known dimsum places. || author=Hazel Sevilleta; rating=5; time=2025-11-16T09:34:42.165530156Z; relative=5 months ago; text=The food was so good and affordable! sample_reviews:
author=Olivia Smith; rating=5; time=2026-03-06T00:07:19.150336583Z; relative=a month ago; text=when I visited with my boyfriend. We were both craving something different, so we decided to try Thai food, and it turned out to be a really good choice.

The food was delicious and full of flavor. Everything we ordered tasted authentic and well prepared, and the portions were satisfying. It was nice sharing the dishes and trying different flavors together.

The place also had a nice and casual vibe, making it a comfortable spot to enjoy a meal. The staff were friendly and the service was smooth. I got the minced pork kraopao which was really spicy for me. I asked them to have the spice mild but I generally have a low spice tolerance so I guess that's why. We also ordered some pad thai, sweet and salty pork and some chicken I forgot the name. Overall it was pretty good and pricing coincides with other restaurants in the mall. Would love to come back again the try more food. || author=N Man; rating=5; time=2025-11-07T14:15:09.493384740Z; relative=5 months ago; text=The taste of the food was exactly what I tried in Bangkok. The ingredients are so fresh, so aromatic. I love the way they cook PadThai sooooo much. The baked tiger prawn with vermicelli, pork jowl salad, and 3-flavored fish are my favorites! If you crave Thai food, this one is highly recommended!! It’s superb 😋 || author=Sherlyn Lois Rivera; rating=3; time=2026-02-16T05:02:23.569760979Z; relative=2 months ago; text=I was craving for Thai food and thought of trying out Co Limited. Since I've seen their store in centralworld, I thought they would serve authentic Thai food.

I ordered Crispy Pork Krapow which was a bit disappointing. It was unlike the ones I had in Thailand. The pork was too oily and tasted burnt—far from the "lechon kawali-like" crispy pork.

My companion ordered Pad Thai and Wonton. I tried the Pad Thai and it was okay but the sourness overpowered, rather than being sweet. I have not tried the wonton as it was also sour (as per my companion).

The only order I loved was the mango sticky rice. The consistency of the coconut milk was perfect, sticky rice was perfectly cooked, and the mangoes were sweet! Would come back for this.

Service was slow—from ordering up to billing out. Hope it can be improved. || author=Chad Alsay; rating=5; time=2025-11-16T03:53:22.674092462Z; relative=5 months ago; text=Just finished eating here and grabe… solid kaayo. I ordered the grilled ox tongue, stir-fried morning glory, pad thai with crispy pork belly, and their four-tube garlic fried rice with ribeye steak and onsen egg — all super worth it.

The ox tongue was tender and smoky, the morning glory had that fresh, crunchy stir-fried bite, and the pad thai with crispy pork belly? Lami kaayo, perfect balance of sweet, salty, and spicy. But the real standout was the garlic fried rice with ribeye and onsen egg — rich, garlicky, and the steak was surprisingly soft and flavorful.

Fast service, clean place, and the flavors taste authentically Thai without being overwhelming.

Highly recommend! Will definitely come back. 🙌🏼🔥

author=Aaron Nuñez; rating=5; time=2025-10-25T15:59:46.663652735Z; relative=6 months ago; text=Paolito’s is always a classic Cebuano favorite, a go-to place when you’re craving comforting local dishes done right. The pochero was a bit on the salty side this time, but still hearty and flavorful enough to hit the spot.

Always a nice place to come back to for a taste of good old Filipino STK classics that feel like home. 🇵🇭🍴 || author=Jan Christopher Lauronilla; rating=1; time=2026-01-11T08:45:27.249834197Z; relative=3 months ago; text=We ordered at 2pm and waited for 50 MINUTES! If we didn't followed up our order then it won't be served. Simply because they FORGOT.

Then they served all the plates cold. The rice was pretty much marshmallow cold.

They took the plates and just MICROWAVED them. Not serving fresh.

The head/supervisor/owner told me it is a first come, first serve. Which contradicts as the food is cold. I also found her sleeping on the COUNTER. infront where the costumers can see. She said she has a stomach pain or some sort. She should have just designate a crew to supervise in lieu of her.

I asked the waitress what happened. She said it's also because plenty of crew went out/ took a break even only a few left to serve the costumers.

The owner told me if i'd like to cancel. Then we wouldn't have anything to eat for the next hour to find another restaurant.

Their service water is also just in a cheap iced tea plastic pitcher without even the cap. The glasses has many cracks. For a known restaurant not even buying a decent equipments. Tsk.

We had a senior citizen and a mother of a baby to feed.

The bill even costs 3.4k php. || author=Karley Tran; rating=5; time=2025-12-29T12:19:43.256069568Z; relative=3 months ago; text=As we arrived in Cebu, we were surprised to see there were a lot of American and other Asian chains. Tuna jaw was excellent and is a must-try,  halo halo has generous portion and was so good, the grilled scallops and squid was also good! We were so happy to find this place and left very satisfied! || author=Judith May Bayawa; rating=1; time=2022-09-13T09:28:20.832438Z; relative=3 years ago; text=We ate here last Sunday, 09/11/22.

1. Service - 7/10 the waiters are nice but took long to get their attention.

2. Food - 5/5

-Fried chicken were raw.
-Shrimp were coated with thick layer of flour.

-Calamares - RAW
-Mixed Vegetable - Excellent
-Pork sisig - good
-Pork barbecue - good
-Baked scallops - Perfect || author=Kyla G; rating=3; time=2025-02-02T12:44:44.994964Z; relative=a year ago; text=A bit disappointed with the food. The tuna belly was overcooked, while the Pochero was too salty. I liked the kinilaw and their halo-halo.

author=Gigiwa; rating=2; time=2026-01-14T21:54:01.699084987Z; relative=3 months ago; text=VERY NOT RECOMMENDABLE!!!

It was my first time eating here, and I didn’t expect the wait to take over an hour. We arrived around 2:00 PM for lunch and were only able to eat after 3:00 PM, when we were advised that waiting time is just 20-25 minutes. This was very inconvenient, especially since my in-laws with us are senior citizens.

The food was okay overall, but I did not like the pork sinigang. I’m not sure how it was seasoned, but it was not good for my taste. The garlic rice was also disappointing and overpriced at ₱95 per serving. There was no platter option, and it was just plain rice with garlic on top—not even fried rice. For the price, it felt unreasonable. But when it comes to the food, it really lacked the satisfaction I was hoping for. It’s okay, but I think there’s a lot of room for improvement, especially in terms of flavor. Hopefully, they can tweak their recipes a bit to match the taste expectations of customers. || author=Kate Pal; rating=5; time=2025-03-09T08:29:25.570753Z; relative=a year ago; text=Cozy Café in Laguna with Great Food and Family-Friendly Touches

This café in Laguna is an absolute gem! The spacious and cozy ambiance made it a perfect place to relax and enjoy a great meal. We loved how well-maintained and inviting the space was, creating the perfect balance between comfort and charm.

The food was fantastic, with a well-curated menu that showcased both classic and unique flavors. We were especially happy to see staples like tinolang manok, beef caldereta, and pancit, all cooked to perfection with rich, authentic tastes. Each dish was hearty and flavorful, reminding us of comforting home-cooked meals.

Another plus was their kid-friendly setup—having a kiddie chair available made dining so much more convenient for families. It’s always great to find a café that’s welcoming to all ages.

Overall, this café offers delicious food, a relaxing ambiance, and thoughtful service. Whether you’re here for a casual meal or a cozy coffee break, it’s a place worth visiting in Laguna. We’ll definitely be back! || author=Shannon Easter; rating=1; time=2026-02-18T10:29:41.709913964Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Me and my girlfriend went in and was seated, they brought us both glass of water but nothing else. sample_reviews: author=Thomas Amparo; rating=4; time=2025-11-10T13:46:58.332029817Z; relative=5 months ago; text=It is inside the Mall, 3rd floor I think. Just come across it whild circling the mall (and im really looking for a good dinner). It hits all things im really looking for that night, chinese food - beef hofan. And yes they have in on their menu. I ordered 1 Beef Hofan (300+php) and advertised to be good for 2-3 pax. Being the only dish, I can be for solo. I also liked the variety of side dishes (banchan), which complemented the grilled meat very well. The staff were attentive and helpful, making sure the grill was changed when needed and that everything was served properly. Overall, the food quality and service were very satisfying. A can of coke costs 99 pesos, that’s DOUBLE of what it usually costs in other restaurants (even movie theaters don’t overprice THAT MUCH). Also I would have loved to taste their fried chicken but I didn’t know they had them included in the package we chose because they only gave us the raw meat so that’s a shame. It's a bit pricey maybe because of the meat they are serving but was satisfied with my overall dining experience. Aside from the unlimited menu or set, they also have meals available if you want one specific meal (ranging from 250-500pesos). Staff also disinfected the tables and since this is inside the mall, vaccination card was required.

Update (January 2023):
Dining experience seem to be lacking since personally service was not smooth. Staff was inattentive and too absorbed whenever we called them, they can't hear or notice us calling out even if we were near the cash registry. sample_reviews:
author=Franz Buns Zaitsev; rating=3; time=2025-08-11T17:50:11.975075190Z; relative=8 months ago; text=The food choices are great, I enjoyed a lot of food selection, the staff were nice and friendly, I have my complains. First is our table, the fire was very slow to cook meatier meat and doesn't cook well, it was very dated you need to either repair the fireplace or just replace it. Second thing please watch the customers all the time, I saw people getting ice cream while putting their whole arm inside the ice cream and nobody bats an eye about it. Anyway everything else is a good experience for a buffet place. || author=Gaynor Laws; rating=5; time=2025-09-28T06:18:54.558241838Z; relative=7 months ago; text=Yaki Mix has a really good buffet. The grill in the tables is a really nice bonus. || author=Danielle Francesco; rating=1; time=2025-11-02T12:59:09.676659481Z; relative=5 months ago; text=To make things short, barely 15 minutes after we sat down, we saw a few cockroaches wriggling out of the grill. We talked to the waitress and asked that the grill be replaced. At this time, we did not take any pictures or videos of the incident since we wanted to resolve this without ranting (what I’m doing right now) in social media, and we also tried to understand that it was a food establishment so maaaybbeee a few mishaps are bound to happen, however, it was evident that the cleanliness of the place was subpar. We shrugged it off because shit happens sometimes.

We asked the staff if we could just move tables but we were not granted this request during the first time. They simply changed the grill and after this, we still tried to continue eating but we were quickly losing our appetites because frankly, who would want to share their meal with cockroaches? I already had my doubts that maybe the grill wasn’t the problem, it was the place itself.

A few minutes later, another cockroach appeared and, unbeknownst to us, made its way to our plate of food. This one was larger. We noticed it once it was already on top of the fried chicken I got. This time, we spoke to a staff member and stated that we’d like to just get out since this was the second time cockroaches sashayed to our food. They asked us if we wanted to change tables, but I think this option was only applicable during the first time. They informed us that the appearance of cockroaches was because they performed a pest control activity the night before. I didn’t know what I was supposed to do with that information. Was I supposed to just chill and drink my iced tea with my newfound cockroach buddies? Maybe I was supposed to shrug it off and thank the pest control that the cockroaches were eating my food.

After all that, I did my best to keep it cool and I’d like to think that given the situation, I did. The staff gave us the bill and made us pay after witnessing first hand the cockroaches on our table. They really just…stood there.

This is charged to experience. Nothing I can do about it now. I guess I’d just like to share this because this transpired inside a mall, where prices aren’t that cheap. I’ll make it a habit to check the cleanliness of an establishment first before making the decision to eat there. Upon checking the reviews from Google, there were also a lot of people who reported seeing cockroaches in the venue. || author=Lem Mendrez; rating=2; time=2025-08-08T03:39:23.140163942Z; relative=8 months ago; text=The food and ambiance were decent, but the service left much to be desired. We chose this place to celebrate the birthdays of two friends, and while the staff acknowledged the celebrants and even checked their IDs for validation, they didn't extend the same cheerful celebration that they offered to others. When I followed up, they simply said, 'It's done, they won’t sing or cheer anymore,' even though only a few minutes had passed. It was disappointing, as we specifically chose this venue to make the occasion special. While it’s not a huge deal, it would’ve been great if they prioritized making their customers feel valued, especially on significant days like this. Hays || author=Jan Christopher Lauronilla; rating=3; time=2026-01-15T10:31:22.534562691Z; relative=3 months ago; text=Food: The chef told me it's been a long time since they received siomai.

The desert, chocolate. There is an obvious fingerprint on top of some so it means they are usiny bare hands setting up the food. I showed to the crew and they changed it from the big box in the fridge.

Cleanliness: the drinks area is usually wet. There are no absorbent mats. Just a big blower on the floor.

It's my 3rd time eating here and there are always small cockroaches. 2 in different occasions crawled on the table. Too many places they can breed. Lots of small humid pocket areas and most likely not cleaned thoroughly.

Staff were welcoming and nice but at times were unattentive, even when the restaurant was primarily empty. Prices are fair for the dishes, though at the time of writing this, some dishes such as the kare-kare were not available for whatever reason. I recommend the garlic rice platter paired with the crispy pata or crispy pork belly. Did not try dessert but have also heard of their halo-halo being excellent. Maybe this was an off-day for service, but give this place a try. || author=Leslie Lidot; rating=3; time=2023-11-11T11:32:56.990514Z; relative=2 years ago; text=3/5 Food - Small serving for its price. When I go to restaurants, I always have that one memorable dish that keeps me coming back. But this time, I don't have one.

Lechon Kawali - expected it to be bigger but as you can see in the photos, it's just two thin cuts of pork belly.

Beef Bulalo - the bowl was too small to accomodate the soup! Could have placed it in a bigger container. It overflows when we tried scooping for the soup. Tastes good though.

Pansit Guisado - most decent out of the dishes we've had. My mom also enjoyed this, so I'm gonna give it an extra +1 point rating. Not too salty, and the veggies are crispy & not overcooked.

Baked Scallops - it's good but the butter was too salty.

Bagnet Crisps - expected too much for this. It's crispy when served but the flavor is just so-so. The spiced vinegar makes it better somehow.

Ube Halo-halo - tastes like its name - we supposedly wanted to try the Classic yet they don't have it so this is just our second choice. They say it's the best halo-halo. But, nothing too special.

3/5 Service - Served the food all at once which was great but staff weren't so polite.

3/5 Ambiance - Expect this place to get very crowded and noisy. Children running around and people walking infront of your table. The place is kinda small. Tables were so close to each other. The CR also needs cleaning every hour - the strong, pungent smell lingers inside the room.

author=Angel Oliquino; rating=2; time=2025-05-08T14:25:49.499099Z; relative=11 months ago; text=We tried this buffet that costs ₱498 per person with unlimited food, but honestly, it wasn’t worth it for the price. Most of the dishes didn’t seem freshly cooked—some looked and tasted like they were reheated or had been sitting out for a while. You kind of feel like lugi ka sa imong ₱498, especially if you're expecting good quality and variety.

The food presentation wasn’t that appealing either. You can't really try everything, not because there’s so much, but because the food doesn’t look appetizing enough to go back for more. The kakanin was bland, and even though there were different dishes, the taste didn’t really stand out.

Yes, we got full, and for that, it’s okay—but if you're coming here for flavor, freshness, or a satisfying experience, then don't expect too much. After all, it’s unlimited, and of course, they also need to make a profit, so we understand that. Still, a little more effort in flavor and food quality would make the price feel more worth it.

If you're just looking for quantity over quality, this might be fine for you. name: RACKS SM Seaside Cebu
types: barbecue_restaurant, diner, steak_house, restaurant, point_of_interest, food, establishment
address: Seaside Avenue SM Seaside City, Cebu City, 6000 Cebu, Philippines
sample_reviews:
author=Charissa Mae Amper; rating=5; time=2026-02-15T18:16:43.060422415Z; relative=2 months ago; text=We had our Valentine’s dinner here & it was special because it was also my papa's birthday. This was only our 2nd time dining here, the 1st was years ago when they had just newly opened in SM Seaside, and we came back because I still remember how creamy and delicious their carbonara was.

Although they usually have fewer customers on regular days (coz of the many competitors in SM Seaside), they were absolutely packed on Valentine’s Day. From a customer’s perspective, I think they did a GREAT job making sure they had enough staff on duty. Despite how busy it was, the service was very attentive. We didn’t have to follow up on our orders at all. The timing of the food was also excellent, just as we were about to finish one batch, the next dishes were served. It really showed they were paying attention.

We were a bit disappointed that the carbonara was already sold out (we were really looking forward to it!), and their house blend iced tea wasn’t available so we were served coke instead. The bolognese spaghetti was delicious and definitely made up for it. All the food we ordered was great.

Maybe they could prepare more stock for bestsellers like the carbonara, especially during peak days like Valentine’s. But overall, we’re happy to see that even after so many years, they still serve consistently good food and maintain great service.

Thank you! ❤️ || author=James T; rating=3; time=2026-01-29T10:30:04.072004556Z; relative=2 months ago; text=Meal was decent and considering it was in SM Seaside Mall the prices are acceptable. What is unacceptable is receiving a meal that doesn't look like the photo presented. I'd say another "reality versus expectations" episode.  I ordered the blackened chicken salad and received my salad with no chicken at all, just bacon chunks and bread crumbs that tasted on the stale side, but bearable. I immediately brought the lack of chicken to the server's attention and he took it back to the kitchen. It was returned 7 minutes later (something like that) with three thin slices of blackened chicken added to the top, while the menu photo has double that amount of meat pictured. I received salad dressing in a separate bowl as requested. It was thick, more like a sandwich spread.  Greens were of a good color and flavor but not super crisp. It would have been nice to have some other variety of vegetable mixed in like tomatoes but it was a Ceasar salad not Garden, so only the chicken, old tasting bacon chunks, and bread crumbs. In my opinion, not worth 425 pesos for this dish. My friend had a chicken, rice, what goes for ribs here, french fries and a side salad with a piece of bread, and was satisfied with the meal giving it four out of five stars.  She didn't like the sauce, complaining that it was too sweet. Coming from a Filipina with a sweet tooth, that means it must have been really sweet lol.  902 pesos for two people ($15.25) including one Coke was not so bad all things considered but beware of the Blackened Chicken Salad

Btw, music piped in on cheap speakers was a bit loud and annoying, but this is the norm in the Philippines and I'm from the West.. || author=anne Bequilla; rating=3; time=2025-08-31T07:10:58.314852402Z; relative=7 months ago; text=i love how warmth their customer service is. very accommodating. what i partially love is the food. i ordered their rib eye steak and i ask them to cook it medium rare, it arrived medium well and the steak was a bit dry. good thing, their steak sauce is superb. the salad that came with the steak was fresh.  i ordered a separate caesar salad and i feel like it was not fresh, the lollo rosso lettuce taste bitter. also ordered their aglio e olio, the pasta is okay but the bacon along with it was very salty. not that I am overreacting but yes, i was able to enjoy the pasta but not the bacon toppings cut in big size  and very salty. the chicken basket was okay. || author=Chichi Guzman; rating=4; time=2025-12-27T03:37:37.210771897Z; relative=4 months ago; text=The food quality remains good. I had my first taste of Racks at SM Megamall back in the 2000s.  The meat is very tender, the sides complement the main dish.  I particularly love the taste of their mashed potato.

The service though takes a while.  They are undermanned. We made at least 4 follow ups for our food to be served.  Also, we ended up asking for our bill at the counter. And waiting for a good 15mins to get our card back.  I saw some clients do the same. So if you are in a hurry and short on patience, this is not the place for you. || author=Rick Bartel; rating=5; time=2025-03-23T21:26:25.364083Z; relative=a year ago; text=I always LOVE eating at a Racks restaurant any time I have the chance. They have the most AWESOME...TENDER...FALL-OFF THE BONE RIBS you could ever imagine! And this branch in SM Seaside Cebu was no different...an absolutely great dining experience with awesome food and a great staff at a fair price...what more can you ask for?

name: Kuzina Guadalupe

sample_reviews:
author=Darrell Cerna; rating=1; time=2025-01-05T08:36:18.347780Z; relative=a year ago; text=This restaurant offers good value and a lovely sea view, but unfortunately, our experience was marred by several cleanliness issues.  Our table, plates, and utensils were oily.  The food was average in taste, but the bulalo and ribs were not fresh; the bulalo, in particular, had an unpleasant smell and taste, rendering it inedible.

Furthermore, a staff member, when asked for a knife, wiped it on a coworker's arm jokingly before using a tissue.  This was deeply concerning.

While the prices are reasonable, significant improvements in cleanliness and hygiene are urgently needed.  We hope management will address these issues promptly. || author=Jalen; rating=4; time=2025-06-10T17:06:32.956545723Z; relative=10 months ago; text=First time eating at this resto, and it was good! We went last Thursday (July 4, 2024), the place wasn’t crowded, very peaceful and clean too. The foods are affordable, I recommend their Chicken Teriyaki *chef’s kiss*. Some menu has little servings but it’s worth the price. || author=Grietz Rosell; rating=2; time=2023-12-23T13:43:29.715677Z; relative=2 years ago; text=Who does not love the tasty lechon from Kuzina Guadalupe? The lechon is great however, there are lots to improve on.
1. The baked scallops looked undercooked. Good thing it did not taste raw.
2. The chicken inasal was undercooked. Few red spots found in the meat.
3. The service is very poor. The cashier was lifeless. We had to ask for our order and the servers were talking without a mask while holding our food. The servers held the cups on the lop part where we drink and this is disgusting. We were not given soup which was included on the meal we ordered. When we asked for it, I was advised that by 1 server that his shift is ending at 8pm something and that he will ask his team, but no one served us the soup. There was no one to give us extra rice which is also included on what we paid for. Good thing a server named Francisco was there. He was, i think, responsible on cleaning the tables but he offered to serve the extra rice. He did this for everyone else who asked him because his team mates were inside the kitchen (not sure what they were doing) it was past 8pm. Only the cashier, the one who chops the lechon and 2 women are outside. Francisco was also very kind on reminding me to be careful since the rest room floor was wet and I am pregnant. The rest room was pretty disgusting too but good thing there was hand soap.

Never going back to Kuzina Guadalupe SM seaside until the service improves. || author=Kyla Varela; rating=5; time=2024-12-20T09:45:04.440315Z; relative=a year ago; text=Kuzina Guadalupe is a great place to try authentic Filipino food 🙂 The food is delicious, the service is friendly, and the prices are reasonable. The unli rice is Ph50.00 per person that come with one time ice tea drink and soup. The food was fairly good.

author=Roland Palaypay; rating=5; time=2026-03-05T03:25:40.570874588Z; relative=a month ago; text=Matsunoya Japanese Restaurant at the SM Seaside branch was an excellent dining experience. The sushi was fresh and well prepared, with great flavour and presentation.

The service was also very good, attentive, friendly, and efficient, which made the meal even more enjoyable.

Combined with the location inside SM Seaside, it’s a great spot to stop for quality Japanese food while shopping or exploring the mall.

Overall, excellent sushi, great flavour, and solid service. Definitely worth visiting if you’re in the area. || author=x; rating=5; time=2024-07-23T08:28:50.085050Z; relative=a year ago; text=We dined here last week hust to give it a try and it did not disappoint. Good food, good service, good ambiance.

Their spicy salmon roll is my new favorite! It has a nice heat that isn’t overpowering that makes you crave for more. The kani maki was delicious as well.
Their cheese omelette has a good balance of sweet and savory.
The ramen tasty and the pork was nice and soft, I did wish the egg was soft boiled though.
The tempura was light and crunchy and the sauce paired well with it.
The chicken nanban with tartar sauce was really good too. I think it had the same batter as the tempura, if not a bit thicker. The chicken itself was kinda bland, but the sauce made it better.
Lastly, if you like matcha, you’ll love their matcha ice cream. It was strong and it has the right amount of creaminess.

The place has many tables and the servers are attentive. We’ll be sure to come here again. || author=Carla Pasqueti Alob; rating=1; time=2024-09-15T15:43:41.968694Z; relative=a year ago; text=Our least and worst Japanese restaurant. The staff were also inattentive; we had to remind them about our order, the drinks took so long to arrive to the point we asked them 4 times even though there were only three other tables occupied. || author=Kathleen Ludoc; rating=1; time=2024-12-03T17:27:19.141353Z; relative=a year ago; text=Giving 1 star for nice ambiance. The rider assigned to pick up my order informed me that the restaurant had given my order to a different rider, which explained the repeated delays. He advised me to cancel the order—so I did. There were a lot of hoops to jump through just to cancel through Grab, but that’s a review for another time.

After the cancellation, the rider contacted me again to say he had reached the rider who took my order and now he had the food and could deliver it to me—but only if I paid in cash. I declined and asked that the order be returned to the restaurant, as I wasn’t willing to pay cash while my online payment was still on hold.

What an experience. The restaurant even called to tell me to re-order at 9:00 PM, despite knowing that they’re only available on Grab until 8:30 PM. Like… what?! lol.

Anyway, thank you, Bonchon. The service could use some improvement. || author=Scent Tolentino; rating=3; time=2023-03-14T11:30:06.577485Z; relative=3 years ago; text=We had dinner here last night and there are a few things I think need to be brought to attention as a first time in this place.

I only ordered their sizzling sisig and burger, the cashier attending at the moment seem reluctant to accept the payment as they didn't have a change for 1K. I tried to look for smaller bills from my wallet - the overall order costs 220Php - I handed her 250, and still was reluctant however I don't have anything else and given it was 7pm, the store should've been able to accumulate spare cash from the day's transactions - the cashies said she'll just give me the change later on in case other customers would pay them some coins,  so i said yes.

The sisig was okay, however the kalamansi added to the dish was not even half of it, more like 1/4 of the whole was given to me plus the stalk. The burger's bun is dry, took only one bite and wasn't able to finish it.

We were there for more  than an hour and unfortunately, until we finish no change was handed, to make it worst, the cashier even forgot about it and the cost of the order.

The sizzling meals overall are tasty, prolly avoid the burgers and train some of the staff better with customer handling.
